overall_summary: This batch of articles covers a range of international news from the last six hours, with a strong focus on the ongoing US-Israel war on Iran and its regional fallout. Other stories include sports, migration, elections, and a significant journalist killing in Gaza.

articles.title: Why Israel’s attacks on Lebanon could cripple US-Iran ceasefire | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Israel carried out a wave of air attacks on Lebanon, killing over 250 people, just hours after a US-Iran ceasefire was announced. This has exposed major disagreements about the ceasefire's scope, as the US and Israel claim it does not cover attacks on Lebanon, while Pakistan, the broker, says it does. The violence raises fears the ceasefire could unravel before permanent settlement talks begin in Islamabad.

articles.title: Funeral held for Al Jazeera’s Wishah killed in Israeli strike in Gaza | Gaza News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Mourners gathered in central Gaza for the funeral of Al Jazeera journalist Mohammed Wishah, killed in an Israeli drone strike. Gaza's Government Media Office stated his killing is part of a pattern, with 262 journalists killed since October 2023, aiming to silence witnesses and obscure the truth. Al Jazeera described the attack as a heinous crime and a deliberate effort to intimidate media workers.

articles.title: Bosnian ball boy who hid Donnarumma’s penalty shootout notes hailed as hero | World Cup 2026 News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A 14-year-old Bosnian ball boy, Afan Cizmic, is being hailed as a hero after hiding Italian goalkeeper Gianluigi Donnarumma's penalty notes during a World Cup qualifying shootout. Bosnia went on to win the shootout, with Cizmic believing his act impacted the game by angering and distracting Donnarumma. He plans to auction the note for charity.

articles.title: Fury vs Makhmudov: All you need to know about the heavyweight boxing bout | Boxing News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Tyson Fury comes out of retirement to fight Arslanbek Makhmudov in a heavyweight bout at London's Tottenham Hotspur Stadium on April 11. Fury, seeking a comeback after two losses, faces a dangerous opponent known for knockouts. The main card will be broadcast on Netflix, with the main event ringwalks expected after 10:00 PM local time.

articles.title: Global energy crisis is ‘the mother of all crises’: Turkish energy minister | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Turkey's energy minister calls the current global energy crisis, caused by the Strait of Hormuz blockade during the US-Israel war on Iran, 'the mother of all crises'. He states it highlights the need to diversify energy routes and that Turkey is well-positioned as a regional energy hub due to investments and location. He notes rising oil prices still burden Turkey's budget significantly.

articles.title: Four die, dozens rescued in attempted Channel crossing from France to UK | Migration News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Four people died while attempting to board an inflatable dinghy off the coast of France near Calais in an attempt to cross the English Channel to the UK. Thirty-eight others were rescued, with one person in critical condition. The incident underscores the ongoing dangers of the crossing, which continues despite enforcement efforts.

articles.title: US-Iran talks in Pakistan: Who’s attending, what’s on the agenda? | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Islamabad is preparing to host talks between the US and Iran this weekend, following a Pakistan-brokered two-week ceasefire. The negotiations aim to find a permanent settlement to the war that began six weeks ago. The talks are already under strain due to differing interpretations of the ceasefire terms and Israel's intensified bombing of Lebanon.

articles.title: What is Iran’s Strait of Hormuz protocol and will other nations accept it? | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Following the ceasefire, Iran has issued official terms for controlling the Strait of Hormuz, including a new navigational map and a toll system for ships. The strait had been effectively closed by Iran during the war, causing a global energy crisis. Analysts suggest Tehran's continued control and new protocol will be unpopular with the US and other nations.

articles.title: Brazil bowler Cardoso takes 9 Lesotho wickets in record-breaking T20 win | Cricket News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Brazil's Laura Cardoso took a record nine wickets, including five in consecutive deliveries, in a Women's T20 International against Lesotho. Her figures of 3-2-4-9 are the best in T20I history for men or women. Brazil won the match by 189 runs after posting a total of 202-8.

articles.title: Has Iran’s 10-point plan changed, as JD Vance claims? | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Confusion surrounds Iran's 10-point peace plan, which is the basis for upcoming US-Iran talks. US Vice President JD Vance dismissed the public version, and there are noted differences between Persian and English versions on key issues like uranium enrichment. This adds to uncertainty about the ceasefire, with US officials offering conflicting interpretations.

articles.title: Israeli attacks on Lebanon aimed to undermine ceasefire, critics say | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Critics suggest Israel's massive bombardment of Lebanon, which killed over 250 people hours after the US-Iran ceasefire, was aimed at undermining the truce. The attacks stem from a dispute over whether Lebanon is included in the ceasefire, with Israel and the US saying it is not. The violence has been widely condemned and comes as Israel's government faces domestic pressure over the terms of the ceasefire.

articles.title: A record-breaking field: What to expect from Peru’s presidential election | Elections News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Peru holds the first round of a presidential election on April 12 amid persistent political instability, with crime and corruption as top issues. A record number of candidates reflects a fragmented electorate, with no single contender decisively leading. If no candidate wins over 50%, a runoff will be held on June 7.

articles.title: Israel bombed Gaza on 36 of the past 40 days while the war raged in Iran | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Despite a ceasefire in Gaza six months ago and global focus on the Iran war, Israel has attacked Gaza on 36 of the past 40 days, killing at least 107 people. Israel has also severely restricted aid and medical evacuations during this period. The report highlights ongoing violence in Gaza alongside the broader regional conflict.

articles.title: India’s Assam and Kerala states hold legislative assembly elections | Elections News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Millions voted in state elections in Assam and Kerala, seen as a test of support for Prime Minister Narendra Modi's BJP. The BJP is expected to retain power in Assam but has struggled in Kerala. These elections are part of a series of key state contests happening this month.

overall_summary: This batch of articles covers escalating tensions in the Middle East, with a fragile US-Iran ceasefire threatened by continued Israeli attacks in Lebanon. Other stories include regional political developments, human rights issues, sports news, and environmental policy changes.

articles.title: Iran war day 41: What’s happening in Lebanon, Middle East and beyond? | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Lebanon declared a national day of mourning after Israeli attacks killed at least 254 people in a single day. Iran demands a halt to Israeli attacks in Lebanon as part of its ceasefire conditions, while a US official states Iran's published 10-point plan is not the agreed framework. UN officials expressed outrage over the scale of killing in Lebanon.

articles.title: Three life terms for Kashmir’s Aasiya Andrabi fit India’s ‘broader pattern’ | Prison News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Activists and legal experts have condemned an Indian court's verdict sentencing prominent Kashmiri separatist Aasiya Andrabi to three life terms. The 64-year-old was convicted under anti-terror laws, though the court found no evidence for major charges like waging war. Experts say the harsh sentencing fits a broader pattern of handling dissenting voices in Kashmir.

articles.title: Trump says US forces to stay near Iran, ready for ‘next conquest’ | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump warned that US forces will remain deployed around Iran and threatened overwhelming military action if Tehran fails to meet Washington's demands. This casts doubt over a fragile ceasefire brokered by Pakistan. Trump said troops would stay until the 'REAL AGREEMENT' is fully implemented, boasting forces were ready for 'its next Conquest'.

articles.title: Eritrea’s AFCON place in doubt after players disappear in South Africa | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Eritrea's participation in the Africa Cup of Nations preliminaries is in question after seven of their 10 local footballers disappeared following a victory in Eswatini. The players went missing when the national team arrived in South Africa en route home. This follows a pattern of Eritrean footballers abandoning national teams when in other African states.

articles.title: Iran has been bloodied, but it is winning against the US-Israel axis | US-Israel war on Iran | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: This opinion piece argues that despite suffering massive human costs, Iran is winning the war against the US-Israel axis by refusing to surrender. It states that Iran's survival and defiance have turned its struggle into a symbol of resistance. The author claims Iran is winning strategically by prolonging the conflict and raising the price of every strike against it.

articles.title: Thousands of Palestinians pray at Al-Aqsa Mosque after Israel’s 40-day ban | Occupied East Jerusalem News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The Al-Aqsa Mosque compound in occupied East Jerusalem has reopened to Palestinian worshippers after a 40-day closure by Israel. Around 3,000 worshippers attended morning prayers on Thursday. Israeli authorities announced the opening of the mosque and the Church of the Holy Sepulchre, citing updated instructions from the Home Front Command.

articles.title: Fury wants boxing clash with Joshua after comeback fight against Makhmudov | Boxing News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Tyson Fury says he wants to fight Anthony Joshua in a long-awaited clash if he wins his comeback fight against Arslanbek Makhmudov on Saturday. Fury, coming out of retirement, stated Joshua is the fight he wants next. Joshua last fought in December, knocking out Jake Paul, and was recently in a car accident in Nigeria.

articles.title: Record animal sacrifice attempts at Al-Aqsa prompt status quo fears | Al-Aqsa Mosque News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Israeli settlers have made seven attempts to smuggle animal sacrifices into the Al-Aqsa Mosque compound during Passover, the highest number documented since 1967. These provocations occurred while the compound was sealed off for 40 days. Experts argue the incidents mask a long-term strategy of encroachment on Al-Aqsa, with the aim of eventual takeover.

articles.title: Ukraine’s veterans’ theatre turns war wounds into catharsis | Russia-Ukraine war News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A veterans' theatre in Kyiv provides catharsis for wounded soldiers, wives, and widows by workshopping and staging plays about their war experiences. Founded in 2024, it functions as a school where participants turn their trauma into art. The plays, often autobiographical, resonate deeply with audiences who share similar hardships from the war.

articles.title: The war on Iran: Nobody won, everyone paid | US-Israel war on Iran | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: This opinion analysis argues the US-Israeli war on Iran was a 'war of choice' that failed to achieve its goals, leaving everyone paying a price. It states the conflict originated from Israel's post-October 7 doctrine and lacked proper international legal justification. The author notes the war caused a major energy disruption by closing the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Several people reported killed in fresh Israeli attacks on Lebanon | Conflict News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: New Israeli air strikes have killed more people in southern Lebanon, raising doubts over the fragile US-Iran ceasefire. Lebanon's news agency reported a strike killed at least seven people in Abbassiyeh. Israel claimed it killed a close aide to Hezbollah's leader and targeted Hezbollah infrastructure, continuing a campaign despite the ceasefire announcement.

articles.title: Djibouti elections: Who’s running against Guelleh and what’s at stake? | Elections News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: As Djibouti prepares for presidential elections, longtime leader President Ismail Omar Guelleh is expected to win a sixth term, extending his 27-year rule. The country is strategically located at the Bab al-Mandeb Strait and hosts military bases for the US, France, and China. Lawmakers removed presidential age limits last year, paving the way for Guelleh's candidacy.

articles.title: Argentina MPs approve bill allowing mining in glaciers | Environment News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Argentina's lower house of Congress has approved a bill pushed by President Javier Milei that authorizes mining in ecologically sensitive glacier and permafrost areas. The amendment, which passed after nearly 12 hours of debate, has outraged environmentalists who say it threatens crucial water sources. Thousands of people demonstrated against the bill outside parliament.

overall_summary: The articles cover recent geopolitical tensions following a US-Iran ceasefire. They focus on President Trump's criticism of NATO allies for their lack of support in the conflict and the ongoing strategic concerns in the Gulf region regarding control of the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Trump slams NATO over Iran after meeting Rutte, renews Greenland threat | NATO News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: President Donald Trump criticized NATO for its reluctance to support the US war on Iran, suggesting the alliance failed a test. Following a meeting with NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte, Trump also revived a threat to seize Greenland from Denmark. Rutte acknowledged Trump's disappointment but defended the contributions of most European allies to the US efforts.

articles.title: For Gulf states, Hormuz uncertainty casts shadow over fragile US-Iran truce | Donald Trump News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Gulf states have welcomed a two-week US-Iran truce but remain wary that the US might agree to a deal granting Iran control over the Strait of Hormuz. Experts express concern that President Trump, seeking a quick political victory, could prioritize optics over Gulf security. The Gulf Cooperation Council countries stress that any deal must ensure the strait reopens and leads to a permanent arrangement to avoid long-term Iranian strategic pressure.

overall_summary: The articles cover a range of international news from the last 6 hours, focusing on escalating conflict in Lebanon following a US-Iran ceasefire, major sports results in the Champions League, and US political developments. They also include reports on legal actions involving a former US president, a celebrity golfer, and a drug case linked to an actor's death.

articles.title: ‘People are afraid’: Lebanese reeling after Israel’s devastating attacks | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Israeli air strikes on Lebanon killed at least 254 people and injured over 1,160, marking a sharp escalation. The attacks occurred hours after a US-Iran ceasefire took effect, with confusion over whether Lebanon was included in the truce. Israel and the US stated Lebanon was not part of the agreement, while Pakistan and Iran insisted it was.

articles.title: PSG beat Liverpool as Champions League defence maintains pace | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Paris Saint-Germain defeated Liverpool 2-0 in the first leg of their Champions League quarterfinal. Goals from Desire Doue and Khvicha Kvaratskhelia gave PSG a commanding lead. Liverpool managed no shots on target and their manager acknowledged they were fortunate the deficit was not larger ahead of the second leg.

articles.title: Atletico Madrid shock 10-man Barcelona 2-0 in Champions League quarterfinal | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Atletico Madrid secured a 2-0 first-leg victory over Barcelona after Barcelona defender Pau Cubarsi was sent off. Julian Alvarez and Alexander Sorloth scored the goals for Atletico. Barcelona must stage a major comeback in the second leg to advance in the competition.

articles.title: Trump threatens 50% tariffs on countries that supply Iran with weapons | Donald Trump News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump threatened to impose immediate 50% tariffs on imports from any country supplying military weapons to Iran. The announcement followed a ceasefire with Iran, but the legal authority for such tariffs is unclear after a recent Supreme Court ruling. Analysts suggested the threat was aimed at China and may be difficult to implement.

articles.title: Trump administration signals it is mulling NATO withdrawal after Iran war | Donald Trump News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The Trump administration has indicated it is considering a withdrawal from NATO, framing the alliance as having failed a 'test' by not contributing military forces to the US-Israel war on Iran. The White House press secretary made the comments ahead of a meeting between Trump and NATO's secretary-general. The secretary-general described their subsequent conversation as 'frank and open' and noted NATO had provided logistical support.

articles.title: Prosecutors move to subpoena Tiger Woods’ prescription drug records | Golf News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Prosecutors in Florida are seeking to subpoena Tiger Woods' prescription drug records from a pharmacy following his recent car crash and arrest on suspicion of driving under the influence. Woods pleaded not guilty last week after deputies found pain pills in his pocket and noted signs of impairment. He has stated he is stepping away to seek treatment.

articles.title: JD Vance says Iran would be ‘dumb’ to let talks collapse over Lebanon | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: US Vice President JD Vance warned Iran it would be 'dumb' to let ceasefire talks collapse over Israel's attacks in Lebanon, which the US says were not part of the truce. Vance characterized conflicting positions on Lebanon's inclusion as a 'legitimate misunderstanding,' despite a Pakistani mediator's statement explicitly including Lebanon. He also suggested Israel had committed to showing some restraint in Lebanon to aid negotiations.

articles.title: Woman given 15 years for role supplying drugs linked to Matthew Perry death | Courts News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Jasveen Sangha was sentenced to 15 years in prison for her role in supplying ketamine linked to actor Matthew Perry's 2023 death. Sangha pleaded guilty to five felony drug counts and expressed regret in court. The judge imposed the harsher sentence partly because Sangha continued selling ketamine after Perry's death.

overall_summary: This batch of articles covers a range of international conflicts, diplomatic tensions, and human rights issues from the last six hours. Major themes include condemnation of Russia's actions against a Nobel laureate group, the killing of a journalist in Gaza, violence against civilians in Yemen and Gaza, a diplomatic rift between Ecuador and Colombia, US political commentary on Hungary, and developments in a US-Iran ceasefire alongside severe attacks in Lebanon. It concludes with a NASA space milestone.

articles.title: Nobel Prize Committee condemns Russian move to criminalise rights group
articles.summary: The Norwegian Nobel Committee has condemned a Russian Justice Ministry petition to designate the Nobel Peace Prize-winning human rights group Memorial as an 'extremist organisation'. The committee's chairman stated this would criminalize all of Memorial's activities, risking imprisonment for anyone involved. The statement calls on Russian authorities to withdraw the claim and cease harassment of the group, which documents human rights abuses.

articles.title: Israeli attack kills Al Jazeera journalist Mohammed Wishah in Gaza
articles.summary: Al Jazeera journalist Mohammed Wishah was killed in an Israeli drone strike on a car in Gaza. Al Jazeera Media Network condemned the killing as a deliberate, systematic policy to target journalists and silence truth. The Gaza Government Media Office stated at least 262 journalists have been killed in Israeli attacks since October 2023.

articles.title: Civilians under fire by gangs in Gaza: What happened in Maghazi?
articles.summary: Armed men, identifying as the 'Popular Forces combating terrorism' and reportedly operating with Israeli army support, raided homes in the Maghazi refugee camp, using them as cover during a gunfight. A family described being detained and interrogated, with their photography equipment confiscated. The clashes resulted in at least 10 Palestinian deaths and 44 injuries, according to health reports.

articles.title: 'Why my child?': Yemen's Taiz mourns sniper's killing of teen
articles.summary: A 14-year-old boy named Ibrahim was killed by a sniper while walking to school in Taiz, Yemen. His family and locals blame Houthi rebels for the shooting. The article describes the ongoing danger in the city, where snipers have vantage points and protective panels have been ineffective, with a UN report noting Taiz governorate accounts for most sniper killings in Yemen.

articles.title: Ecuador recalls its ambassador from Colombia over Jorge Glas comments
articles.summary: Ecuador has recalled its ambassador from Colombia following Colombian criticism of the case of former Ecuadorian Vice President Jorge Glas. Ecuador's government denounces such comments as foreign interference and a violation of sovereignty. The tension relates to the two-year anniversary of a controversial police raid on the Mexican embassy in Quito to arrest Glas, which caused a diplomatic rupture with Mexico.

articles.title: JD Vance slams Zelenskyy comments on Orban ahead of Hungary election
articles.summary: US Vice President JD Vance, visiting Hungary, called Ukrainian President Zelenskyy's recent remarks about Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban 'scandalous' and accused Ukraine of trying to influence Hungary's upcoming election. Vance criticized what he described as double standards in media coverage of foreign interference, comparing it to allegations about the 2016 US election. The European Commission stated it would convey concerns to US counterparts about Vance's comments.

articles.title: White House says Trump's 'red line' against Iran nuclear enrichment remains
articles.summary: The White House reiterated that the US rejects any uranium enrichment inside Iran, stating President Trump's 'red lines' have not changed. A spokesperson said an initial Iranian proposal was rejected, but a revised plan is a 'workable basis' for negotiations. The US team, led by Vice President JD Vance, is set to begin talks in Islamabad on Saturday, though Iran's parliament speaker later expressed doubts due to alleged ceasefire violations.

articles.title: Iranians breathe a 'ceasefire' sigh of relief as all sides claim victory
articles.summary: Tehran residents experienced a partial relief from bombardment as a two-week US-Iran ceasefire began, though with widespread scepticism about its durability. Some normal activity returned to streets, but air defence activations and attacks on oil facilities in southern Iran created uncertainty. The ceasefire was announced just before a US deadline, following intensified Israeli attacks on Iranian infrastructure.

articles.title: World reacts to 'brutal' Israeli attacks on Lebanon after US-Iran truce
articles.summary: Israeli forces conducted a major bombardment across Lebanon, killing at least 254 people, shortly after a US-Iran ceasefire was announced. Lebanese officials and Hezbollah condemned the attacks as war crimes and a violation of the ceasefire, with Hezbollah stating it reserves the right to respond. Conflicting statements emerged on whether Lebanon was included in the truce, with the US and Israel suggesting it was separate.

articles.title: NASA releases picture of 'Earthset' shot by Artemis crew
articles.summary: NASA released a new photograph called 'Earthset', showing Earth dipping below the lunar horizon, taken by the Artemis II crew during a lunar flyby. The image consciously echoes the iconic 1968 'Earthrise' photo. The four-member crew is on a mission to orbit the moon, paving the way for a planned landing in 2028.

overall_summary: This batch of articles covers a range of topics, with a primary focus on the aftermath of a US-Iran ceasefire and its regional implications. Key themes include continued attacks in the Gulf and Lebanon, the severe economic impact of the conflict on global energy and local livelihoods, and political reactions in India and Israel. Additional stories cover sports and African football governance.

articles.title: ‘We’re new miyas’: Will BJP naming some Assamese Muslims ‘Indigenous’ work? | Elections News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article reports on the BJP's political outreach to Assamese Muslims it recognizes as 'Indigenous' ahead of state elections. It highlights skepticism within the community, exemplified by Akram Ali, a Goriya Muslim whose home was demolished despite his recognized Indigenous identity. The piece questions whether this distinction offers real protection from state actions targeting Muslim neighborhoods.

articles.title: S Arabia, UAE, Kuwait, Qatar, Bahrain report attacks after Iran-US truce | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Several Gulf nations reported missile and drone attacks hours after a US-Iran ceasefire was announced. The UAE said its air defences intercepted projectiles from Iran, causing a fire at a gas complex and injuries. Kuwait, Bahrain, and Qatar also reported interceptions and attacks, with Kuwait mentioning damage to oil facilities and Bahrain reporting minor injuries and property damage.

articles.title: Mumbai’s historic dock quietens as fuel crisis chokes fishing trade | Oil and Gas News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article describes how soaring diesel prices, linked to regional conflict, have crippled Mumbai's historic Sassoon Dock fishing trade. Boat owner Shekhar Chogle and others have been forced to keep vessels docked, threatening livelihoods. While a ceasefire offers some hope, the piece notes the crisis extends across India and Asia, with fuel supply normalization expected to take time.

articles.title: World Athletics postpones Doha Diamond League amid US-Israel war on Iran | Athletics News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: World Athletics postponed the season-opening Doha Diamond League event, citing athlete and spectator safety concerns due to the ongoing conflict. The event is rescheduled from May 8 to June 19, conditional on the regional security situation. The decision follows reports of missile interceptions in Qatar and other Gulf states despite a US-Iran ceasefire.

articles.title: Defeat from the jaws of victory: Israel reacts to Trump’s Iran ceasefire | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article details Israeli political reactions to the US-Iran ceasefire. While Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's office expressed support, opposition leader Yair Lapid called it a political disaster. The piece notes Netanyahu stated the ceasefire does not extend to Israel's war on Lebanon, and analysts suggest the conflict has left Israel weakened and Iran strategically stronger.

articles.title: ‘Stone Age’ to ‘Golden Age’: How the final hours before the truce unfolded | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: This article recounts the tense final hours before the US-Iran ceasefire, highlighting President Trump's apocalyptic threats to destroy Iran's infrastructure. It describes how last-minute diplomacy mediated by Pakistan culminated in a two-week truce less than 90 minutes before Trump's deadline. The deal involved stopping attacks and reopening the Strait of Hormuz, with talks scheduled in Islamabad.

articles.title: Empty ships and shut wells: Why the Iran war oil crisis is not over yet | Oil and Gas News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article explains that despite a ceasefire, the global oil crisis triggered by the war is far from over. Reopening the Strait of Hormuz is a key step, but logistical challenges, like restarting shut wells and repositioning tankers, will cause delays. It cites data showing a 44% drop in Gulf oil exports in March, with Iraq hit hardest, and warns impacts on prices and supply will persist.

articles.title: Israeli attacks across Lebanon kill dozens hours after Iran-US ceasefire | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Israel launched a major coordinated strike across Lebanon hours after the US-Iran ceasefire, targeting areas in Beirut, the Bekaa Valley, and southern Lebanon. Dozens were killed and hundreds wounded, according to Lebanese health officials. Israel said it struck over 100 Hezbollah sites, while Lebanon's Red Cross responded with ambulances and hospitals became overcrowded.

articles.title: Medvedev thrashed by Berrettini as racquets smashed in Monte Carlo defeat | Tennis News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Daniil Medvedev suffered a stunning 6-0, 6-0 defeat to Matteo Berrettini in the Monte Carlo Masters second round. Medvedev, frustrated, smashed his racquet multiple times during the match. In another match, Alexander Zverev staged a comeback to defeat Cristian Garin after trailing in the third set.

articles.title: CAF’s Motsepe to visit both Senegal and Morocco amid AFCON fallout | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: CAF President Patrice Motsepe is visiting Senegal and Morocco to address the fallout from CAF's decision to strip Senegal of the Africa Cup of Nations title and award it to Morocco. Senegal has appealed the decision to the Court of Arbitration for Sport. Motsepe stated he would respect and implement the CAS ruling, calling for unity in African football.

articles.title: Did America lose yet another war? | US-Israel war on Iran | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: This opinion piece argues the US war on Iran repeats past failures defined by hubris and flawed strategic judgement. It draws parallels to Vietnam, Afghanistan, and Iraq, citing a lack of preparedness and understanding of conditions for using force. The author suggests the US, despite military advantage, may have been drawn into the conflict due to Israeli pressure and a misjudgment of Iran's strategic control over the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Israel strikes central Beirut without warning | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Israeli air raids struck densely populated commercial and residential districts in central Beirut without warning, hours after the US-Iran ceasefire. Dozens were killed and hundreds wounded, with rescue workers searching rubble. Israel described it as its largest coordinated strike of the war, targeting over 100 Hezbollah-linked sites, while Lebanon insisted the hit buildings were not military sites.

articles.title: Hegseth touts US ‘victory’ over Iran as Tehran hails its own ‘historic’ win | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth declared a decisive military victory over Iran, stating its missile program was functionally destroyed. Simultaneously, Iran's Supreme National Security Council hailed its own 'historic' victory. Hegseth said the ceasefire means Iran will never possess nuclear weapons, but admitted Iran can still launch limited attacks and that US forces remain in the region to monitor compliance.

articles.title: Iran, US both claim victory, but did they actually concede ground? | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Both the US and Iran claimed victory after agreeing to a two-week ceasefire, but the article suggests both sides shifted from earlier red lines. The US agreed to suspend bombing, while Iran said it would allow safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz. Analysts note these apparent concessions could become sticking points in upcoming talks in Islamabad aimed at a permanent settlement.

articles.title: Trump says Lebanon not included in US-Iran ceasefire amid Israeli assault | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: President Trump stated Lebanon was not included in the US-Iran ceasefire, calling Israel's war on Hezbollah a 'separate skirmish'. This contradicts mediator Pakistan's assertion that the truce covered the entire region. The article notes Iranian media reported a suspension of oil tanker movement through the Strait of Hormuz in response to the Israeli assault on Lebanon, though officials did not confirm this.

articles.title: How Pakistan managed to get the US and Iran to a ceasefire | US-Israel war on Iran | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article details Pakistan's central role as mediator in securing the US-Iran ceasefire. Both President Trump and Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi publicly acknowledged and thanked Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif and Field Marshal Asim Munir for their efforts. Sharif announced the immediate, region-wide ceasefire and invited both sides to talks in Islamabad on Friday, though key differences in the agreement's terms remain.

overall_summary: The articles cover two main topics: a ceasefire in the US-Iran conflict and Arsenal's recent football performance. Multiple reports detail international reactions to the US-Iran truce announcement, while one sports article analyzes Arsenal's Champions League match.

articles.title: Is Arteta’s intensity Arsenal’s Premier and Champions League hope, or fear? | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Arsenal secured a narrow 1-0 away win against Sporting Lisbon in the Champions League quarter-finals with a late Kai Havertz goal. The article questions whether manager Mikel Arteta's intense approach is a strength or a concern, noting the team's recent losses in domestic cups and a below-par performance in Lisbon despite their strong Premier League position. It also mentions that some within Arsenal's hierarchy are reportedly concerned that Arteta's visible intensity at crucial moments could be holding the team back.

articles.title: Netanyahu says US-Iran ceasefire ‘does not include Lebanon’ | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's office has stated that while Israel supports the US decision to suspend strikes on Iran, the announced two-week ceasefire does not apply to Israel's military operations in Lebanon. This contradicts a statement from Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif, who said the ceasefire was agreed 'everywhere, including Lebanon'. The article notes Hezbollah entered the conflict in March and that Israeli attacks on Lebanon have reportedly killed over 1,500 people and displaced more than a million since then.

articles.title: World welcomes US-Iran ceasefire, urges lasting peace in the Middle East | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Countries and international bodies have welcomed the two-week US-Iran ceasefire, which includes Iran reopening the Strait of Hormuz, and urged a lasting peace. Reactions included support from Israel (with the noted exception for Lebanon), calls for full commitment from Iraq and Egypt, and a welcome from UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres, who emphasized protecting civilian lives. Talks to finalize a peace deal are set to begin in Islamabad, Pakistan, on Friday.

articles.title: US politicians react to Trump’s Iran ceasefire with caution, relief | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: US politicians have reacted to the Iran ceasefire with a mix of relief and caution. While Democrats welcomed the truce and called for accountability over what they term an 'illegal war', some Republican allies of President Trump, like Senator Lindsey Graham, expressed skepticism. Graham emphasized that Iran must not be rewarded for attacking the Strait of Hormuz and stressed that lawmakers would review any final deal.

overall_summary: The articles cover a range of international news from the last 6 hours, including a major diplomatic development in the US-Iran conflict, several Champions League football matches, and investigations into Libyan finance. The batch is dominated by updates on the tense situation in the Middle East, where a last-minute ceasefire was announced.

articles.title: Libyan financier enabled ‘$300m in loans’ for Haftar’s Tripoli offensive | News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A new investigation alleges Libyan businessman Ahmed Gadalla was a key enabler for Khalifa Haftar's family, helping secure $300m in loans from a UAE-based bank for Haftar's failed 2019-2020 offensive on Tripoli. The report states the unpaid loans have burdened the Libyan public, while Gadalla has faced no accountability and has since expanded his influence over eastern Libya's financial system. The investigation also links him to potential military procurement for Sudan and currency counterfeiting.

articles.title: Barcelona’s Flick to defend ’emotional’ teen Lamine Yamal against criticism | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Barcelona coach Hansi Flick says he will defend 18-year-old winger Lamine Yamal against criticism for showing frustration, such as not celebrating a goal and being angry at substitutions. Flick acknowledges Yamal's emotional reactions sometimes create unwanted headlines but calls him an "unbelievable" player who he will always support. The coach made these comments ahead of Barcelona's Champions League quarterfinal against Atletico Madrid.

articles.title: Iranians brace for possible devastation as Trump’s deadline looms | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: With a US deadline approaching, Iranians are preparing for potential widespread attacks on civilian infrastructure threatened by President Donald Trump if Iran does not reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Residents report anxiety, stockpiling essentials, and buying expensive generators in anticipation of possible power cuts that would impact medical care and daily life. Trump has threatened to bomb Iran's electricity, bridges, and other critical infrastructure.

articles.title: Kane leads Bayern to 2-1 win over Real Madrid in Champions League thriller | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Bayern Munich beat Real Madrid 2-1 in the first leg of their Champions League quarterfinal, with goals from Luis Diaz and Harry Kane. Kylian Mbappe scored a late goal for Real Madrid to keep them in the tie ahead of the second leg in Munich. The match was described as a European classic, with Bayern dominating large periods of play.

articles.title: Pakistan appeals to Trump to extend deadline, Iran to reopen Hormuz Strait | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Pakistan's Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has appealed to US President Donald Trump to extend his deadline on Iran by two weeks and urged Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz for the same period. Sharif cited progress in diplomatic efforts to end the war and called for a two-week ceasefire to allow diplomacy to work. The White House said Trump was aware of the proposal, and an Iranian official said Tehran was reviewing it positively.

articles.title: Iran warns region and beyond as tension builds ahead of US ultimatum | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Iran has threatened to target energy facilities in Saudi Arabia and the UAE if its own infrastructure is attacked by the US, as a deadline set by President Trump loomed. Regional Gulf states took precautionary measures, with Bahrain temporarily suspending port operations and Kuwait advising people to stay home. US strikes reportedly hit Iranian targets including bridges and an oil terminal on Kharg Island.

articles.title: Arsenal beat Sporting with late Havertz goal in Champions League quarters | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Arsenal secured a 1-0 away win against Sporting Lisbon in their Champions League quarterfinal first leg thanks to a stoppage-time goal from Kai Havertz. The victory came after two consecutive domestic cup defeats for Arsenal, with the team described as winning 'ugly' while below their best. Arsenal are now favourites to advance to the semifinals ahead of the second leg in London.

articles.title: Trump announces two-week ceasefire as Iran agrees to reopen Hormuz Strait | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump announced he would suspend planned attacks on Iran for two weeks, crediting Pakistan for mediation. The suspension is contingent on Iran agreeing to the 'complete, immediate, and safe opening' of the Strait of Hormuz. Iran's foreign minister confirmed a tentative agreement, stating safe passage through the strait would be possible via coordination with Iranian forces.

articles.title: Billionaire investor Ackman makes $64bn bid for Universal Music Group | Music News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Billionaire Bill Ackman's Pershing Square has made a $64bn takeover bid for Universal Music Group (UMG), offering a 78% premium on its share price. The deal, which would see UMG move its listing from Amsterdam to New York, requires support from major shareholders like Bollore Group and Vivendi. Ackman has pursued UMG for years, citing its lagging share price despite the music industry's growth.

articles.title: Iran says talks with US will begin in Pakistan’s Islamabad on Friday | News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Iran's National Security Council says talks with the United States will begin in Islamabad, Pakistan, on Friday, based on Tehran's 10-point proposal. This follows the announcement of a two-week ceasefire, which is contingent on Iran reopening the Strait of Hormuz. Pakistan's prime minister said an immediate ceasefire was effective and invited delegations to Islamabad for further negotiations.

articles.title: Oil prices slide, stocks surge as Trump announces two-week Iran ceasefire | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Financial markets reacted positively to the US-Iran ceasefire announcement, with oil prices falling sharply and stock futures surging. The relief rally was driven by the prospect of resumed oil and gas flows through the Strait of Hormuz, which Iran had effectively closed. Analysts noted the deal could pave the way for a more permanent reopening, though uncertainties remain.

articles.title: Trump administration reaffirms plans to deport Abrego Garcia to Liberia | Donald Trump News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The Trump administration has told a US court it still plans to deport Salvadoran immigrant Kilmar Abrego Garcia to Liberia, despite his preference for Costa Rica. Critics accuse the government of seeking retribution, as Abrego Garcia was wrongfully deported to El Salvador in 2025 before being returned to the US by court order. He currently faces criminal charges for human smuggling in the US.

articles.title: Climate activist Greta Thunberg slams Trump’s threats against Iran | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Climate activist Greta Thunberg condemned US President Donald Trump's threat that 'a whole civilization will die tonight' against Iran, criticizing the muted public reaction. She linked the rhetoric to a normalization of war crimes and environmental destruction. The article notes polls showing young people in the US, across the political spectrum, are more sceptical of the war on Iran than older generations.

overall_summary: The articles cover escalating tensions between the US and Iran, with a looming deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Other major stories include Russia confirming foreign fighters' deaths in Ukraine, the WHO suspending Gaza medical evacuations, and various diplomatic developments. Additional reports cover migration, sports, and cultural incidents.

articles.title: Russia confirms 16 Cameroonian soldiers killed in Ukraine war | Russia-Ukraine war News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Russia has confirmed that 16 Cameroonian soldiers were killed while fighting in its war against Ukraine. Cameroon's Foreign Ministry called on families of the deceased to contact officials, marking the first time Cameroon has discussed its nationals' involvement. The statement did not specify how the soldiers ended up fighting for Russia or provide details on the circumstances of their deaths.

articles.title: US denies nuclear plan as deadline on threat to Iran ‘civilisation’ looms | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The White House has denied having plans to use nuclear weapons against Iran as a deadline set by President Donald Trump approaches. Trump warned that a 'whole civilisation will die tonight' if Iran does not capitulate to his demands to open the Strait of Hormuz. The Iranian Revolutionary Guards Corps responded defiantly, warning it would retaliate against US infrastructure if attacked.

articles.title: Top university says US-Israel attack targeted Iran’s progress, AI learning | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The head of Iran's Sharif University of Technology says a US-Israeli attack that damaged the university targeted symbols of Iran's progress, specifically its artificial intelligence research. The university president stated the attack destroyed an AI center housing critical databases and that the research was developed domestically due to sanctions. More than 30 universities have been affected by such attacks since the war began.

articles.title: WHO suspends Gaza medical evacuations after Israeli fire kills driver | Israel-Palestine conflict News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The World Health Organization has suspended medical evacuations from Gaza to Egypt after a contract worker was killed by Israeli fire. WHO Director General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus said the incident is under investigation and evacuations will remain suspended until further notice. Israel's mission stated troops fired at an unmarked vehicle they perceived as an immediate threat, and the incident is under review.

articles.title: Why JD Vance joined Pakistan’s last-ditch US-Iran mediation efforts | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: US Vice President JD Vance has quietly emerged as a central actor in last-ditch mediation efforts facilitated by Pakistan to de-escalate US-Iran tensions. President Trump confirmed Vance and Secretary of State Marco Rubio are lead negotiators as a deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz approaches. Sources say Pakistan's army chief spoke with Vance as part of backchannel talks aiming for a two-stage halt to the war.

articles.title: UK blocks rapper Kanye West from entry over anti-Semitism and Nazi support | Music News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The United Kingdom has blocked US rapper Kanye West from entering the country, citing his anti-Semitic comments and celebrations of Nazism. The Home Office refused his travel application on grounds his presence would not be conducive to the public good. The Wireless Festival in London, where West was booked to headline, has been canceled, with organizers issuing refunds to ticket holders.

articles.title: Afghanistan brands China peace talks with Pakistan ‘useful’ | News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Afghanistan has described peace talks with Pakistan being held in China as 'useful'. The talks aim to halt cross-border fighting that began in February, which has displaced thousands and alarmed the international community. Afghanistan's acting foreign minister thanked China for hosting the talks and expressed hope that minor interpretations would not hinder progress.

articles.title: Iraqi armed group releases abducted US journalist Shelly Kittleson | Freedom of the Press News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The Iraqi armed group Kataib Hezbollah has announced it will release abducted US journalist Shelly Kittleson on condition she leaves Iraq immediately. The group said its decision came in response to 'national stances' of Iraq's outgoing prime minister. An anonymous Iraqi official and members of the militia confirmed her release, which reportedly involved an exchange for imprisoned Kataib Hezbollah members.

articles.title: French nationals return home following Iran prison release | News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Two French nationals detained in Iran for over three years are returning to France following negotiations led by Oman. French President Emmanuel Macron announced their release, which comes amid an apparent thaw in relations as France criticizes the US-Israel war on Iran. Iran's official news agency reported the release followed an understanding that France would release an Iranian student and withdraw a complaint at the International Court of Justice.

articles.title: Trump to pursue stability with China’s Xi in May meeting, USTR Greer says | Donald Trump News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer says President Donald Trump will aim to maintain stability in economic relations with China during a May meeting with President Xi Jinping. Greer stated the US is 'not looking for a massive confrontation' and that the two countries have settled into a stable situation regarding tariffs and access to Chinese rare earth minerals. The summit was postponed from March due to the US-Israel war on Iran.

articles.title: ‘No end in sight’ if Trump acts on threat to destroy Iran infrastructure | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Analysts warn that if President Trump acts on his threat to destroy Iran's civilian infrastructure, it could prolong and expand the conflict with no swift victory in sight. They say such attacks would likely lead to Iranian retaliation against regional energy facilities, further raising oil prices. The warning comes as Trump's deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz approaches.

articles.title: FIFA opens disciplinary probe against Spanish FA after Islamaphobic chants | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: FIFA has opened disciplinary proceedings against the Spanish football federation over anti-Muslim chants heard during a Spain-Egypt friendly match. A section of fans chanted 'Whoever doesn't jump is a Muslim' at the Barcelona stadium. Spain forward Lamine Yamal, who is Muslim, blasted the chants as disrespectful and racist.

articles.title: Mediterranean migrant deaths mounting towards 1,000 in 2026: UN | Migration News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The UN's International Organization for Migration reports nearly 1,000 migrant deaths in the Mediterranean since the start of 2026, calling it one of the deadliest starts to a year since record-keeping began. At least 181 people have died or gone missing in five separate shipwrecks in the past 10 days. The agency calls for stronger efforts to stop traffickers and expand safe migration pathways.

articles.title: Russia and China block UN resolution on Strait of Hormuz | United Nations News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Russia and China have vetoed a UN Security Council resolution aimed at protecting commercial shipping in the Strait of Hormuz. The resolution, proposed by Bahrain, had 11 votes in favor but was blocked by Russia and China, who said it was biased against Iran. The US ambassador condemned the vetoes, while Iran's ambassador praised Russia and China for preventing what he called the legitimization of aggression.

articles.title: ‘Policy of abuse’: Women march in Cuba against US energy blockade | Miguel Diaz-Canel News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Hundreds of women marched in Havana to protest the US de facto oil blockade against Cuba. Protesters carried banners calling to 'Tear down the blockade' and denounced the US campaign as collective punishment. The Cuban government has organized several protests recently against policies implemented under President Trump, which have severely restricted Cuba's foreign oil imports.

overall_summary: This batch of articles covers a diverse range of global news from the last six hours. Major themes include the escalating US-Iran conflict and its regional consequences, significant political meetings in Asia, and sports news ranging from cricket to football. Several stories also highlight human rights and humanitarian crises.

articles.title: In Ghana Town, a ‘stateless’ future for hundreds born and raised in Gambia | Migration News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article reports on a community in The Gambia called Ghana Town, founded by Ghanaian fishermen decades ago. Hundreds of descendants born and raised there lack citizenship, passports, and national ID cards due to Gambian law, which does not grant citizenship to those born to non-Gambian parents. This legal grey zone creates daily uncertainty and barriers, such as preventing children from enrolling in public schools.

articles.title: Barcelona vs Atletico Madrid: Champions League – team news, start, lineups | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: This article previews the first leg of the Champions League quarterfinal between Barcelona and Atletico Madrid at Camp Nou. It notes this match is part of a series of five meetings between the teams in two months, with Barcelona having recently won a league encounter. Both coaches suggested their teams would perform better in this Champions League fixture.

articles.title: Taiwanese opposition leader to meet China’s Xi in a test of diplomatic skill | Xi Jinping News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article reports that Taiwanese opposition leader Cheng Li-wun of the Kuomintang (KMT) is meeting Chinese President Xi Jinping in Beijing. Analysts suggest the trip is a high-stakes test for the KMT's political prospects, as it aims to demonstrate that Taiwan and China are not destined for war. The meeting occurs in a context of surging Taiwanese nationalism and the KMT's waning power in recent presidential elections.

articles.title: What’s Iran’s 10-point peace plan that Trump says is ‘not good enough’? | International Trade News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Iran has proposed a 10-point peace plan to end the ongoing war, as a deadline set by US President Donald Trump for Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz looms. Trump called the plan a 'significant step' but 'not good enough,' threatening to attack Iranian civilian infrastructure if his demands are not met. The article states details of the 10 clauses have not been published, but they reportedly include calls for an end to hostilities and the lifting of sanctions.

articles.title: ‘Get a grip’: How Iranian embassies mocked Trump’s vulgar threat | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Iranian embassies worldwide launched a sarcastic social media campaign mocking a profanity-laced threat from US President Donald Trump. The embassies used tongue-in-cheek responses, such as claiming to have 'lost the keys' to the Strait of Hormuz, and referenced the 25th Amendment, suggesting Trump is unfit for office. The campaign aimed to paint the US president as mentally unfit while avoiding matching his aggressive rhetoric.

articles.title: How Israel’s invasion of southern Lebanon created a humanitarian crisis | Israel attacks Lebanon News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Israel's invasion of southern Lebanon has displaced 1.2 million people, creating one of the world's fastest-growing displacement crises. Israeli forces have launched over 1,840 attacks since March 2, killing more than 1,497 people, according to Lebanese health ministry figures cited in the article. The attacks have targeted bridges and crossings, aiming to isolate communities, with the Israeli army stating it is targeting Hezbollah strongholds.

articles.title: Australian and IPL cricket great David Warner charged with drink-driving | Cricket News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Former Australian international cricketer David Warner has been charged with mid-range drink-driving after being pulled over in Sydney. The 39-year-old, who retired from international cricket in 2024 but still plays T20 franchise cricket, was arrested and breath-tested after officers approached his vehicle. He is due to appear in court on May 7.

articles.title: ICE says its agents don’t carry guns in Canada as World Cup approaches | World Cup 2026 News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: US Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) says its agents do not carry guns or make arrests while operating in Canada, amid fears of their deployment at World Cup venues. The agency stated its personnel work with Canadian partners on joint investigations but have no authority on Canadian soil. This clarification comes after Toronto's city council passed a motion opposing the potential presence of ICE agents at World Cup games.

articles.title: Trump on Iran: ‘A whole civilisation will die tonight’ | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump escalated threats against Iran, suggesting the US would destroy Iranian 'civilisation' if Tehran does not open the Strait of Hormuz. Democratic rivals condemned the rhetoric as 'sick' and 'genocidal,' with some calling for Trump's removal via the 25th Amendment. The article notes legal experts say targeting civilian infrastructure is a war crime, and critics argue Trump launched the military campaign without congressional authorisation.

articles.title: Bangladesh appoints Tamim Iqbal as country’s youngest cricket chief | Cricket News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Bangladesh has dissolved its cricket board due to 'gross irregularities' in a 2025 election and appointed an interim committee led by former captain Tamim Iqbal. The 39-year-old becomes the country's youngest cricket chief and is tasked with holding new elections within three months. The decision follows political turmoil and a previous board's refusal to let the team play in the World Cup in India, citing security concerns.

articles.title: India’s nuclear leap: Why its fast breeder reactor success matters | Nuclear Energy News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: India's prototype fast breeder reactor has reached a self-sustaining stage, marking a major advance for its nuclear programme. This type of reactor produces more fuel than it consumes, potentially reducing India's dependence on uranium and paving the way for using its vast thorium reserves. Once fully operational, India will become the second country after Russia to have a commercial fast breeder reactor.

articles.title: From 9pm shutdowns to remote work: Egypt cuts fuel amid power crisis | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Egypt is implementing energy conservation measures, including fuel price hikes, due to a global crisis sparked by the US-Israel war on Iran. The war has led to a blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, halting most oil and gas shipments and damaging regional energy infrastructure, which has sent prices soaring. Egypt's government says it is among the 'best-performing' countries in tackling the crisis, though its energy import bill has more than doubled since January.

overall_summary: The articles cover escalating military tensions in the US-Israel war on Iran, including threats to civilian infrastructure and retaliatory strikes. They also report on regional impacts, such as Pakistan's solar energy resilience, internal debates within Yemen's Houthis, and separate news on sports, a shooting in Turkiye, and conflicts in Ukraine and Gaza.

articles.title: Israel threatens Iran’s trains, railways before Trump’s deadline expires | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The Israeli military has warned people in Iran to avoid trains and railway lines, indicating potential strikes on civilian infrastructure before a US deadline. US President Donald Trump threatened to bomb Iran's bridges and power plants if the Strait of Hormuz is not reopened. Iran has threatened devastating retaliation, and a bridge linking Saudi Arabia and Bahrain was briefly closed over security fears.

articles.title: Iran war: What is happening on day 39 of US-Israeli attacks? | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: US President Trump has threatened the 'complete demolition' of Iran's key infrastructure if the Strait of Hormuz is not reopened, while Iran dismisses the threats as 'delusional'. US-Israeli attacks have intensified, targeting airports, petrochemical complexes, and killing an Iranian intelligence chief. Iran has rejected a ceasefire proposal and put forward its own demands, including sanctions relief.

articles.title: How Pakistan’s solar boom is shielding it from worst of Iran war crisis | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Pakistan's growing adoption of solar panels is helping insulate households and farmers from global energy price shocks caused by the war on Iran and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. A farmer's story illustrates how solar power has replaced costly diesel for irrigation. However, Pakistan remains vulnerable due to heavy reliance on oil and gas imports through the strait.

articles.title: What is driving the Houthis’ decision-making on joining the Iran war | US-Israel war on Iran | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Internal debates within Yemen's Houthis have led to a cautious approach to joining the US-Israel war on Iran. One faction favors avoiding open confrontation to preserve gains and understandings with Saudi Arabia, while another believes the group must engage to secure its place in the regional 'axis of resistance'. This has resulted in limited, calculated operations rather than full-scale involvement.

articles.title: Synagogue in Tehran ‘completely destroyed’ in US-Israeli attack | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A synagogue in Tehran was completely destroyed in a US-Israeli strike that hit an adjacent residential building, according to Iranian media. A Jewish representative in Iran's parliament condemned the attack, which occurred during Jewish holidays. The strikes were part of overnight attacks that killed at least 15 people across Iran.

articles.title: Iran releases on bail Japan national jailed amid antigovernment protests | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Iran has released a Japanese citizen on bail months after their detention during antigovernment protests. The individual is believed to be NHK's Tehran bureau chief and faces security-related charges, with a trial expected. Japan confirmed the release but noted the person is not allowed to leave Iran.

articles.title: Los Angeles Stadium workers urge FIFA to bar ICE from World Cup | World Cup 2026 News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A union representing food service workers at the Los Angeles Stadium has asked FIFA to keep US Immigration and Customs Enforcement away from World Cup operations. The union warns of potential strikes and demands job protections, affordable housing support, and assurances that automation won't eliminate union jobs during the tournament.

articles.title: USA striker Patrick Agyemang’s World Cup in doubt after stretcher exit | World Cup 2026 News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: USA striker Patrick Agyemang's World Cup participation is in doubt after he was stretchered off with a leg injury during a club match. His manager stated the injury 'is not good' and that Agyemang has gone for a scan. The forward has been a key scorer for his club and recently featured for the national team.

articles.title: Three killed in shooting near Israeli consulate in Turkiye’s Istanbul | News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Three people were killed and two police officers injured in a shooting near the building housing the Israeli consulate in Istanbul. Turkish police eliminated the attackers, whose motives and identities are unknown. No Israeli diplomats are currently serving at the consulate, which is staffed by local employees.

articles.title: Taiwan’s gender row boxer Lin takes bronze medal at Asian championships | Boxing News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Taiwanese boxer Lin Yu-ting won a bronze medal at the Asian championships in her first competition since a gender-eligibility controversy at the 2024 Olympics. She was cleared to compete by World Boxing after an appeal. The International Olympic Committee has since adopted a policy limiting women's events to biological females.

articles.title: Ukraine, Russia kill three civilians each in tit-for-tat drone attacks | Russia-Ukraine war News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Drone attacks killed three civilians in Russia's Vladimir region and three in Ukraine's Dnipropetrovsk region. A Ukrainian drone hit a residential building in Russia, while a Russian drone struck a city bus in Ukraine. The attacks occurred after Ukraine's president reiterated a conditional ceasefire offer for Easter, which he said Russia appears unwilling to accept.

articles.title: How US, Israel are waging a war on Iranian culture, education | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: US-Israeli attacks in Iran have damaged numerous cultural heritage sites, universities, and libraries, which Iranian officials describe as an attack on Iranian identity. The war began with a strike on a girls' school, and at least 30 universities have been hit. Iran's science minister stated that scientists have been long-term targets.

articles.title: ‘Dying of thirst’: Inside Gaza’s al-Mawasi water crisis | Israel-Palestine conflict News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Residents in Gaza's al-Mawasi camp face severe water shortages, spending hours waiting at filling stations for clean water. The crisis worsened after a key water provider stopped operations due to funding issues. Displaced Palestinians fear the situation will become catastrophic with the arrival of summer heat and have held protests demanding intervention.

articles.title: Palestine weekly wrap: Protests sweep West Bank after death penalty law | Israel-Palestine conflict News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A new Israeli law allowing the death penalty for Palestinians convicted of 'terrorism' sparked a general strike and protests across the West Bank and East Jerusalem. Several countries and the UN condemned the law as discriminatory and a potential war crime. Tensions are heightened amid ongoing restrictions on access to holy sites in Jerusalem.

articles.title: Vinicius hails Lamine Yamal for condemning anti-Muslim fan chants | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Real Madrid's Vinicius Junior praised Barcelona's Lamine Yamal for condemning anti-Muslim chants by fans during a Spain match. Vinicius, who has faced racist abuse, emphasized the need for players to unite against discrimination. Yamal, who is Muslim, called the chants disrespectful and intolerable.

overall_summary: The batch of articles covers legal and political developments in Australia, including the arrest of a former soldier for alleged war crimes in Afghanistan and domestic policy changes. It also reports on international reactions to a recent Israeli military operation in Gaza.

articles.title: Australia arrests ex-soldier Roberts-Smith over alleged Afghan war crimes | Human Rights News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Former Australian special forces soldier Ben Roberts-Smith has been arrested and is expected to face five counts of the war crime of murder related to unarmed Afghan nationals. The arrest follows a complex investigation and comes after he lost defamation proceedings against journalists who reported on the allegations. An advocacy group stated the arrest is a significant step for Afghan victims and for Australia meeting its international legal obligations.

overall_summary: This batch of articles covers international developments including a Russian corruption case linked to Ukraine's incursion, US proposals for the Strait of Hormuz amid conflict with Iran, US political support for Hungary's leader ahead of elections, and escalating Israeli attacks deepening divisions in Lebanon.

articles.title: Russia jails former Kursk governor in Ukraine incursion-linked graft probe | Russia-Ukraine war News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A Russian court has sentenced former Kursk governor Alexei Smirnov to 14 years in a penal colony for corruption. He was found guilty of taking bribes from construction firms contracted to build defensive fortifications along the Ukraine border, which were reportedly substandard. The case is linked to Ukraine's 2024 incursion into the Kursk region, which encountered little resistance due to these weak defenses.

articles.title: Trump says US could charge for Strait of Hormuz passage amid Iran war | News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump has suggested the United States may charge tolls for passage through the Strait of Hormuz after the war with Iran, stating 'We're the winner. We won.' He issued what he called a 'final' ultimatum for Iran to reopen the strategic waterway and agree to Washington's terms. The article notes that Iran has reportedly been charging tolls for some ships and that its officials have called for 'new arrangements' to manage the strait after the conflict.

articles.title: Vance heads to Budapest to shore up Orban’s support before Sunday vote | Politics News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: US Vice President JD Vance is traveling to Budapest to bolster support for Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban ahead of parliamentary elections. The trip highlights the alignment between the US and Hungarian far-right, following President Trump's earlier endorsement of Orban. Most polls show Orban's Fidesz party trailing the opposition Tisza Party led by Peter Magyar, who campaigns on issues including corruption and Hungary's relationship with the EU.

articles.title: ‘They want to create a rift’: Israeli attacks deepen Lebanon fissures | Israel attacks Lebanon | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Escalating Israeli bombardment and ground invasion are deepening divisions within Lebanese society. The attacks have killed approximately 1,500 people and displaced over 1.2 million, with recent victims including Christians in addition to the predominantly Shia Muslim casualties. Analysts suggest Israel aims to create rifts between Lebanese communities, particularly to isolate the Shia community, as society splits between those blaming Israel and those blaming Hezbollah for the conflict.

overall_summary: The articles cover a range of international conflicts and political developments. Key themes include ongoing violence in Gaza and Lebanon, escalating tensions between the US and Iran over the Strait of Hormuz, and domestic US political reactions. A separate report highlights a milestone in space exploration.

articles.title: Israeli army fire on WHO vehicle in southern Gaza kills one, medics report | Israel-Palestine conflict News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A World Health Organization driver was killed and a WHO doctor was wounded when the Israeli army fired on their vehicle in eastern Khan Younis, according to medical sources and an Al Jazeera correspondent. The WHO stated the incident was under review and that it had put medical evacuations from Gaza via Rafah on hold as a result. The report says Israeli forces shot indiscriminately at people and vehicles along Salah al-Din Street.

articles.title: Libya’s oil disputes mirror Hormuz crisis, fuel European energy fears | Oil and Gas News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article states that Libya's internal oil disputes are creating a second energy chokepoint for Europe, mirroring the crisis caused by Iran's closure of the Strait of Hormuz. Libya's oil sector is divided, with the internationally recognized government in Tripoli signing contracts but renegade commander Khalifa Haftar controlling the oil fields and terminals. Europe's reliance on Libyan oil is growing as it seeks alternatives to Gulf supplies disrupted by the Hormuz closure.

articles.title: Trump warns Hormuz deadline ‘final’ as Iran pushes proposal to end war | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump warned that an Iranian proposal to end the war is not enough to avert his threat to destroy Iranian infrastructure unless Tehran reopens the Strait of Hormuz by a Tuesday deadline. The article reports that Iran, via Pakistan, put forward a proposal for a permanent end to hostilities, which includes safe passage through the strait, but Trump called it insufficient. A White House official confirmed the proposal was under consideration but said the president had not signed off and military operations continue.

articles.title: Democrats blast Trump for Iran ‘war crimes’ threat; Republicans supportive | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Several Democratic members of Congress condemned President Donald Trump after he renewed threats to destroy Iran's civilian infrastructure in an expletive-laden social media post. Legislators questioned his mental stability, with one calling for his removal via the 25th Amendment, and legal experts said such attacks would constitute war crimes. The article notes the post coincided with Easter and invoked the name of Allah, which critics found especially jarring.

articles.title: Artemis II breaks Apollo 13 record for farthest human travel from Earth | News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: NASA's Artemis II mission has broken the record for the farthest human travel from Earth, surpassing the distance set by Apollo 13 in 1970. The four astronauts on the Orion spacecraft are on a flyby around the far side of the moon and will reach a maximum distance of roughly 406,788 kilometers from Earth. The crew, which includes several historic firsts, will document lunar features before returning on a free-return trajectory.

articles.title: North Korea keeping Iran at arm’s length, reports Seoul | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: South Korea's National Intelligence Service believes North Korea is distancing itself from Iran in hopes of opening diplomatic dialogue with the United States. The spy agency reports no signs of North Korean weapons supplies to Iran since the war began and notes Pyongyang has issued only two toned-down statements on the conflict. The NIS also said it believes Supreme Leader Kim Jong Un is grooming his teenage daughter as his successor, based on recent public displays of her military aptitude.

articles.title: Lebanon’s Hezbollah and Yemen’s Houthis join Iran in strike on Israel | Israel-Palestine conflict News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Yemen's Houthi rebels say they launched an attack on Israel coordinated with Iran and Lebanon's Hezbollah, targeting several sites with cruise missiles and drones. The report comes as Israel continues strikes in Lebanon, targeting Hezbollah, with recent attacks killing paramedics and civilians. The WHO chief stated that 92 attacks on health facilities and personnel in the conflict have been verified.

articles.title: US Supreme Court clears path for Steve Bannon criminal case dismissal | Donald Trump News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The US Supreme Court cleared the way for the Justice Department to dismiss the criminal case against Steve Bannon, a key ally of President Donald Trump. Bannon was convicted in 2022 for contempt of Congress and served a four-month prison sentence. The Justice Department under Trump argued dismissal was 'in the interests of justice,' and a dismissal would remove the conviction from his record.

articles.title: Israeli air strike kills at least 10 Palestinians near Gaza school | Gaza News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: An Israeli air strike killed at least 10 people and wounded others near a school housing displaced Palestinians in central Gaza's Maghazi refugee camp, according to health officials. The strike occurred during clashes between Palestinians and an Israeli-backed militia that had reportedly attacked the school. The camp had been designated a 'safe zone' by the Israeli military but has been repeatedly targeted.

overall_summary: The articles cover escalating tensions in the US-Israel war on Iran, including ceasefire efforts and threats to critical infrastructure. They also report on Ukraine's strikes on Russian energy facilities, the humanitarian crisis in Gaza, and a preview of a Champions League football match.

articles.title: Real Madrid vs Bayern Munich: Champions League – team news, start, lineups | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article previews the first leg of the Champions League quarterfinal between Real Madrid and Bayern Munich at the Santiago Bernabeu. It notes Bayern's confidence and the likely return of striker Harry Kane from injury, while describing Real Madrid's season as inconsistent despite their historic prestige in the competition.

articles.title: Trump’s ‘Bridge Day’ threat: Can a last-ditch ceasefire plan work? | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump has threatened to destroy Iran's bridges and power plants if Tehran does not reopen the Strait of Hormuz by a new deadline. Mediators including Pakistan, Turkiye, and Egypt are reportedly proposing a 45-day ceasefire plan to create a window for broader negotiations, though Pakistan's foreign ministry has not confirmed the plan.

articles.title: Pakistan offers ‘two-phased’ truce deal to end US-Israel war on Iran | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Pakistan has proposed a two-stage plan to end the war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, according to a source. The proposal involves an immediate ceasefire followed by talks to finalize a broader settlement. However, a senior Iranian official stated Iran will not reopen the strait as part of a temporary ceasefire and is reviewing the proposal.

articles.title: Iran threatens Bab al-Mandeb closure: How would that affect world trade? | Shipping News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A top adviser to Iran's Supreme Leader has threatened that Iranian allies could shut the Bab al-Mandeb shipping route, following the effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz. The article explains that closing both chokepoints would block a quarter of the world's energy and a significant portion of Asia's exports to Europe, deepening the global supply crisis.

articles.title: Has Trump confirmed Iran’s claim that protesters were US-armed? | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump stated in an interview that Washington had armed Iranian opposition groups, specifically Kurds, during mass anti-government protests in December and January. The article notes this could lend credence to Iran's claims that the protests were foreign-backed, but cautions that Trump's frequently shifting statements make the extent of US involvement unclear.

articles.title: How US operation to rescue air officer from Iran unfolded | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
