articles.summary: President Trump announced the US military rescued a missing Air Force officer whose F-15 jet was shot down over Iran. The article describes a two-raid rescue mission involving a firefight in Iranian territory, with the officer evading capture for two days before being recovered. Iran had called on the public to hand over the airman.

articles.title: ‘Smell’ of war comes to St Petersburg as Ukraine hammers Russian refineries | Russia-Ukraine war News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A resident of St Petersburg describes smelling burning crude and chemicals from Ukrainian drone attacks on major Russian oil terminals at Ust-Luga and Primorsk. The strikes are part of a Ukrainian campaign to reduce Russia's oil export revenue, which has already cost Moscow an estimated $1bn and disrupted shipments from the Baltic ports.

articles.title: Jobless young Palestinians trapped as Israel holds Gaza’s economy hostage | Gaza | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article highlights the extreme unemployment and economic collapse in Gaza, where 80% are jobless and GDP has plunged 87%. It profiles young graduates whose lives and education have been destroyed, trapped in a besieged enclave with systematically destroyed infrastructure, describing a generational catastrophe.

articles.title: The US could still try to play the ethnic card in Iran | US-Israel war on Iran | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: This opinion piece examines the possibility of the US encouraging ethnic insurgencies within Iran, noting Trump's acknowledgment of arming Kurdish groups. It outlines historical grievances among minorities in Iran but argues that such a strategy is likely doomed to fail due to past experience and geopolitical realities.

articles.title: In maps and photos: Five Iranian civilian bridges at risk of US strikes | Conflict | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article details five major Iranian bridges that could be targeted under President Trump's 'Bridge Day' ultimatum. It describes their economic and strategic importance, noting that one bridge was already damaged in a recent strike, and warns that attacking such civilian infrastructure could constitute a war crime.

articles.title: Ukraine strikes Russian Black Sea energy hub Novorossiysk | Russia-Ukraine war News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Ukraine reports striking a Russian warship and a drilling rig at the Novorossiysk oil port on the Black Sea. The attack damaged infrastructure at the Caspian Pipeline Consortium terminal, which handles oil from Kazakhstan. Ukraine is intensifying attacks on Russian energy infrastructure to disrupt export revenues funding the war.

articles.title: Where are Iran’s power plants that Trump has threatened to destroy? | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article maps Iran's power plants, which are concentrated near major population centers and along the Gulf coast, following President Trump's threats to destroy them if Iran does not reopen the Strait of Hormuz. It notes these include gas, hydro, and the Bushehr nuclear power plant, and that targeting such civilian sites may violate laws of war.

overall_summary: The articles cover escalating conflict in the Middle East, focusing on US-Israeli attacks on Iran and Israel's invasion of Lebanon, alongside regional political and humanitarian fallout. Other stories include Indian election campaigning, a major NASA space mission, and a sports celebration.

articles.title: ‘Cocktail of Hindutva and welfarism’: How Modi’s BJP is wooing Assam voters | Elections News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article reports on the BJP's campaign strategy in Assam ahead of state assembly elections, combining Hindu nationalist (Hindutva) policies with welfare schemes aimed at women. It cites voter testimonials about benefiting from direct cash transfers and describes the party's historical labeling of Bengali-speaking Muslims as 'foreigners'. The piece notes accusations that Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma has pursued a hardline Hindu supremacist and anti-Muslim agenda.

articles.title: Iran war: What is happening on day 38 of US-Israeli attacks? | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article details escalating tensions as a deadline set by US President Donald Trump for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz approaches. It reports on US-Israeli attacks causing casualties and damage in Iran, including the deaths of children, and interceptions of drones by Gulf countries and Israel. Trump is quoted threatening to attack Iranian civilian infrastructure if the strait is not reopened.

articles.title: Iran’s top university bombed as US, Israel intensify attacks; 34 killed | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article states that at least 34 people, including six children, were killed in widespread US-Israeli attacks across Iran. It reports significant damage to Sharif University in Tehran and that Iranian authorities say at least 30 universities have been hit since the war began. The piece also notes the confirmed killing of a senior Iranian Revolutionary Guard Corps intelligence official during the attacks.

articles.title: A visual guide to Artemis II and previous missions to the moon | Space News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article is a guide to NASA's Artemis II mission, which is set to break the record for the farthest distance humans have traveled from Earth. It describes the mission's planned lunar flyby, its crew of four astronauts, and its objectives to test spacecraft systems for future deep-space missions. The piece notes Artemis II is part of NASA's broader programme to return humans to the moon and eventually enable missions to Mars.

articles.title: Trump says US armed Iranian dissidents via Kurds, Kurdish groups deny claim | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article reports that President Donald Trump claimed the US tried to covertly arm Iranian protesters through Kurdish intermediaries before the current war. Several Iranian Kurdish opposition groups denied receiving any weapons. The piece notes Trump's claim gives credence to Iranian assertions that the January protests were backed by foreign actors, and it mentions conflicting death toll figures from those protests.

articles.title: Yemen’s civilians fear fallout after Houthis enter Iran war | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article reports on fears among civilians in Sanaa, Yemen, after Houthi rebels involved themselves in the US-Israeli war on Iran. Residents worry about potential Israeli air strikes, price hikes, and fuel shortages exacerbating the country's humanitarian crisis. It quotes individuals concerned about a return to the 'horror' of past strikes and the unpredictable consequences of the conflict.

articles.title: Families fleeing Israeli attacks take refuge in Lebanon’s mountains | Israel attacks Lebanon News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article describes the displacement of over one million people in Lebanon due to Israeli attacks, focusing on families taking refuge in a converted school in the mountains. It quotes displaced individuals about their difficult journeys and uncertain futures. The piece notes the escalation followed Hezbollah's response to the killing of Iran's Supreme Leader in a US-Israeli attack.

articles.title: Up to 1.2 million people forced to flee as Israel pummels Lebanon | Israel attacks Lebanon News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article reports Israeli air raids on Lebanon have killed more than 1,450 people and displaced up to 1.2 million since March 2. It details specific attacks killing civilians, including children, and describes the overwhelming of shelter capacity leading to repeated trauma for many displaced. The piece notes Lebanese President Joseph Aoun's calls for negotiation to prevent further destruction.

articles.title: World Cup qualification earns Iraq coach Arnold a hero’s welcome in Sydney | World Cup 2026 News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article describes Iraqi football fans in Sydney giving a rapturous welcome to coach Graham Arnold after he guided Iraq to its first FIFA World Cup in 40 years. It notes the celebratory scenes at the airport and quotes Arnold expressing pride in making Iraqis happy. The piece mentions the team overcame logistical challenges due to regional conflict to qualify.

articles.title: Israel destroys southern Lebanon towns, hits ‘safe’ areas around Beirut | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article reports on renewed Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon and areas around Beirut, deepening a humanitarian crisis. It states attacks have targeted bridges and transport routes, which an aid official says is systematically isolating populations and cutting off humanitarian access. The piece also notes Hezbollah claimed rocket barrages on northern Israel in response.

overall_summary: The batch includes an opinion piece on regional security in the Strait of Hormuz and two sports articles covering NBA and Premier League news. The articles are unrelated, covering distinct geopolitical and athletic developments.

articles.title: A new regional order for the Strait of Hormuz | GCC | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The opinion piece argues that the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) and Iran have a collective opportunity to establish a new, locally managed security architecture for the Strait of Hormuz. It suggests this could provide the US with a political exit strategy from the ongoing conflict and prevent a unilateral order imposed by Tehran. The article states the current conflict has shattered the regional status quo and that GCC states are caught between confronting the US or being seen as participants in aggression against Iran.

articles.title: Steph Curry returns from injury with 29 points but Warriors lose to Rockets | Basketball News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Stephen Curry returned from a nine-week injury absence to score 29 points, but the Golden State Warriors lost 117-116 to the Houston Rockets. The Rockets' Alperen Sengun scored the go-ahead basket with 11.1 seconds left, and Kevin Durant led all scorers with 31 points for Houston. The Warriors have virtually assured themselves of the 10th seed in the Western Conference play-in tournament.

articles.title: Man City captain Silva to leave Premier League club at season’s end | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Manchester City assistant manager Pep Lijnders confirmed that club captain Bernardo Silva will leave at the end of the season when his contract expires. The 31-year-old Portuguese midfielder has won six Premier League titles and the Champions League during his nine years at the club. Silva will depart as a free agent, becoming the second major player to leave a top-four Premier League club this season after Liverpool's Mohamed Salah.

overall_summary: The articles cover international security developments and domestic economic impacts stemming from the ongoing US-Israeli war on Iran. They report on Ukraine's expanding military cooperation in the Middle East, protests and arrests at a UK airbase, and the severe economic fallout in Vietnam due to disrupted oil shipments.

articles.title: Ukraine and Syria to cooperate on security, Zelenskyy says | Military News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy announced greater security cooperation with Syria during a visit to Damascus, stating there is 'great interest in exchanging military and security experience.' Zelenskyy said the leaders agreed to work together to provide more security and development opportunities, and also discussed Ukraine as a reliable grain supplier to strengthen regional food security. The visit is part of Ukraine's broader effort to offer its military expertise, particularly in countering drones, to countries in the Middle East.

articles.title: UK police arrest seven protesters near RAF base used by US | Israel-Palestine conflict News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: British police arrested seven people at a protest near the RAF Lakenheath airbase, which is used by US forces, on suspicion of supporting the banned group Palestine Action. The protesters were part of a peace encampment objecting to the alleged use of the base for US aircraft involved in the war on Iran. Police stated they have a duty to enforce the law as it currently stands, referencing the government's ban on Palestine Action, which remains in effect despite a court ruling calling it 'disproportionate.'

articles.title: Vietnam’s gig workers slammed by rising fuel costs amid fallout of Iran war | Business and Economy News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Gig economy workers in Vietnam, like e-hailing drivers, are facing severe financial strain as fuel prices have skyrocketed due to the war on Iran disrupting oil shipments through the Strait of Hormuz. Diesel prices have more than doubled, forcing many drivers to stop working as fuel costs consume a large portion of their earnings. The Vietnamese government has suspended an environmental tax on fuel until mid-April to help stabilize prices, but the economic strain is evident with overloaded public transport and reduced domestic flights.

overall_summary: The articles cover escalating military threats and actions in the Middle East, including US threats against Iran and Israeli strikes in Lebanon. They also report on regional economic impacts, a historic space mission, wartime conditions in Gaza and Iran, and a statement from Hamas.

articles.title: Trump threatens ‘hell’ for Iran over Hormuz Strait as deadline approaches | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump threatened in a social media post to attack Iranian civilian infrastructure like power plants and bridges if Iran does not reopen the Strait of Hormuz by his Monday deadline. He stated Iran is currently negotiating with the US and a deal could be reached. Iranian officials condemned the threats as potential war crimes and stated the strait would only reopen following reparations paid via a new transit fee system.

articles.title: Earth in rear-view of Artemis II astronauts | Space News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: NASA's Artemis II mission has passed the halfway point between Earth and the moon, with the crew sending back images of Earth from the Orion spacecraft. The astronauts are expected to swing around the far side of the moon early this week, potentially traveling farther from Earth than any humans in history. The mission is part of NASA's broader plan to establish a permanent lunar base for deeper space exploration.

articles.title: At least 14 people killed in Israeli strikes across Lebanon | Israel attacks Lebanon News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon and Beirut's suburbs killed at least 14 people, with dozens more wounded. The Israeli military stated it was striking Hezbollah infrastructure sites. This follows an Israeli threat to hit the vital Masnaa border crossing with Syria, which led to its evacuation and temporary closure.

articles.title: OPEC+ agrees to hike oil output, warns of slow recovery after attacks | OPEC News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: OPEC+ agreed to a largely symbolic increase in oil output quotas for May, as key members cannot raise production due to the war and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. The group warned that restoring damaged energy infrastructure is costly and time-consuming. The statement signaled readiness to raise output once the strait reopens, as crude prices have surged near $120 a barrel.

articles.title: ‘Refuse to celebrate’: Christians in Gaza mark sombre Easter amid genocide | Israel-Palestine conflict | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Gaza's small Christian community marked a sombre Easter amid displacement and severe shortages caused by Israel's war, which the article describes as a genocide. Traditional celebrations were hampered by a lack of basic items like eggs and meat. Christians held quiet prayers and gatherings, with many having fled the territory where they have lived for over 2,000 years.

articles.title: Frustration grows as Iran’s wartime internet shutdown breaks grim record | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Iran's state-imposed internet shutdown is now the longest nationwide blackout on record, with connectivity at about one percent of pre-war levels since the US-Israel war began. The shutdown, which follows an earlier blackout in January, has severely impacted jobs and businesses. A global monitor stated no other war has sent an entire country offline like this, and the government has not explained how the digital sector will cope.

articles.title: Hamas armed wing says disarmament demands not acceptable | Israel-Palestine conflict News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Hamas's armed wing spokesperson, Abu Obeida, rejected calls for the group to disarm, stating that discussing disarmament before Israel fulfills the first phase of the US-brokered ceasefire amounts to continuing the genocide against Palestinians. He said raising the issue 'in a crude manner' is unacceptable. The issue is a major obstacle in talks to implement the US plan for Gaza.

overall_summary: The articles cover a range of developments from the last 6 hours, primarily focused on the ongoing US-Israel war on Iran and its regional and global repercussions. Key themes include military actions, diplomatic efforts, economic impacts, and unrelated news on health and sports.

articles.title: US pilot from downed F-15E plane rescued in Iran: What we know | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The second pilot from a US F-15E jet shot down in Iran has been rescued by US forces after 48 hours, following a firefight with Iranian forces. President Donald Trump confirmed the rescue, which analysts say was a significant test for the US military. The conflict, now in its 37th day, has resulted in over 2,000 reported deaths in Iran.

articles.title: Lamine Yamal’s anger after Barcelona’s La Liga win downplayed by Flick | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Barcelona manager Hansi Flick stated that Lamine Yamal's visible anger at the end of a win over Atletico Madrid was due to his frustration over missed scoring opportunities, not any off-field incident. The 18-year-old forward hit the post and provided a key pass that wasn't converted. Yamal has recently been involved in speaking out against anti-Muslim chants in Spanish football.

articles.title: Oman, Iran discuss smooth transit in Strait of Hormuz, Muscat says | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Oman and Iran held deputy foreign minister-level talks to discuss ensuring smooth vessel transit through the Strait of Hormuz, which Iran has severely restricted. The Omani Foreign Ministry stated that experts presented proposals for study. This comes as three Omani ships were seen transiting the strait outside Iran's approved corridor, and after US President Trump threatened consequences if the waterway is not opened.

articles.title: Iran’s ex-FM Zarif proposes peace roadmap; Gulf points at erosion of trust | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Former Iranian Foreign Minister Mohammad Javad Zarif has published a roadmap proposing Iran place limits on its nuclear program under international monitoring and reopen the Strait of Hormuz in exchange for an end to all sanctions. The plan, published in Foreign Affairs magazine, suggests this could help end the ongoing US-Israeli war on Iran. Zarif called US demands for zero uranium enrichment 'fanciful' and also proposed a mutual nonaggression pact with the United States.

articles.title: Nepal announces two-day weekends as fuel crisis caused by Iran war deepens | Energy News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Nepal has reduced the government work week from six days to five, instituting a two-day weekend (Saturday and Sunday) to cope with a fuel crisis caused by the US-Israel war on Iran. The landlocked nation, which relies almost entirely on India for fuel, has seen price hikes and supply issues. The government is also exploring legal measures to convert petrol and diesel vehicles to electric power.

articles.title: Suspected measles outbreak kills nearly 100 children in Bangladesh | Health News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Bangladesh suspects a measles outbreak has killed at least 98 children in the past three weeks, with over 6,400 children showing suspected symptoms. A health official attributed the potential outbreak to multifactorial causes, including a vaccine shortage. The government has ramped up vaccination efforts in the worst-affected areas, though a previous vaccination drive was delayed due to political unrest.

articles.title: Why an attack on Bushehr nuclear plant would be catastrophic for the Gulf | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Iran's Bushehr nuclear power plant has come under repeated attacks in the ongoing war, raising fears of a catastrophic nuclear incident that could affect the entire Gulf region. Iranian officials state the plant has been attacked four times since the war began, with the latest strike killing a security guard. Experts warn an attack could release hazardous radioactive material like Caesium-137, contaminating the environment for decades.

articles.title: Iran’s World Cup participation depends on relocation from US, minister says | World Cup 2026 News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Iran's sports minister says the country's participation in the upcoming World Cup is uncertain unless FIFA relocates its matches from the United States to Mexico, citing security concerns amid the ongoing war. The Iranian Football Federation has requested the move but has not received a response from FIFA, which has previously stated matches will proceed as scheduled. Iranian officials have expressed concerns about team security in the US.

articles.title: DR Congo to receive ‘third-country’ deportees from the US under new deal | Migration News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) says it will begin receiving 'third-country' nationals deported from the United States this month under a temporary deal with the Trump administration. The US will pay for the deportations, and the DRC government will face no costs. Such third-country deportation agreements have drawn criticism from rights groups over due process and safety concerns.

overall_summary: The articles cover a range of international news from the last six hours, including a major US pilot rescue in Iran amid ongoing conflict, regional protests and attacks in the Middle East, a high-profile boxing match, and sports events. Several reports detail escalating military actions and civilian impacts.

articles.title: Wilder edges retiring Chisora in chaotic heavyweight boxing fight | Boxing News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Deontay Wilder defeated Derek Chisora by split decision in a chaotic heavyweight fight in London, sending Chisora into retirement. The bout featured frequent slips and a knockdown by Wilder in the eighth round, though he was also deducted a point. Wilder suggested he held back from knocking out Chisora for the sake of his family.

articles.title: ‘Miraculous’: US rescues second pilot downed in Iran, Trump confirms | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: President Donald Trump confirmed the rescue of a second US F-15 crew member who had been missing in Iran after his jet was shot down. The rescue operation involved a daylight firefight and fierce resistance. Iran separately claimed it shot down a US C-130 aircraft involved in the search operation in Isfahan province.

articles.title: Iran war: What is happening on day 37 of US-Israeli attacks? | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article reports on developments in the ongoing conflict, including the rescue of the missing US airman after a heavy firefight. It notes President Trump issued a 48-hour deadline to open the Strait of Hormuz, which Iran dismissed. Iranian media reported strikes killed five people overnight and claimed Iranian forces shot down US aircraft during the rescue operation.

articles.title: Under Israeli restrictions, Palestinian Christians mark quiet Holy Week | Israel-Palestine conflict News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Holy Week observances in Jerusalem's Christian Quarter are severely disrupted by Israeli restrictions, with deserted streets and forced business closures. A shopkeeper describes a complete lack of business, while a school principal calls it the most difficult month. Israeli authorities have even barred senior church officials from entering the Church of the Holy Sepulchre for Palm Sunday services.

articles.title: Thousands rally in Iraq against ‘senseless’ US-Israel war on Iran | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Tens of thousands of supporters of Iraqi Shia leader Muqtada al-Sadr rallied in Baghdad and other cities to protest the US-Israel war on Iran. Protesters chanted against the US and Israel, calling the war 'senseless' and targeting civilians. Speakers urged the global community to confront what they described as US and Israeli arrogance and meddling in the region.

articles.title: Messi scores as Inter Miami open new stadium with a draw in the MLS | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Lionel Messi scored a headed goal as Inter Miami opened their new Nu Stadium with a 2-2 draw against Austin FC. Co-owner David Beckham called the stadium opening a 'dream come true'. Luis Suarez came off the bench to score a late equalizer, but a potential winning goal was ruled out for offside.

articles.title: Israel’s attack on UNRWA is central to its genocide of the Palestinians | Israel-Palestine conflict | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: This opinion piece argues that Israel's campaign to dismantle UNRWA is an essential part of a genocidal project against Palestinians. It states UNRWA's aid delivery and archival records of Palestinian rights are seen as a threat by Israel. The author cites Israeli officials' statements and links the dehumanization of UNRWA staff to genocidal tactics.

articles.title: Kuwait’s power, water plants damaged as Iran keeps attacking Gulf states | News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Kuwait reported Iranian drone attacks damaged two power and water desalination plants and sparked a fire at an oil complex, with no injuries. A spokesperson called it a 'criminal aggression' causing serious material damage. Separate attacks were also reported in Bahrain and the UAE, causing fires that were extinguished.

articles.title: Ukraine hits port in Russia’s Primorsk, oil refinery in Nizhny Novgorod | News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Ukrainian drone strikes hit Russia's Baltic Sea port of Primorsk, causing a fuel leak, and the NORSI oil refinery in Nizhny Novgorod, starting a fire. Russian officials reported damage to facilities but no injuries. Ukraine has increased attacks on Russian oil infrastructure to cut revenue funding the war, with diplomatic efforts to end the conflict stalled.

overall_summary: The batch contains a single, detailed feature article focusing on the personal experience of becoming a sole carer for a parent with a serious illness in the UK. It highlights the sudden life shift and emotional strain involved.

articles.title: ‘Nothing prepares you’: The invisible lives of carers in the UK | Health | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article is a first-person narrative describing the moment the author learned her mother was diagnosed with myeloma, a rare blood cancer. It details the immediate aftermath, including the author's realization that she would become her mother's sole carer and her father's limited capacity to help. The text conveys the emotional weight, the practical challenges of chemotherapy, and the cultural stigma surrounding cancer within their South Asian community.

overall_summary: This batch of articles covers a range of international news from the last six hours. The stories include a call for a royal meeting with Epstein survivors, a key football match result, tightened Israeli control at a holy site, a parade accident in the US, a satellite imagery blackout related to the Iran conflict, and Iran's announcement on shipping in the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Giuffre family urges UK’s King Charles to meet Epstein survivors in US | Sexual Assault News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The family of Virginia Giuffre, a prominent survivor of Jeffrey Epstein, has called on King Charles to meet with Epstein survivors during his upcoming state visit to the United States. The visit coincides with the anniversary of Giuffre's death, and the family hopes such a meeting could lead to further action against Epstein's co-conspirators. The article notes Giuffre had accused Epstein of trafficking her to the King's brother, the former Prince Andrew.

articles.title: Lewandowski claims late La Liga win for Barcelona at Atletico | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Barcelona defeated Atletico Madrid 2-1 with a late goal from Robert Lewandowski, moving seven points clear at the top of La Liga. The win came after second-placed Real Madrid lost earlier the same day. Marcus Rashford scored Barcelona's equalizer after Giuliano Simeone's opener for Atletico, who played most of the match with ten men after a first-half red card.

articles.title: Israeli measures tighten grip on Hebron’s Ibrahimi Mosque | Israel-Palestine conflict News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article reports on intensified Israeli control over Hebron's Ibrahimi Mosque, comparing the measures to those at Al-Aqsa Mosque. It details a history of restrictions since a 1994 massacre, including the division of the city and mosque between Palestinians and settlers. A local resident describes how access for his family has been severely restricted, with conditions worsening since the start of the war on Gaza in October 2023.

articles.title: Car slams into Louisiana Lao New Year parade, injuring about 15 people | Health News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A car struck participants at a Lao New Year parade in New Iberia, Louisiana, injuring an estimated 15 people, some seriously. Authorities stated the incident does not appear to be intentional. The festival's musical events were cancelled, though vendors remained open, and organizers stated prayers for the victims.

articles.title: US satellite firm Planet Labs announces blackout on war on Iran images | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Satellite imaging company Planet Labs says it will indefinitely withhold imagery of Iran and the conflict region in the Middle East after a request from the US government. The policy expands on previous delays and applies to imagery dating back to March 9. The company stated it will move to a managed distribution system, releasing images on a case-by-case basis for urgent needs or the public interest.

articles.title: Iran says Iraqi ships can pass Strait of Hormuz as transits tick up | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Iran has announced that Iraqi ships are exempt from restrictions and free to pass through the Strait of Hormuz, praising Iraq's 'struggle' against the US. The announcement comes as maritime transits through the strait have increased slightly but remain down over 90% from normal levels since the war began. The collapse of shipping has impacted global energy markets and severely reduced Iraq's oil production and exports.

overall_summary: The articles cover escalating tensions in the US-Israel war on Iran, including threats, diplomatic moves, and attacks on civilian infrastructure. They also report on related regional impacts and domestic US political developments.

articles.title: What the Iran-Iraq war can tell us about the US-Israeli war on Iran | US-Israel war on Iran | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: This opinion piece draws parallels between the current US-Israeli war on Iran and the 1980-1988 Iran-Iraq war. The author, recalling personal experiences as a student during the Iraq invasion, describes how the US and Israel have bombed civilian sites while calling for Iranians to rise up. The article argues that expecting populations to overthrow their government to stop bombing is an illusion, as was the case during the Iran-Iraq war.

articles.title: Trump says Iran has 48 hours to make deal as search for US pilot continues | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump has threatened Iran, giving it 48 hours to 'make a deal or open up the Hormuz Strait.' The post did not reference the ongoing search for a US pilot whose jet was shot down over Iran. Trump's threat relates to a 10-day deadline announced on March 26, set to expire on Monday, while Iran has rejected a US peace plan and says it remains open to diplomacy.

articles.title: Marco Rubio says he has stripped Qassem Soleimani’s niece of US residency | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The US has revoked the permanent residency of two women it says are related to the late Iranian commander Qassem Soleimani. The State Department accused Hamideh Soleimani Afshar of being an 'outspoken supporter' of Iran, while Iranian media quoted Soleimani's daughter denying any family connection. Secretary of State Marco Rubio took credit for the decision, which follows similar action against another relative of an Iranian official this month.

articles.title: Italy’s Meloni meets Qatar emir to discuss energy issues amid Iran war | Energy News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni met Qatar's Emir in Doha to discuss the energy crisis caused by the US-Israeli war on Iran. Both sides stressed the need for de-escalation and political dialogue, with Italy offering to help rehabilitate Qatari energy infrastructure. Meloni is the first EU or NATO leader to visit the region since the war began, seeking to secure energy supplies as Iran's blocking of the Strait of Hormuz affects global markets.

articles.title: Universities hit as US, Israel ramp up attacks on Iran’s infrastructure | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: US and Israeli strikes have hit at least 30 universities in Iran since the war began, including research centers at Shahid Beheshti University and Tehran's Science and Technology University. Iranian officials say these attacks target the country's scientific environment and academics, with no casualties reported because classes have moved online. The attacks follow President Trump's threat to bomb Iran 'back to the Stone Ages' by hitting infrastructure.

articles.title: Trump files emergency motion to continue building White House ballroom | Donald Trump News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The Trump administration has filed an emergency motion to resume construction of a White House ballroom, arguing that pausing the work creates national security risks. The motion claims the court-ordered suspension leaves a 'massive excavation' site near the executive mansion. This follows a judge's ruling that halted the project, which has drawn criticism from preservationists, especially after the East Wing was demolished.

articles.title: Indonesia receives bodies of peacekeepers killed in southern Lebanon | United Nations News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Indonesia has received the bodies of three UN peacekeepers killed in southern Lebanon during Israel's invasion amid the wider US-Israel war on Iran. Foreign Minister Sugiono called for a thorough UN investigation, saying such incidents should not happen in a peacekeeping mission. The deaths occurred as the conflict spread to Lebanon after Hezbollah fired rockets at Israel following the assassination of Iran's Supreme Leader.

overall_summary: The articles cover a range of topics from escalating military conflicts in the Middle East to sports and a criminal case in the UK. They report on strikes near an Iranian nuclear plant, Israeli attacks in Lebanon, a major football match, and a court proceeding following an arson attack on a Jewish charity.

articles.title: Projectile hits near Iran’s Bushehr nuclear plant, killing one: IAEA | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A projectile strike near Iran's Bushehr nuclear plant killed one person, according to the IAEA, which cited Iranian authorities. The agency reported no increase in radiation levels and said an auxiliary building was damaged, while the main plant sections were unaffected. Iran's foreign minister stated the facility has been attacked four times since the war began, and Russian staff were evacuated following the incident.

articles.title: Israeli strikes damage hospital in Lebanon’s Tyre; ground invasion advances | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Israeli air strikes damaged the Lebanese Italian Hospital in Tyre, injuring at least 11 people, with nearby buildings destroyed. The Israeli military issued a displacement order for Tyre residents as it continued ground operations, bombing bridges and infrastructure in southern Lebanon. An analyst suggested Israel's offensive is evolving into an open-ended occupation rather than achieving its stated goal of disarming Hezbollah.

articles.title: Haaland grabs hat-trick as Man City thrash Liverpool to reach FA Cup semis | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Erling Haaland scored a hat-trick as Manchester City defeated Liverpool 4-0 to advance to the FA Cup semifinals. Liverpool's Mohamed Salah missed a penalty during the match, compounding a difficult period for manager Arne Slot. The result increases pressure on Slot, with his team struggling for form and Champions League qualification.

articles.title: Three suspects ordered to stay in UK custody over Jewish charity attack | Courts News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Three men have been remanded in custody after being charged with arson for an attack that destroyed four ambulances belonging to the Jewish charity Hatzola in London. The suspects, two British nationals and one UK-Pakistani dual national, are accused of being reckless as to whether life would be endangered. Police are treating the incident as an antisemitic hate crime, with a fourth person also arrested in connection.

overall_summary: The articles report on separate incidents of conflict and natural disaster. They cover an earthquake in Afghanistan, executions in Iran, and ongoing military attacks in the Russia-Ukraine war.

articles.title: Afghanistan earthquake kills eight members of same family | Earthquakes News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: An earthquake in Afghanistan killed eight members of the same family when their home collapsed in Kabul province, with a two-year-old boy being the only survivor. The 5.8-magnitude quake struck in Badakhshan province, with tremors felt in Kabul, New Delhi, and parts of Pakistan. Afghanistan is frequently hit by earthquakes, and impoverished, remote areas often struggle to respond to such disasters.

articles.title: Iran executes two convicted members of banned opposition group | Death Penalty News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Iran executed two men convicted of being members of the banned PMOI/MEK opposition group after the Supreme Court upheld their sentences for 'armed rebellion' and involvement in terrorist acts. This follows the execution of four other members of the group in late March. Rights groups have criticized the executions, with Amnesty International accusing authorities of torturing the men and raising fears of more planned executions.

articles.title: At least four people killed in Russian attacks on Ukraine | Russia-Ukraine war News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Russian air attacks on northeast Ukraine killed at least four people and injured 11 others, with strikes targeting Kharkiv city and other towns. In a separate incident, a Russian drone strike injured 11 people, including a child, in the Sumy region. Meanwhile, in Russia's Rostov region, at least one person was killed and four injured in drone and missile attacks, with a foreign-flagged cargo vessel also damaged by falling drone debris in the Sea of Azov.

overall_summary: The articles cover escalating military actions and diplomatic tensions in the ongoing US-Israeli conflict with Iran. They report on specific battlefield incidents, casualties, and stalled ceasefire efforts. The batch also includes reports on regional impacts and a separate story about a political crisis in Pakistan.

articles.title: Iran war: What is happening on day 36 of US-Israeli attacks? | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Iran claims it shot down two US warplanes, with one American crewmember missing and search operations underway. Iranian officials celebrated the action as a demonstration of their military capability despite US claims of damage to their defenses. The article notes a reported rejection of a US ceasefire proposal and rising casualties, with over 2,000 reported killed in Iran since the conflict began.

overall_summary: The articles cover a diverse set of topics from the last six hours. They include a major space mission milestone, a significant legal settlement related to a high-profile criminal case, and a sports preview for a key football match.

articles.title: NASA releases stunning first photos of Earth from Artemis II moon mission | Science and Technology News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: NASA has released the first images taken from inside the Artemis II Orion spacecraft, showing views of Earth from deep space. The four-person crew, about 100,000 miles from Earth, is on a 10-day mission to fly around the moon and return without landing. The spacecraft is expected to reach the moon on Monday and splash down in the Pacific Ocean on April 11.

articles.title: Lawyers search for Epstein survivors for Bank of America $72.5m settlement | Courts News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Lawyers are working to notify victims of Jeffrey Epstein about a $72.5 million settlement with Bank of America, which is accused of facilitating his crimes. A judge has given preliminary approval to the settlement, which Bank of America agreed to while maintaining it did not participate in the sex trafficking. This is the third such settlement with a major bank, following similar deals with JPMorgan Chase and Deutsche Bank.

articles.title: Atletico Madrid vs Barcelona: La Liga preview – teams, start time, lineups | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Barcelona faces Atletico Madrid in a key La Liga match on Saturday, with the added pressure of an injury to winger Raphinha. This places more responsibility on 18-year-old star Lamine Yamal, who has been in strong recent form. The match is the first of three upcoming games between the two clubs, which also includes a Champions League quarterfinal tie.

overall_summary: The articles cover escalating military conflicts in the Middle East, particularly between Israel and Lebanon and the US-Israel coalition against Iran, with reports of infrastructure attacks, downed aircraft, and threats to seize oil resources. Other stories include Ukraine's battlefield progress, US domestic politics and economy, and separate incidents in Cambodia, Sudan, and the UAE.

articles.title: Israel threatens to destroy more Lebanon bridges as crisis mounts | Israel attacks Lebanon News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Israel's military has threatened to destroy the Sohmor and Mashghara bridges over the Litani River in Lebanon's western Bekaa Valley, claiming Hezbollah uses them. An Al Jazeera reporter says destroying these main bridges would essentially isolate the western Bekaa from the rest of Lebanon, impacting movement of goods and people. Israel has already destroyed at least six other bridges over the Litani since early March, and strikes have also damaged key water infrastructure in southern Lebanon.

articles.title: Trump says with more time, US can ‘take the oil’ in Iran | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump suggested in a social media post that with more time, the US could 'easily' open the Strait of Hormuz and 'take the oil' from Iran, claiming it would be lucrative. He has previously promised to reopen the strait, which Iran has effectively blocked, but the US military has said it is 'not ready' to escort ships there. Trump has regularly called for 'taking the oil' in countries where the US is militarily involved, though he provided no details on how this would be achieved in Iran.

articles.title: Iran says it downed two US jets as search for one pilot continues | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Iranian forces claim they struck down two US fighter jets, an F-15 over southwest Iran and an A-10 near the Strait of Hormuz. The fate of at least one pilot from the F-15 crew is unknown, with reports that one was rescued but another remains missing. US President Trump said the downing would not affect the prospect of talks with Tehran, stating 'We're in war.' Iranian officials have called on civilians to be on the lookout for survivors.

articles.title: Ukraine slows enemy advances, liberates land, drains Russia’s war chest | Russia-Ukraine war News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Ukraine has devastated Russia's oil export capacity, stopped Russian advances, and reclaimed occupied territory, with one estimate saying it halved the Russian rate of advance in three months. President Zelenskyy has secured agreements with several Gulf states to export Ukrainian drone know-how in return for joint production support. Commander-in-chief Syrskii said the rate of drone production is key to Ukraine's success, with first-person view drones now responsible for 90% of Russian casualties, and Ukraine has reclaimed 470 sq km of territory this year.

articles.title: Trump seeks historic $1.5 trillion for military in Congress budget request | Donald Trump News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: President Trump has requested a $1.5 trillion military budget for the next fiscal year, a nearly 40% increase over the previous year, which would be the largest in decades. The request, which is not legally binding, emphasizes military spending and law enforcement while proposing $73bn in cuts to domestic programmes like climate initiatives and refugee aid. The budget is separate from a previous $200bn emergency request for the US-Israeli war with Iran and is expected to face lengthy congressional negotiations.

articles.title: Omani, French and Japanese vessels transit the Strait of Hormuz | News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A French container ship, three Oman-linked tankers, and a Japanese-owned gas carrier have crossed the Strait of Hormuz, with the French vessel being the first Western ship known to do so since Iran effectively closed the strait. It was not immediately clear how the vessels secured safe passage; the French ship appeared to signal its owner's nationality before crossing. Only about 150 vessels have transited the strait since March 1, most linked to Iran, China, India, and Pakistan.

articles.title: US unemployment rate drops despite economic uncertainty and Iran war | Business and Economy News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The US unemployment rate fell to 4.3% in March as non-farm payrolls grew by 178,000 jobs, rebounding from a revised loss in February. The biggest gains were in healthcare (adding 76,000 jobs, partly due to a nursing strike ending) and construction (adding 26,000 jobs), while the federal government cut 18,000 positions. The White House praised the report as evidence of Trump's economic policies working, though experts caution the impact of the Iran war is not yet reflected in job numbers.

articles.title: Cambodia parliament approves law to combat cybercrime scam rings | News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Cambodia's National Assembly has approved its first law specifically targeting scam centres accused of defrauding foreigners of billions of dollars. The law sets prison sentences of 2-5 years and fines up to $125,000 for online scams, with higher penalties for gang activity or human trafficking, including up to 20 years in prison. Justice Minister Keut Rith said the law aims to enhance a 'cleaning operation' and send a message that Cambodia is not a place for scams.

articles.title: Drone strike on Sudan hospital kills 10, medical charity MSF says | News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A drone attack on Al Jabalain Hospital in Sudan's White Nile State killed at least 10 people, including seven medical staff, according to Doctors Without Borders (MSF). The attack, which MSF said was 'reportedly' carried out by the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces (RSF), hit an operating theatre and a maternity ward during a children's immunisation campaign. This is part of a series of attacks on healthcare in Sudan's ongoing war, with over 200 attacks on facilities reported since the conflict began.

articles.title: At least one killed at UAE’s Habshan gas facility after intercepted attack | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Debris from an intercepted attack caused fires at the UAE's Habshan gas facility, killing one Egyptian national and injuring four others. The Abu Dhabi Media Office said the debris fell after a successful interception by air defence systems, causing 'significant damage' to the country's largest natural gas processing site. The incident occurred amid intense Iranian missile and drone attacks on the UAE, with officials reporting at least 18 ballistic missiles, 4 cruise missiles, and 47 drones fired from Iran over 24 hours.

articles.title: More than 20 attacks on Iranian healthcare facilities since March 1: WHO | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The World Health Organization (WHO) has verified over 20 attacks on healthcare facilities in Iran since March 1, resulting in at least nine deaths. WHO chief Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus cited damage to the Pasteur Institute in Tehran, which was rendered unable to continue health services, and attacks on other facilities including a psychiatric hospital and a pharmaceutical plant. The attacks appear to be part of a widening of US-Israeli targets beyond military and political sites to include health, education, and transport infrastructure.

articles.title: US judge upholds decision to toss subpoenas into Fed Chair Jerome Powell | Donald Trump News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A US federal judge has again rejected subpoenas from the Trump administration seeking information about Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell, stating the government's arguments 'do not come close to convincing the Court' of their legitimacy. Judge James Boasberg previously nullified the subpoenas in March, ruling they were issued for an 'improper purpose' to pressure Powell into compliance with Trump's demands. The administration has focused on cost overruns in Fed building renovations as evidence of malfeasance, which Powell has called a 'pretext' to undermine the central bank's independence.

articles.title: Two dozen Democrat-led states sue Trump over mail-in ballot limits | Donald Trump News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: About two dozen Democrat-led states and the District of Columbia have filed a lawsuit to block a Trump executive order that sets new limits on mail-in ballots. The order requires the Department of Homeland Security to compile lists of eligible voters and the Postal Service to send ballots only to those on state-specific lists. The states argue the order violates the US Constitution by interfering with state authority over elections and could create chaos ahead of November's midterm elections.

overall_summary: The articles cover a range of international news from the last six hours, focusing primarily on legal condemnation of the US-Israeli war on Iran, the humanitarian impact in Gaza and Iran, political developments in Burkina Faso, and social issues in Spain. They also include analysis of the war's effects on Israel and an opinion piece on the erosion of international law.

articles.title: Over 100 US legal experts condemn strikes on Iran as possible ‘war crimes’ | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: More than 100 US-based international law experts have signed an open letter condemning US and Israeli military strikes on Iran. The letter states the campaign violates the UN Charter and may amount to war crimes, citing attacks on schools and hospitals and alarming rhetoric from senior US officials. The scholars argue the war lacks UN Security Council authorization and credible evidence of an imminent Iranian threat.

articles.title: Rubble, mud and hair: How to rebuild a home in Gaza | Israel-Palestine conflict News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A Gaza resident, Mohammed al-Jadba, is building a temporary shelter from rubble, mud, and human hair due to a lack of construction materials under Israeli import restrictions. His family has been living in tents since the October ceasefire, and he accelerated construction after his mother was injured by gunfire. The article describes this as a partial, improvised solution in the absence of large-scale reconstruction in Gaza.

articles.title: Burkina Faso military leader Traore says ‘forget democracy’ | Politics News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Burkina Faso's military leader, Ibrahim Traore, stated in an interview that people should 'forget about democracy,' calling it 'slavery.' This follows his government's dissolution of all political parties in January. Traore, who seized power in a 2022 coup, had initially promised elections but later delayed them, citing security concerns from armed groups.

articles.title: ‘I don’t know how we’ll emerge from this’: How much more can Israelis take? | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Analysts say years of war have transformed Israel's politics, economy, and society, with the current conflict with Iran described as an 'existential battle.' The article notes the high financial cost of recent wars and that Israel's war aims against Iran seem distant, with no strong signs of public disquiet in Iran. Israelis face frequent air raid warnings and increased strain, yet many see the war as inevitable.

articles.title: War crimes are no longer shameful. That should terrify you | US-Israel war on Iran | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: This opinion piece argues that the US, Israel, and Iran are showing 'swaggering contempt' for international law protecting civilians, unlike past leaders who might have pleaded ignorance. It cites statements from US and Israeli officials that dismiss legal restraints and notes attacks causing civilian casualties and displacement. The author warns that without urgent action, the international community may be acquiescing to the destruction of these norms.

articles.title: Real Madrid coach insists Spain ‘not racist’ despite Islamophobic chant | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Real Madrid coach Alvaro Arbeloa said Spain is 'not a racist country' and is 'very tolerant,' despite Islamophobic chants during a Spain-Egypt friendly. Barcelona forward Lamine Yamal, who is Muslim, condemned the chants as 'ignorant and racist.' The article notes past incidents of racism in Spanish football and that police are investigating the latest chants.

articles.title: How the US and Israel are waging war on Iran’s medicines, vaccines | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article reports that the US and Israel have carried out multiple attacks on medical facilities in Iran, including the Pasteur Institute in Tehran. Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian appealed to international health organizations to respond, calling it a 'crime against humanity.' The Pasteur Institute is a key research and vaccine production center, and the WHO has verified over 20 attacks on health facilities in Iran since March 1.

overall_summary: The articles cover a range of topics from the ongoing US-Israel war on Iran, including military developments and regional impacts, to US domestic political changes and international sports news. Several reports detail specific consequences of the conflict, such as attacks on infrastructure and healthcare, while others focus on unrelated political, legal, and sports events.

articles.title: Iran war: What is happening on day 35 of US-Israeli attacks? | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The US and Israel have widened attacks on Iran, striking infrastructure including a medical research center and a bridge near Tehran. US officials justify the bridge strike by claiming it was used for military drone materials, while Iran calls it a civilian target. The conflict has caused significant casualties and damage, with regional efforts underway to address the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Hegseth fires US Army chief of staff in reported string of dismissals | Military News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has fired Army Chief of Staff General Randy George, reportedly to install someone aligned with his and President Trump's vision. The dismissal follows clashes over Hegseth's decision to block promotions of four officers, leading to questions about potential bias. General George, appointed in 2023, is retiring immediately amid the ongoing war.

articles.title: Iranian women footballers reveal ‘enormous pressure’ of the Asian Cup saga | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Iranian footballer Mona Hamoudi describes the intense pressure faced by the national women's team during the Asian Cup in Australia, where players were scrutinized for their silent protest during the anthem. Several players initially sought asylum but later reversed their decision to return to Iran. The squad faced criticism from Iranian state media and expectations from the diaspora, creating a charged atmosphere.

articles.title: How Israel is destroying healthcare infrastructure in southern Lebanon | Israel attacks Lebanon News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Israel's attacks on Lebanon have severely damaged healthcare infrastructure, killing medical workers and forcing hospital closures, which experts say is part of an effort to displace people. Since intensifying strikes in March, 53 medical workers have been killed and five hospitals closed, exacerbating a pre-existing healthcare crisis. The destruction and mass evacuation orders have cut off access to care for many in southern Lebanon.

articles.title: Iran condemns US-Israeli ‘moral collapse’ after attacks on civilian sites | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: President Trump has threatened to expand attacks on Iran's infrastructure, including bridges and power plants, following a strike on a major bridge. Iran's Foreign Minister condemned the targeting of civilian structures, calling it a sign of 'moral collapse' by the enemy. The article notes regional instability and potential Iranian retaliation against bridges in other Gulf countries.

articles.title: Lakers handed one of heaviest defeats by Thunder and hit by Doncic injury | Basketball News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The Oklahoma City Thunder defeated the Los Angeles Lakers 139-96, ending the Lakers' winning streak. Lakers star Luka Doncic suffered a strained left hamstring during the game and will undergo an MRI. The Thunder controlled the game from the start, with key performances from Shai Gilgeous-Alexander and Luguentz Dort, who also left the game with an injury.

articles.title: Why did Trump fire Pam Bondi from Justice Department, who is Todd Blanche? | Donald Trump News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: President Trump has fired Attorney General Pam Bondi, who will transition to a private sector role. Trump appointed Deputy Attorney General Todd Blanche, his former criminal defense lawyer, as acting attorney general. Bondi's tenure faced scrutiny over various issues, including her record on sex trafficking cases related to Jeffrey Epstein.

articles.title: Man City vs Liverpool: FA Cup quarterfinal – team news, start, lineups | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Liverpool faces Manchester City in a crucial FA Cup quarterfinal, with the FA Cup and Champions League being Liverpool's only remaining chances for silverware this season. Manager Arne Slot is under pressure to win a trophy, and Mohamed Salah aims to end his Liverpool career on a high note. The match is set for April 4 at the Etihad Stadium.

articles.title: Tiger Woods told police after car crash he was ‘talking to the president’ | Golf News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Tiger Woods told police after a car crash that he was 'talking to the president,' possibly referring to President Trump, according to body camera footage. Woods was arrested for driving under the influence after failing a sobriety test and was found with prescription painkillers. The footage shows Woods appearing impaired and being handcuffed following the accident.

articles.title: Kuwait desalination plant, oil refinery hit by missile and drone strikes | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Kuwait reports that a desalination plant and the Mina al-Ahmadi oil refinery have been hit by Iranian missile and drone strikes, causing fires but no employee injuries. This is part of ongoing retaliatory strikes in the US-Israel war on Iran, with Kuwait being a frequent target due to its proximity. The UAE also faced attacks, with interceptions reported and damage to a gas facility.

articles.title: Cuba to pardon more than 2,000 prisoners amid US pressure | Prison News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Cuba's government will pardon 2,010 prisoners as a humanitarian gesture during Easter, following recent talks with the US amid increasing pressure. The release includes individuals based on good conduct and health, though the government denies acting under US coercion. This is the second amnesty this year, with the US long demanding the release of political prisoners.

articles.title: Ten years since Panama Papers: What did they reveal, did anything change? | Panama Papers News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The Panama Papers, leaked ten years ago, exposed a global network of offshore shell companies used by politicians, business leaders, and other elites to hide wealth. The leak involved 11.5 million documents from the law firm Mossack Fonseca, analyzed by hundreds of journalists worldwide. It revealed connections to figures like former Argentine President Mauricio Macri and former Ukrainian President Petro Poroshenko.

overall_summary: The articles cover the economic and political fallout from the US-Israel war on Iran, focusing on energy market disruptions in Pakistan and China, and a separate political development in Myanmar. The first two articles detail how the conflict has impacted global LNG and oil supplies, while the third reports on the military leader's formal election as president.

articles.title: How war on Iran turned Pakistan’s LNG surplus into a looming shortage | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Pakistan is facing a looming gas shortage crisis after starting the year with an LNG surplus. This shift occurred after the US-Israel war on Iran led to attacks on key gas facilities in Qatar and Iran, disrupting supplies through the Strait of Hormuz. As a result, Qatar halted LNG production and declared force majeure, severely impacting Pakistan, which relies heavily on imports from Qatar and the UAE with no emergency reserves.

articles.title: How China’s ‘teapot’ refineries are cushioning it from Iran war oil crisis | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: China's small independent 'teapot' refineries are helping cushion the country from the oil crisis caused by the war on Iran and the paralysis of the Strait of Hormuz. These refineries had stockpiled oil from countries like Iran, Russia, and Venezuela, capitalizing on cheap oil due to sanctions. While China's oil supply is not entirely immune, its seaborne crude imports in March remained broadly in line with 2025 averages, though most arrivals were loaded before the conflict began.

articles.title: Myanmar’s coup leader elected president by pro-military parliament | Politics News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Myanmar's coup leader, Min Aung Hlaing, has been elected president by the country's pro-military parliament, formalizing his political power five years after ousting an elected government. He received at least 293 votes, passing the majority threshold, following a lopsided election won by an army-backed party. His ascent to the presidency, after a military leadership reshuffle, is seen as a strategic move to consolidate power under a nominally civilian government while a civil war continues to rage.

overall_summary: The articles cover a range of international and U.S. domestic news from a future date in 2026. Key themes include geopolitical energy shipments, a major sports upset, immigration enforcement controversies, a legal ruling on election interference, and a new U.S. tariff policy on pharmaceuticals.

articles.title: Russia sending second ship with oil to Cuba amid US blockade | Oil and Gas News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Russia's energy minister announced a second oil shipment is being loaded for Cuba, following a first delivery of about 700,000 barrels earlier in the week. The move is framed as support for Cuba, which is struggling under a U.S. fuel blockade that has caused blackouts and shortages. The U.S. administration granted a waiver for the first delivery on humanitarian grounds and says future decisions will be case-by-case.

articles.title: Unbridled Bosnian joy marks World Cup qualification win over Italy | World Cup 2026 News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Bosnia and Herzegovina's national football team defeated Italy in a dramatic penalty shootout to qualify for the 2026 World Cup, sparking wild celebrations. The match took place at the Bilino Polje Stadium in Zenica, a venue known for its intimidating atmosphere for visiting teams. Thousands of fans filled the city and the stadium, with many more gathering in fan zones and cafes to witness the historic victory.

articles.title: Rights groups, Milwaukee leaders slam ICE’s arrest of Palestinian advocate | Donald Trump News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Ten Muslim civil rights groups have jointly denounced the arrest of Salah Sarsour, a Palestinian American community leader and president of the Islamic Society of Milwaukee, by ICE agents. The groups allege he is being targeted for his Palestinian and Muslim background and his pro-Palestinian advocacy, linking his detention to a broader administration trend. Sarsour, a lawful permanent resident, was detained and transferred to facilities in Illinois and Indiana, leaving his family unsure of his location.

articles.title: US court orders resentencing for Colorado clerk involved in election scheme | Courts News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A Colorado appeals court overturned the nine-year prison sentence of former county clerk Tina Peters, convicted for her role in a scheme to tamper with voting machines after the 2020 election. The court ruled the sentencing judge improperly considered Peters's personal beliefs about election fraud, though her conviction stands. Peters, who became a cause célèbre for the election denial movement and former President Donald Trump, was pardoned by Trump in December, though the pardon's practical effect is unclear from the provided text.

articles.title: Trump unveils 100 percent tariff on drugs to push for pharmaceutical deals | Donald Trump News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: President Donald Trump signed an executive order imposing tariffs of up to 100 percent on some patented drugs to pressure pharmaceutical companies into pricing deals and building U.S. facilities. Companies with pricing deals and U.S. construction projects face a 0% tariff, while others face a 20% tariff that could rise to 100% within four years if they do not negotiate. The administration official stated companies have several months to negotiate, and critics warn the tariffs could increase costs and jeopardize investments.

overall_summary: The articles cover a range of international conflicts, diplomatic tensions, and sports news from the last six hours. Key themes include ongoing wars in the Middle East affecting Iran and regional security, political and legal developments in the US, and significant events in international football.

articles.title: Pakistan, Afghanistan hold talks in China to end months of conflict | Conflict News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Pakistan and Afghanistan are holding talks in Urumqi, China, aimed at ending months of cross-border conflict that has killed dozens and disrupted trade. Pakistan accuses Afghanistan of harboring fighters, which Kabul denies. China is mediating, following a temporary ceasefire for Ramadan, though sporadic attacks have been reported.

articles.title: World Cup 2026: Italy’s football chief resigns after qualifying failure | World Cup 2026 News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Gabriele Gravina has resigned as head of Italy's football federation after the men's national team failed to qualify for the World Cup for a third consecutive time. His resignation follows a call from the sport minister and a playoff loss to Bosnia and Herzegovina. A vote for a new president is scheduled for June 22.

articles.title: World Cup 2026: Iran and Infantino talks progressing as war darkens US trip | World Cup 2026 News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Iranian football officials held a face-to-face meeting with FIFA President Gianni Infantino, making progress on Iran's participation in the World Cup amid the US-Israel war on Iran. The article states talk of a boycott or moving games to Mexico has faded, and Iran is on track to prepare for the tournament in the US, though uncertainty remains.

articles.title: Argentina expels Iranian diplomat amid rift over blacklisting IRGC | Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Argentina has expelled Iran's charge d'affaires, declaring him persona non grata, following Iran's rejection of Argentina's designation of the IRGC as a terrorist group. Argentina called Iran's statements false and an interference in internal affairs. The move escalates tensions, with Iran accusing Argentina of acting under Israeli influence.

articles.title: Trump fires Pam Bondi as US attorney general, elevates Todd Blanche | Donald Trump News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: President Donald Trump has announced the dismissal of Attorney General Pam Bondi, with Deputy Attorney General Todd Blanche taking over temporarily. Trump praised Bondi's service but did not specify a reason for the change. Bondi's tenure faced criticism over the independence of the Justice Department and investigations into Trump's political opponents.

articles.title: US Homeland Security shutdown to stretch on, despite Senate passing funding | Donald Trump News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A partial US government shutdown affecting the Department of Homeland Security will continue, as the House has not voted on a Senate-passed funding bill. The stalemate centers on immigration procedure reforms, impacting TSA agents and causing airport delays. President Trump has endorsed a two-track funding plan proposed by Republican leaders.

articles.title: UN experts urge investigation into Israel’s killing of Lebanese journalists | Israel attacks Lebanon News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Three UN experts have called for an independent investigation into Israel's killing of three journalists in Lebanon, calling it a war crime and an attack on press freedom. They rejected Israel's claim that one journalist was a fighter, stating that working for media affiliated with an armed group does not make a journalist a legitimate target.

articles.title: Children’s hospital in Tehran keeps hopes and smiles alive during war | Health News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Staff at a children's hospital in Tehran are organizing activities to provide joy for young patients amid the ongoing US-Israeli war on Iran. Doctors pooled money and donations for events during the Nowruz holidays, as families celebrated outdoors despite the conflict. The hospital has not been directly hit, but bombs have struck nearby areas.

articles.title: GCC chief urges UN to halt Iranian attacks, protect Gulf waterways | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The GCC secretary-general has called on the UN Security Council to act to halt Iranian attacks across the region, condemning them as a violation of international law. He urged measures to protect maritime navigation and include GCC states in any talks with Iran. The article notes that 85% of Iranian projectiles have targeted Gulf countries, with the UAE hardest hit.

articles.title: Barcelona thump Real Madrid 6-0 to reach women’s Champions League semifinal | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Barcelona's women's team defeated Real Madrid 6-0 to reach the Champions League semifinals 12-2 on aggregate. Alexia Putellas and Caroline Graham Hansen were among the scorers in front of a large crowd. Barcelona will face Bayern Munich in the next round.

articles.title: Yamal slams anti-Muslim fan chants as Spain’s World Cup final hopes tainted | World Cup 2026 News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Spain's hopes of hosting the 2030 World Cup final were marred by anti-Muslim chants during a friendly match against Egypt. Spanish police are investigating the incident, which prompted star player Lamine Yamal to condemn the chants as disrespectful and racist. Analysts cited the event as evidence of structural racism in Spanish society.

articles.title: Planning commission approves Trump’s White House ballroom plans | Donald Trump News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A planning commission has approved President Trump's proposal to build a large ballroom at the White House, though a federal judge ruled the project needs Congressional authorization. Trump has proceeded with construction despite legal challenges, arguing previous work did not require such approval. The project is privately funded and estimated to cost about $400 million.

articles.title: Legal groups condemn arrival of a dozen deportees from US to Uganda | Donald Trump News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Legal groups in Uganda have condemned the arrival of a dozen deportees from the US, calling it an act of 'transnational repression' and challenging it in court. This is the first confirmed deportation from the US to Uganda under a deal with the Trump administration. The article notes Trump's efforts to send deportees to third countries, some of which receive financial incentives.

articles.title: As war drags on, US reiterates Trump open to diplomacy with Iran | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A US State Department spokesperson stated President Trump is open to diplomacy with Iran but accused Iran of pursuing a nuclear weapon. The comments come as the US-Israel war on Iran continues, with Trump having shared footage of destroyed Iranian infrastructure. Iran denies seeking nuclear weapons and insists on its right to enrich uranium.

articles.title: At least 43 people killed in ADF attack in northeast DR Congo, army says | Conflict News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: At least 43 people were killed in an attack blamed on the ADF rebel group in northeast Democratic Republic of Congo. The army reported houses were torched, and local officials said some victims were killed with machetes. ADF attacks on civilians have intensified in the region despite joint military operations.

overall_summary: This batch of articles covers a range of international conflicts, regional tensions, and human rights issues. The primary focus is on the ongoing US-Israel war with Iran and its regional repercussions, including developments in Yemen, Lebanon, and the strategic Strait of Hormuz. Other stories report on condemnations of Israeli policies, internal conflicts in Africa, a tragic attack in Uganda, and sports-related news.

articles.title: Yemen’s Houthis coordinate with Iran, but retain independence, despite war | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article analyzes the relationship between Yemen's Houthis and Iran, describing it as an unequal partnership where Iran provides support and political cover. It states the Houthis retain significant independence and a margin of manoeuvrability to serve their domestic objectives in Yemen, despite aligning with Iran's regional strategy. Their involvement in the broader Iran-US-Israel conflict has so far been limited, as they likely seek to avoid a broad confrontation that could drain their resources.

articles.title: Eight countries condemn Israel’s one-sided death penalty for Palestinians | Israel-Palestine conflict News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Eight Muslim-majority nations issued a joint statement strongly condemning Israel's new law imposing the death penalty on Palestinians convicted of fatal attacks, which does not apply to Jewish Israelis. The countries denounced what they called increasingly discriminatory practices that entrench a system of apartheid. They also expressed deep concern over conditions for Palestinian prisoners, warning of abuses including torture and starvation.

articles.title: Libyan military leader may have combat drones despite UN embargo | Khalifa Haftar News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: According to a Reuters report, eastern Libyan military leader Khalifa Haftar may have acquired Chinese and Turkish combat drones despite a UN arms embargo. Commercial satellite images from 2025 reportedly show drones at an airbase east of Benghazi. Experts who reviewed the images said one drone appears to be a Chinese-made Feilong-1, while others seem to be Turkish Bayraktar TB2s, but the supplier and operator remain unclear.

articles.title: How Israel’s record budget will finance expanding illegal settlements | Benjamin Netanyahu News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Israel's record $271 billion budget includes significant funding for projects analysts say will entrench the occupation of the West Bank. A key allocation is 400 million shekels to the Ministry of Settlement and National Missions, which authorizes settlements on Palestinian land. Analysts describe the budget as a tool for imposing permanent realities on the ground and enabling a shift from military to civilian dominance in the occupied territory.

articles.title: Israeli strikes kill 7 in southern Lebanon, Hezbollah targets ground troops | Israel attacks Lebanon News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Israeli air raids killed at least seven people in southern Lebanon, according to local media reports. Lebanese Prime Minister Nawaf Salam warned the situation is 'very critical' as Israel's offensive enters its second month, displacing over one million people. Meanwhile, Hezbollah claimed rocket attacks targeting Israeli troops and infrastructure in northern Israel.

articles.title: Four children killed in nursery school stabbing attack in Uganda | Child Rights News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A man killed four children in a stabbing attack at a nursery school in Kampala, Uganda. Police said the 34-year-old suspect, who disguised himself as a parent, was apprehended, but the motive remains unknown.
