The attack, described as rare for Kampala, led to police firing into the air to disperse an angry crowd that had gathered near the school.

articles.title: Women’s Asian Cup finalists accuse governing body over equal money | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Women's Asian Cup finalists Japan and Australia renewed calls for equal prize money with the men's tournament, accusing the Asian Football Confederation (AFC) of ignoring their requests. They stated the women's tournament remains the lowest-paying continental event despite record crowds and revenue potential. The teams also highlighted challenges faced by other squads, including the Iranian team's asylum claims and kit issues for India.

articles.title: ‘Bomb back to the Stone Age’: US history of threats and carpet bombing | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article contextualizes US President Donald Trump's recent threat to bomb Iran 'back to the stone ages' within a history of similar US rhetoric and actions. It notes that such a threat, implying the destruction of civilian infrastructure characterizing modern society, would be illegal under international humanitarian law. An expert states the threat repudiates the claim the US is fighting only the Iranian regime, implying a war against Iranian society.

articles.title: Pakistan to continue with Iran-US mediation despite ‘obstacles’ | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Pakistan's Foreign Ministry stated it will continue efforts to mediate between the US and Iran despite unspecified 'obstacles,' a comment made hours after President Trump's threat against Iran. The spokesperson said both sides have confidence in Pakistan's role as a neutral intermediary. He also noted Iran has allowed Pakistani-flagged vessels to transit the Strait of Hormuz, calling it a positive step for regional stability.

articles.title: UK-led coalition of 40 countries vows action on Hormuz Strait gridlock | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The UK is hosting a virtual summit with about 40 countries to discuss diplomatic and political measures to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, which has been choked off due to the war. The US is not attending the meeting. Participants have signed a statement demanding Iran stop blocking the strait and pledging to contribute to efforts ensuring safe passage, though the focus is on non-military solutions.

articles.title: Spanish cyclist Guardeno airlifted to intensive care after crash with car | Cycling News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Spanish cyclist Jaume Guardeno is in intensive care after a serious training crash where he collided with a vehicle. His team stated he was airlifted to a hospital with serious injuries. Spanish media reported he suffered a head injury after losing control of his bike when he rode over a stone.

articles.title: Burkina Faso military, allies committing ‘horrific’ civilian abuses: HRW | Armed Groups News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A Human Rights Watch report accuses Burkina Faso's military and allied militias of committing atrocities, including the ethnic cleansing of Fulani civilians, amounting to war crimes and crimes against humanity. The report states over 1,800 civilians have been killed since 2023, with more than 1,200 deaths attributed to government forces. HRW says President Ibrahim Traore and senior commanders should be investigated for potential liability.

articles.title: UEFA’s Euro 2032 warning the latest blow to Italy after World Cup exit | World Cup 2026 News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: UEFA President Aleksander Ceferin warned that Italy risks not cohosting Euro 2032 with Turkiye due to the poor condition of its football stadiums, which he called 'some of the worst in Europe.' He stated the tournament will only be held in Italy if the infrastructure is ready. This comes days after Italy failed to qualify for the 2026 World Cup.

articles.title: Why Lebanon should join the International Criminal Court | Human Rights | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: This opinion piece argues that Lebanon should join the International Criminal Court (ICC) to seek accountability for potential Israeli war crimes committed during its ongoing offensive. The author states that, unlike Palestine which is an ICC member, Lebanon has not exhausted this legal avenue, leaving a gap in the pursuit of justice for atrocities. Joining the ICC could bring renewed political pressure and a chance for accountability.

articles.title: Can Starmer’s 40-nation coalition open the Strait of Hormuz? | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article details the UK-led coalition of about 40 countries discussing strategies to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, which Iran has blocked since the war began. The blockade has caused global oil prices to surge. The US is not participating, and the focus of the talks is on diplomatic and political measures, as no country appears willing to try to open the strait by force while fighting continues.

articles.title: European Parliament member Rima Hassan taken into French police custody | Police News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: European Parliament member Rima Hassan, a French-Palestinian activist, has been detained by French police on suspicion of 'apology for terrorism' related to a social media post. Members of her La France Insoumise party condemned the detention as an attempt to intimidate Palestine supporters. French media also reported a small amount of synthetic drugs was found on her during the arrest.

articles.title: Can the US seize Iran’s enriched uranium – and what are the risks? | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article reports that US President Donald Trump is reportedly considering sending special forces to seize Iran's stockpile of highly enriched uranium. Experts say such a military operation would be complicated and risky, facing significant chemical, logistical, and tactical hurdles. Iran is believed to have about 440kg of uranium enriched to 60%, much of it stored in underground facilities like Isfahan that have been targeted in past strikes.

overall_summary: The articles cover a range of global events from the last 6 hours, primarily focused on the ongoing US-Israel war on Iran and its regional and global consequences. Other stories include political developments in Israel, Hungary, Zimbabwe, and the UK, as well as economic and humanitarian reports.

articles.title: Israel’s death penalty law is not about executing Palestinians | Israel-Palestine conflict | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: This opinion piece argues that Israel's new death penalty law, allowing execution for 'terror offences' within 90 days, is part of a broader strategy to eliminate Palestinian existence. The author states the law institutionalizes impunity, citing recent Israeli actions including dropping charges against soldiers accused of mass rape and intensified violence in the West Bank. The timing and context are presented as a deliberate message to Palestinians.

articles.title: ‘It all depends on the crop’: Gulf crisis hits South Asia farmers | Agriculture News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article reports that the US-Israel war on Iran and the resulting closure of the Strait of Hormuz are disrupting fertiliser supplies to South Asia. This scarcity and rising cost are forcing farmers, like one profiled in Punjab, India, to make difficult financial choices affecting crops and family expenses. The region's heavy reliance on fertiliser-intensive farming makes it vulnerable to these supply chain disruptions.

articles.title: Mourners vow resistance as Iran marks Islamic Republic’s 47th anniversary | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Thousands of Iranians gathered in Tehran for the funeral of a senior IRGC commander killed by Israel, coinciding with the 47th anniversary of the Islamic Republic. Mourners pledged continued resistance against US and Israeli attacks, dismissing threats from US President Donald Trump. The report notes the ongoing war and the public display of defiance despite the loss of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in earlier strikes.

articles.title: Iran war: What is happening on day 34 of US-Israel attacks? | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: On the 34th day of the war, Iran launched a new missile wave at Israel after US President Trump claimed Washington was close to achieving its objectives and that Iran had requested a ceasefire, which Tehran denied. Key developments include a senior Iranian politician being wounded in a strike, Iran calling US demands 'irrational', and the UAE intercepting Iranian missiles. Diplomacy appears stalled with trust 'at zero'.

articles.title: Oil price surges, Asian stocks fall as Trump vows to continue Iran attacks | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Oil prices rose sharply and Asian stock markets fell after President Trump vowed to continue attacks on Iran without a clear end date, raising fears of sustained supply disruptions. The closure of the Strait of Hormuz has triggered a major energy crisis. South Korea's president urged passing a supplementary budget to address the 'worst energy security threat,' while markets across Southeast Asia and China declined.

articles.title: Iran’s ex-foreign minister Kharazi ‘gravely wounded’ in attack on his home | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Former Iranian Foreign Minister Kamal Kharazi was seriously wounded and his wife killed in an apparent assassination attempt via an air strike on his Tehran home. Iranian armed forces vowed to continue the war until the US and Israel face 'permanent surrender,' promising more destructive attacks. The report notes Kharazi had recently been involved in back-channel communications and that over 2,000 people have been killed in Iran since the war began.

articles.title: ‘Orban constantly vetoes’: Europe braces for Hungary election | Politics News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article states that Europe sees Hungary's upcoming election as pivotal due to Prime Minister Viktor Orban's frequent vetoes of EU policies on Ukraine, migration, and energy. Observers say a victory or defeat for Orban's Fidesz party will affect how the EU governs. The opposition Tisza party, which polls show leading, advocates for a stronger European orientation and an end to Hungary's obstructionist role.

articles.title: Mandelson tried to get Epstein’s ‘goddaughter’ access to 10 Downing Street | Politics News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Emails from the Epstein files reveal that British politician Peter Mandelson, while serving as business secretary, tried to help arrange access to 10 Downing Street and the House of Lords for Jeffrey Epstein's 15-year-old 'goddaughter' while Epstein was a convicted sex offender. The emails also indicate the girl later met then-Prince Andrew at Buckingham Palace. The exchange shows Epstein leveraging his relationship with Mandelson.

articles.title: French court denies extradition of ex-Tunisian President Ben Ali’s daughter | Courts News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A French court refused to extradite Halima Ben Ali, daughter of Tunisia's late deposed president, who is wanted in Tunisia on financial crime charges. The court based its decision on Tunisia's failure to provide guarantees of a trial by an independent and impartial court. Her lawyer argued extradition would be akin to a 'death sentence,' and the arrest was part of Tunisia's push to recover misappropriated assets.

articles.title: Nearly 1,200 children killed or injured in Yemen despite truce: NGO | Child Rights News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Save the Children reports that nearly 1,200 children have been killed or injured in Yemen since a UN-led truce in 2022, with nearly half the casualties caused by landmines and explosive remnants of war. The NGO warns that escalating regional tensions risk triggering a wider confrontation in Yemen, undermining progress in reducing violence. Children are disproportionately affected by these explosives, often suffering permanent disabilities.

articles.title: Trump’s tariffs one year on: How Americans are paying the price | Interactive News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: One year after implementation, the article states that Trump's tariffs have cost US households an extra $1,000 on goods, with lower-income families hardest hit. Although the Supreme Court ruled most tariffs illegal, their effects reshaped the economy, raising the average effective tariff rate to its highest since WWII. Economists found nearly 90% of the burden fell on US businesses and consumers, not foreign exporters.

articles.title: Zimbabweans fear planned constitutional change will kill political choice | Elections News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Zimbabwe is holding public hearings on a constitutional amendment bill that would extend presidential term limits and allow parliament to elect the president. Critics fear this will entrench the ruling ZANU-PF party's power and eliminate genuine political choice. The report presents divided public opinion, with some supporters arguing it would reduce election violence and opponents saying MPs are too detached from citizens' woes.

overall_summary: The articles cover three distinct topics from the last six hours: a legal and personal update from golfer Tiger Woods, a political analysis of a speech by former US President Donald Trump on the Iran conflict, and a report on the successful launch and planned trajectory of NASA's Artemis II moon mission.

articles.title: Tiger Woods declines 2027 Ryder Cup captaincy; can leave US for treatment | Golf News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Tiger Woods has declined the 2027 Ryder Cup captaincy to focus on his health. A Florida judge granted his request to leave the United States to enter an inpatient treatment facility as he faces misdemeanor DUI charges related to a recent car crash. According to court documents, his lawyer argued that treatment abroad is necessary due to his complex clinical needs and the inability to receive effective care in the US amid public scrutiny.

articles.title: Trump’s primetime speech on Iran war: Key takeaways | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: In a primetime address, former US President Donald Trump reiterated familiar points about the war on Iran, stating it is necessary, already won, must continue, and will end soon. The article notes that analysts expected a major announcement, but the speech contained no new details on how the conflict would conclude. Experts cited in the piece characterized the address as a repetition of past statements, suggesting a lack of a concrete plan.

articles.title: How long will Artemis II take to reach the moon, and what happens next? | Science and Technology News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: NASA's Artemis II mission has successfully launched, sending four astronauts on a journey to the moon's vicinity for the first time since the Apollo era. The Orion capsule is expected to reach the moon around April 6, following a 'free-return' trajectory that will swing around it without landing. The launch proceeded after engineers resolved technical issues, including monitoring for hydrogen leaks that had caused previous delays.

overall_summary: This batch of articles covers major developments in business, international relations, space exploration, security operations, and natural disasters. Key stories include SpaceX's IPO filing, US sanctions changes in Venezuela, NASA's moon mission launch, a UN-backed force arriving in Haiti, an FBI investigation in Cuba, and a major earthquake in Indonesia.

articles.title: SpaceX files to go public, paving way to make Elon Musk first trillionaire
articles.summary: SpaceX has taken steps for its first public share offering, which could become one of the largest IPOs in modern history and potentially make founder Elon Musk the world's first trillionaire. The company, considered the most valuable privately held firm, filed for an IPO that could raise up to $75bn and take place as soon as June or July. Despite Musk's controversial public profile, analysts note SpaceX is operationally mature, technologically advanced, and profitable, which may drive investor enthusiasm.

articles.title: US removes sanctions on Venezuela’s interim President Delcy Rodriguez
articles.summary: The United States has lifted sanctions against Venezuela's interim President Delcy Rodriguez, following the abduction and imprisonment of her predecessor Nicolas Maduro. Rodriguez hailed the decision as a step toward normalizing relations and called for more sanctions to be lifted. Since taking office, Rodriguez has taken a conciliatory approach toward the US, including signing laws to open Venezuela's oil and mining sectors to private investment.

articles.title: NASA successfully launches historic Artemis II moon mission
articles.summary: NASA's Artemis II mission has successfully launched from Florida, sending four astronauts on a journey around the moon—the first human travel beyond low-Earth orbit in over 50 years. The crew will conduct a nearly 10-day mission, performing extensive systems checks before a critical engine burn to head toward the moon. The launch proceeded despite pre-launch tensions over hydrogen fuel leaks and technical issues with the flight-termination system and capsule batteries, which were resolved.

articles.title: First troops from UN-backed Gang Suppression Force arrive in Haiti
articles.summary: The first foreign troops from a new UN-backed Gang Suppression Force have arrived in Haiti to combat escalating gang violence, with an initial group from Chad deploying alongside a UN special representative. The force, authorized by the UN Security Council, is expected to grow to 5,500 personnel with a 12-month mandate and will have powers to make arrests, unlike the previous Kenyan-led mission. The deployment follows a history of troubled foreign interventions in Haiti and comes amid widespread violence that intensified after the 2021 assassination of President Jovenel Moise.

articles.title: FBI agents from US arrive in Cuba to probe lethal speedboat shooting
articles.summary: A technical team from the FBI has arrived in Cuba to conduct an independent investigation into a deadly shootout between Cuban border patrol and a Florida-tagged speedboat carrying armed individuals. Cuban authorities say 10 Cuban nationals attempted to enter the island with weapons and ammunition on February 25, resulting in five deaths. The US embassy stated the investigation will verify facts independently, amid heightened tensions following a US oil blockade on Cuba and the abduction of Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro, a key Cuban ally.

articles.title: Magnitude 7.4 quake hits off Indonesia’s Ternate, tsunami warning lifted
articles.summary: A magnitude 7.4 earthquake struck off the coast of Ternate, Indonesia, killing at least one person and initially triggering a tsunami warning that was later lifted. The quake, which occurred at a depth of 35km, caused building damage and led to evacuation preparations in some cities. Tsunami waves up to 75 centimeters were recorded in some areas, but the Pacific Tsunami Warning Center stated the threat had passed about two hours after the tremor.

overall_summary: The articles cover a range of international developments, including diplomatic efforts to reopen the Strait of Hormuz amid conflict, India's controversial census, and new Israeli legislation criticized as discriminatory. Other stories report on protests, a military explosion in Burundi, a US Supreme Court case on birthright citizenship, Ecuador's anti-crime campaign, and Iranian President Pezeshkian's open letter to the American public.

articles.title: UK to host meeting of 35 countries on reopening Strait of Hormuz | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The UK will host a virtual meeting of 35 countries to discuss diplomatic and political measures to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, which has been effectively closed due to the US-Israeli war on Iran. Prime Minister Keir Starmer stated reopening the strait 'will not be easy' and that military planners will also convene to plan for safe access after the fighting stops. The closure has caused global energy prices to soar, prompting countries to release strategic reserves.

articles.title: History’s biggest census: Why India’s new population count is controversial | Politics News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: India has launched the world's largest census, a $1.24bn exercise delayed by five years, which will include caste enumeration for the first time in nearly a century. Over three million officials will survey about 1.4 billion people using digital tools across two phases, collecting data on household composition, living conditions, and socioeconomic details. The census is scheduled to conclude next year.

articles.title: 'This is an apartheid regime': Critics decry Israel's new death penalty law | Israel-Palestine conflict News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Israel has passed a death penalty law that applies solely to Palestinians convicted by military courts in the occupied West Bank for deadly attacks classified as 'terrorism'. Critics, including Israeli rights groups, describe it as part of an 'apartheid' legal system that codifies discrimination, noting that Palestinians face a 99.74% conviction rate in military courts, while Israelis are tried in civilian courts with a much lower conviction rate.

articles.title: Iran authorities await war 'victory' as supporters mark 1979 anniversary | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Iranian government supporters and officials, including President Masoud Pezeshkian, took to the streets to mark Islamic Republic Day, celebrating the 1979 referendum that solidified the Islamic Republic's hold on power. The celebrations occurred as US and Israeli attacks continued, with Washington bombing the site of the former US embassy in Tehran. Authorities urged continued public presence on the streets to fend off dissent and support the war effort.

articles.title: Palestinians in West Bank protest, strike against Israeli death penalty law | Israel-Palestine conflict News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Palestinians in the occupied West Bank and East Jerusalem held a general strike and protests against Israel's new death penalty law that targets Palestinians. Hundreds marched in Ramallah, with shops and public institutions closed, while Israeli soldiers forced some shop owners to open their businesses. The UN human rights chief condemned the law, stating its application to Palestinians would constitute a war crime.

articles.title: Burundi says at least 13 killed, dozens injured in military base blast | Military News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: At least 13 civilians were killed and 57 others wounded in an explosion at a military ammunition depot in Bujumbura, Burundi, caused by an electrical short circuit. The blast destroyed houses, vehicles, and military equipment, with three soldiers among the injured. Authorities warned citizens not to touch unexploded munitions and to report them by phone.

articles.title: 'Hits close to home': US Supreme Court hears birthright citizenship case | Donald Trump News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The US Supreme Court heard oral arguments on President Donald Trump's effort to end birthright citizenship, which grants automatic citizenship to anyone born in the United States. Trump attended the hearing, a first for a sitting president, as protesters gathered outside. Lawyers for the administration argued the practice is based on a 'misreading' of the Constitution, while challengers said it runs counter to constitutional and federal law.

articles.title: Ecuador claims 28% drop in homicides amid concerns over anti-crime campaign | Government News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Ecuador's government announced a 28% drop in intentional homicides in March compared to last year, crediting President Daniel Noboa's hardline anti-crime campaign and collaboration with the US. Officials reported 4,300 arrests and 2,200 search warrants executed as part of the crackdown. However, the campaign has raised concerns about potential human rights abuses amid a broader offensive against cartels in Latin America.

articles.title: 'Which interests being served by war?' Iran's Pezeshkian asks US public | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian published an open letter to the American public, urging them to look beyond 'distortions' and question whose interests are served by the US-Israeli war on Iran. He rejected portrayals of Iran as a threat, noted Iranian participation in nuclear talks when attacks occurred, and stated that attacking Iran's infrastructure constitutes a war crime with broader consequences. The letter came hours after President Trump threatened further attacks unless the Strait of Hormuz is reopened.

overall_summary: This batch of articles covers a range of international news from the last six hours, including reports on refugee abuse in Europe, geopolitical tensions over the Iran war, significant legal and political developments in the US and UK, and major sports events. The stories highlight conflicts, human rights issues, and cultural moments across different regions.

articles.title: Assaulted, robbed: Refugees abused on Bosnia-Croatia border | Refugees News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article reports that more than a dozen refugees and migrants attempting to reach the EU from Bosnia have accused Croatian border officials and police of violence and robbery. They describe being forcibly returned to Bosnia in illegal pushbacks, often left bruised and stripped of belongings. Testimonies include accounts of physical assault, with one man alleging a beating by Bosnian police left him permanently disabled.

articles.title: Iraq celebrates qualification for FIFA World Cup 2026 | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Iraq's national football team qualified for the FIFA World Cup for the first time since 1986 with a 2-1 victory over Bolivia. The celebration in Baghdad occurred despite the country being involved in the US-Israel war on Iran, which has caused casualties. The team faced logistical challenges due to the war, including suspended air travel, but secured their spot in Group I.

articles.title: US Supreme Court to hear constitutional test of birthright citizenship | Donald Trump News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The US Supreme Court is set to hear a case challenging President Donald Trump's executive order that seeks to end birthright citizenship, a long-standing interpretation of the 14th Amendment. Advocates argue the order represents an overreach of presidential power to rewrite the Constitution. The case will be heard by a conservative-majority court that has previously ruled in Trump's favour on immigration issues.

articles.title: How are NATO allies pushing back against Trump’s Iran war demands? | News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: NATO allies are resisting US President Donald Trump's demands for support in the US-Israel war on Iran, causing a transatlantic rift. Several countries, including Spain and Italy, have denied the use of their military bases or airspace for the conflict. Trump has responded with criticism, threatening to pull out of NATO and cut trade with uncooperative allies.

articles.title: UK finds pro-Palestine protest organisers guilty of breaching police rules | Protests News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Two UK civil society leaders, Ben Jamal and Chris Nineham, were found guilty of breaching police conditions during a pro-Palestine demonstration in London in January 2025. The court ruled they failed to keep the protest within a designated area, with Jamal also convicted of inciting others to breach conditions. Human Rights Watch criticized the verdict as a political move to silence dissent.

articles.title: ‘Permanent scar’: Iran minister on US-Israeli attacks on monuments | Conflict | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Iran's cultural heritage minister states that US-Israeli attacks have damaged at least 56 museums and historical monuments, calling it a deliberate assault on Iranian identity. He describes the destruction at sites like Tehran's Golestan Palace as causing irreversible loss, with some restoration expected to take years. The minister criticizes the international community, including UNESCO, for not intervening to protect cultural property.

articles.title: Minerals for aid: Are new US health deals ‘exploiting’ African countries? | Health News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The US has proposed bilateral health agreements to African nations that request sensitive health data or access to minerals in return for aid, following global health aid cuts. Zimbabwe and Zambia have pushed back against what they see as 'lopsided' and 'exploitative' terms. Critics argue that linking aid to mineral extraction could have negative consequences for African countries and global health security.

articles.title: ‘The World Cup curse’: Outcry as Italy miss out for third time in a row | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Italy failed to qualify for the FIFA World Cup for the third consecutive time after losing a playoff to Bosnia and Herzegovina in a penalty shootout. The defeat has caused outrage in Italy, with the sport minister calling for the football federation president to resign. The loss extends a period of poor performance for the national team since their last World Cup win in 2006.

articles.title: Russia claims to take full control of Ukraine’s Luhansk region | Russia-Ukraine war News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Russia's defence ministry claims its forces have taken full control of Ukraine's Luhansk region, an area it had not fully captured since the 2022 invasion. There was no immediate confirmation from Ukraine. The report comes alongside claims of advances in other villages and renewed Russian demands for a Ukrainian withdrawal from the Donbas area.

articles.title: Ali Zafar wins defamation case against Meesha Shafi: Why it matters | Gender Equity News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A Lahore court ruled in favour of singer Ali Zafar in a defamation case against fellow singer Meesha Shafi, ordering her to pay damages. The case stems from Shafi's 2018 sexual harassment allegations, which the court found to be unproven and defamatory. Shafi's lawyer stated they will appeal the verdict, challenging the court's interpretation of evidence.

articles.title: Iran denies Trump’s claim Iranian president requested ceasefire | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A senior Iranian official denied US President Donald Trump's claim that Iran's new president had requested a ceasefire in the ongoing war. Trump stated on social media that the US would continue 'blasting Iran into oblivion' until the Strait of Hormuz is reopened. The denial comes ahead of a scheduled speech by Trump providing an update on the conflict.

articles.title: Iran War: What a Marine Expeditionary Unit is – and other US military terms | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article explains US military deployments to the Gulf amid the war with Iran, detailing units like Carrier Strike Groups and Amphibious Ready Groups. It notes the deployment of a third aircraft carrier and the arrival of Marine Expeditionary Units, which are rapid-response forces for sea-based combat. The piece serves as a glossary of terms related to the US military buildup in the region.

articles.title: Mojtaba Khamenei’s rumoured injury or death won’t change Iran’s trajectory | US-Israel war on Iran | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: This opinion piece argues that rumours about Mojtaba Khamenei's injury or death are less significant than the structural transformation of Iran's political economy. It describes the consolidation of a 'military-bonyad complex' linking security institutions to parastatal capital, a process deepened by international sanctions. The author contends that this system's logic will persist regardless of changes in leadership.

articles.title: Russia says will continue helping Cuba after first oil shipment arrives | Oil and Gas News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Russia has pledged continued assistance to Cuba after a Russian tanker delivered the first shipment of crude oil to the island in three months, alleviating a severe energy crisis. A Russian foreign ministry spokeswoman called on the US to lift its energy blockade on Cuba. The US allowed the shipment to pass for humanitarian reasons, though President Trump dismissed its significance.

overall_summary: The articles cover a range of global events from the last six hours. The dominant theme is the ongoing US-Israel war on Iran, with updates on military strikes, diplomatic stances, and regional fallout. Other major stories include sports news related to World Cup qualifiers and incidents of racism, significant political developments in India, and the imminent launch of NASA's Artemis II moon mission.

articles.title: Iran war: What is happening on day 33 of US-Israel attacks? | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: On day 33 of the conflict, US-Israeli air attacks continue across Iran, targeting industrial and civilian sites including pharmaceutical plants, steel facilities, and a desalination plant. US President Donald Trump stated the war could end in two to three weeks without a deal, but Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said Tehran has 'zero' trust in talks with Washington. The article also notes NATO allies restricting support and China pushing for a ceasefire.

articles.title: Spain’s FA condemns Islamophobic chants during game with Egypt | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The Royal Spanish Football Federation and coach Luis de la Fuente have condemned anti-Muslim chants that marred a pre-World Cup friendly against Egypt in Barcelona. Egypt's national anthem was jeered, and the public announcement system repeatedly appealed to fans to stop the offensive comments. This incident is described as part of a string of similar racist and Islamophobic incidents in Spanish football in recent years.

articles.title: What is NASA’s Artemis II moon mission, and when will it launch? | Space News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: NASA's Artemis II mission is set to launch, sending four astronauts on a lunar flyby, marking the first crewed mission to the moon since 1972. The launch window opens on April 1, with weather being the primary constraint after previous technical delays. The mission's goal is to test the Orion spacecraft's life support and other systems in deep space as part of NASA's broader programme to return humans to the moon.

articles.title: Iran attacks cause fire in Kuwait, Bahrain; kill man in UAE | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Iranian drone attacks have caused a large fire at fuel tanks in Kuwait's international airport and a fire at a company facility in Bahrain. In the UAE, shrapnel from a drone interception killed a Bangladeshi national. The article also reports a tanker was hit by a projectile near Qatar, with Qatar's defence ministry stating it was targeted by Iranian cruise missiles.

articles.title: War on Iran: Three key takeaways from Araghchi’s interview with Al Jazeera | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: In an interview, Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi confirmed direct contact with US envoy Steve Witkoff but denied it constituted negotiations, stating Iran has 'zero' trust in the US. He argued that Iran and Oman would decide the future of the Strait of Hormuz after the war. Araghchi also referenced Iran's preparations for a potential US ground invasion.

articles.title: ‘Gerrymandering’ in India’s Assam cuts Muslim representation before vote | Politics | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A redrawing of electoral constituency boundaries in Assam state has significantly reduced the number of seats where Muslims form a majority, down from about 35 to about 20 out of 126. Analysts and activists describe this as 'communal gerrymandering' that could politically marginalise Assam's Muslim population ahead of state elections on April 9. The ruling BJP has chosen Hindu candidates for constituencies that previously had a chance of electing Muslim representatives.

articles.title: India begins world’s largest population census | Politics News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: India has begun a yearlong national population census involving over three million officials, which will collect data on housing, living conditions, and later, social and economic characteristics including caste and religion. The data could reshape welfare programmes and political representation, potentially leading to a redrawing of India's political map. This is the first census since 2011, and India is now estimated to be the world's most populous nation.

articles.title: ‘An incredible feeling’: Turkiye beat Kosovo to end 24-year World Cup wait | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Turkiye has qualified for the FIFA World Cup for the first time since 2002 after a tense 1-0 playoff win against Kosovo. The victory, secured by a Kerem Akturkoglu goal, prompted wild celebrations among players and fans. Coach Vincenzo Montella expressed pride in his squad, which blends emerging young stars with seasoned players.

articles.title: Which teams are in the FIFA World Cup 2026? | World Cup 2026 News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The 48-team lineup for the 2026 FIFA World Cup is now complete following the final playoffs, with Iraq and the Democratic Republic of the Congo among the last to qualify. The tournament, hosted by Canada, Mexico, and the US, will be the largest ever, featuring debutants like Cape Verde and Uzbekistan. The article provides a breakdown of the teams and groups, noting Argentina will defend its title.

articles.title: Israel kills 7 people in Beirut, Hezbollah fights invasion in south Lebanon | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Israeli attacks on Beirut's southern suburbs and the town of Khaldeh have killed at least seven people, according to Lebanon's health ministry. The Israeli military said it aimed to kill a senior Hezbollah commander. Meanwhile, Hezbollah claimed cross-border attacks and said its fighters were engaged in clashes with Israeli soldiers in southern Lebanon, where Israel has launched a ground invasion.

overall_summary: The articles cover a major sports qualification event and a significant political development. The main story reports Iraq's historic qualification for the 2026 FIFA World Cup after a long absence. Another article details a major political shift in the United Kingdom.

articles.title: Iraq defeat Bolivia 2-1 to qualify for FIFA World Cup 2026 | World Cup 2026 News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Iraq has qualified for the FIFA World Cup for the first time since 1986 by defeating Bolivia 2-1 in an intercontinental playoff match in Mexico. Goals from Ali Al-Hamadi and Aymen Hussein secured the victory, ending a 30-year wait. The team overcame significant travel challenges due to conflict in the Middle East to participate in the playoff.

overall_summary: This batch of articles covers a range of global news from the last 6 hours, including major sports results from World Cup 2026 qualifying playoffs, significant US legal and political developments under the Trump administration, and the ongoing economic and energy impacts of international tensions, particularly involving Cuba and Iran.

articles.title: Turkiye and Sweden book World Cup 2026 spots as Kosovo and Poland bow out | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Turkiye qualified for the World Cup for the first time since 2002 by beating Kosovo 1-0, ending a 24-year wait. Sweden secured their spot by defeating Poland 3-2, with Viktor Gyokeres scoring a late winner. Sweden will join Group F alongside the Netherlands, Japan, and Tunisia for the 2026 tournament.

articles.title: FBI agents who investigated Trump file lawsuit alleging retaliatory firing | Courts News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Three former FBI agents have filed a class-action lawsuit alleging they were wrongfully fired in retaliation for their work on an investigation into Donald Trump's efforts to stay in power after the 2020 election. They claim their termination, ordered by FBI Director Kash Patel, was part of a 'retribution campaign' and violated their due process. The agents had been assigned to support special counsel Jack Smith's investigation, which led to Trump's 2023 indictment before being dropped after his re-election.

articles.title: 'I need to leave': Cuba's tourism workers reel from US oil blockade | Tourism News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Cuba's tourism industry has collapsed due to a de facto US oil blockade imposed in January, leaving workers like taxi drivers and tour guides struggling with little paid work. Tourism, once a pillar of Cuba's economy, has seen visitor numbers plummet from a peak of 4.8 million in 2018 to 1.6 million last year. Workers report the blockade has created an economic crisis, with many considering leaving the island as they can no longer afford basic necessities.

articles.title: Russian tanker reaches Cuba amid critical energy shortage | Oil and Gas News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A Russian oil tanker, the Anatoly Kolodkin, has delivered 730,000 barrels of oil to Cuba, the first such shipment in three months, to address a critical energy shortage. The US allowed the sanctioned vessel to proceed for humanitarian reasons. The fuel could meet Cuba's energy needs for up to 10 days, providing temporary relief from blackouts that have crippled hospitals, transport, and farm production.

articles.title: US Supreme Court rejects Colorado ban on LGBTQ child 'conversion therapy' | Courts News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The US Supreme Court, in an 8-1 decision, ruled against a Colorado law banning 'conversion therapy' for LGBTQ children, arguing it violates free speech protections. The majority held that states cannot limit what a therapist discusses with a patient, even if aimed at changing a child's LGBTQ identity. Justice Ketanji Brown Jackson dissented, warning the decision impairs states' ability to regulate medical care and overlooks the practice's documented harms.

articles.title: Italy out as Bosnia and Herzegovina reach World Cup 2026 on penalties | World Cup 2026 News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Bosnia and Herzegovina defeated Italy 4-1 on penalties after a 1-1 draw in extra time, eliminating the four-time champions from World Cup 2026 qualification. Italy, reduced to 10 men in the first half, will miss a third consecutive World Cup finals. Bosnia secured a spot in Group B, marking their second World Cup appearance and first since 2014.

articles.title: Tiger Woods pleads not guilty to DUI in car crash where opioids found | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Golfer Tiger Woods pleaded not guilty to driving under the influence charges in Florida. A sheriff's report stated he showed signs of impairment at the crash scene, with bloodshot eyes, dilated pupils, and opioid pills in his pocket. Deputies reported his movements were slow and lethargic, and he told them he had taken prescription medication before the accident.

articles.title: US exempts Gulf of Mexico oil drillers from endangered species protections | Environment News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A Trump administration committee has unanimously exempted Gulf of Mexico oil and gas drillers from Endangered Species Act protections, citing national security and energy competitiveness amid the war on Iran. Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth argued the rules could hobble US energy production. Environmental groups plan to challenge the decision, which affects species like the critically endangered Rice's whale.

articles.title: After Strait of Hormuz opens, turmoil would still last months, analysts say | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Analysts warn that even if the Strait of Hormuz reopens soon, global supply chain disruptions will persist for months due to a backlog of about 2,000 stranded ships and damaged regional infrastructure. Shipping experts note it will take time to clear the accumulated oil, gas, and goods, with logistics facilities operating at full capacity. The effective shutdown has forced diversions and caused significant delays in energy and trade flows.

articles.title: DR Congo beat Jamaica to reach World Cup via intercontinental finals | World Cup 2026 News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The Democratic Republic of the Congo qualified for the World Cup by beating Jamaica 1-0 in extra time of an intercontinental playoff, with former Manchester United defender Axel Tuanzebe scoring the decisive goal. This marks only the DRC's second World Cup appearance, their first since 1974. They will join Group G alongside Portugal, Colombia, and Uzbekistan for the 2026 tournament.

articles.title: Trump tells allies 'get your own oil', says Iran war could end in 2-3 weeks | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: President Donald Trump stated the US could stop attacking Iran within two to three weeks and that no deal is necessary to end the war, despite Iranian officials denying any negotiations are occurring. Trump claimed the US would leave once Iran is set back 'into the Stone Ages' and unable to develop nuclear weapons. His comments come as domestic US petrol prices surpass $4 a gallon due to the conflict's disruption of energy supplies.

articles.title: Judge temporarily halts Trump's $400m White House ballroom project | Donald Trump News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A US district judge has temporarily halted President Donald Trump's $400m White House ballroom project, ruling construction cannot proceed without Congressional authorization. Judge Richard Leon stated the president does not own the White House and exceeded his authority by demolishing the historic East Wing. The ruling pauses the project while a lawsuit by the National Trust for Historic Preservation continues, though the Trump administration has filed an appeal.

overall_summary: The articles cover escalating conflicts in the Middle East, including a deepening Israeli invasion of Lebanon and major US-Israel attacks on Iran. They also report on regional economic impacts, a kidnapping in Iraq, UK activist arrests, and separate updates on US job markets and Ukraine's diplomacy.

articles.title: UN aid chief warns of new Israeli occupation in southern Lebanon | Conflict News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The UN humanitarian chief asked the Security Council how it will protect civilians in Lebanon as Israel expands its ground invasion. He warned of potential coercive displacement and a 'new addition to the list of occupied territories,' referencing Israeli ministers' stated aims. The meeting followed Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz saying Israel plans to occupy parts of southern Lebanon after the current escalation.

articles.title: UK accused of ‘intimidation tactics’ against bailed pro-Palestine activists | Israel-Palestine conflict News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Civil rights groups accuse the UK of intimidation after two young women linked to Palestine Action were arrested while on bail. Qesser Zuhrah was detained over a social media post, and Audrey Corno was arrested for allegedly tampering with an electronic tag, which she denies. Both had previously been imprisoned over alleged involvement in raids on military hardware manufacturers in 2024.

articles.title: Foreign journalist kidnapped in Iraq: Interior Ministry | Media News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Iraq's Interior Ministry confirmed a foreign journalist was kidnapped in Baghdad by 'unknown individuals,' without identifying the reporter. Security forces arrested one suspect and seized a vehicle used in the abduction. The Committee to Protect Journalists expressed deep concern, citing reports that US freelance journalist Shelley Kittleson was abducted, and a US State Department official said they were aware of the reported kidnapping of an American journalist.

articles.title: One month of war on Iran cost Arab countries up to $194bn: UNDP | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A UNDP report estimates the US-Israel war on Iran caused a regional GDP decline of 3.7 to 6 percent in one month, equivalent to $120bn to $194bn. The conflict is projected to lead to 3.7 million lost jobs and push about four million more people into poverty, with impacts concentrated in the Levant and fragile states like Sudan and Yemen. The report warns Lebanon is especially impacted due to ongoing air strikes and large-scale displacement.

articles.title: US-Israel launch major attacks as Iranian authorities maintain defiance | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: US and Israeli strikes hit Tehran, Isfahan, and other Iranian cities, with bunker-buster bombs causing massive secondary explosions near Isfahan. Strikes damaged a major power line in Tehran and targeted a key pharmaceuticals company, among other industrial and civilian sites. Iranian authorities say more than 2,000 people have been killed since the war began on February 28, with residential areas, schools, hospitals, and historical sites impacted.

articles.title: Iran’s FM confirms contact with US envoy Witkoff, denies talks under way | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi confirmed messages have been exchanged with the US, including directly with envoy Steve Witkoff, but denied negotiations are taking place. He stated Iran has 'zero' trust in negotiations with the US, citing past experiences like the US withdrawal from the 2015 nuclear deal. Araghchi also addressed the Strait of Hormuz, saying it is closed only to ships of countries at war with Iran and that its future security will be decided by Oman and Iran after the conflict.

articles.title: Analysis: Hezbollah backs Iran ambassador as conflict deepens Lebanon rifts | Israel attacks Lebanon News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Iran's ambassador to Beirut remains in Lebanon despite being declared persona non grata and given a deadline to leave, highlighting deep political divides. The situation underscores the contest between supporters and opponents of Hezbollah, which is backed by Iran. The analysis notes Hezbollah's power has waned in recent years, but the group has been re-engaged in war with Israel reportedly under IRGC command amid the broader US-Israeli war on Iran.

articles.title: Hiring in the US drops to pandemic lows as job market under Trump stagnates | Business and Economy News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: US job openings dropped to 6.882 million in February, the lowest level in six years, and hiring fell to its lowest since March 2020. The report indicates stagnation in the job market, with fewer people quitting their jobs. An economist links declining consumer sentiment to frustration with President Donald Trump's second-term policies, citing rising costs and economic uncertainty.

articles.title: Ukraine to ask US mediators to pass on Easter ceasefire offer to Russia | Russia-Ukraine war News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said he will ask US mediators to relay an offer of an Easter truce to Russia, halting attacks on energy facilities. He made the statement ahead of online talks with US envoys, saying Ukraine is ready for compromises except on dignity and sovereignty. The offer comes amid stalled peace talks and a day after Zelenskyy said allies sent 'signals' about possibly scaling back Ukrainian strikes on Russia's oil sector.

overall_summary: The articles cover a range of international news from the last six hours, including sports controversies, ongoing conflicts in the Middle East, regional diplomacy, and domestic political tensions. Major themes include the Israel-Palestine conflict, the US-Israel war on Iran, and related regional and global repercussions.

articles.title: Caster Semenya pledges to fight against Olympic gender-testing policy | Athletics News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Double Olympic champion Caster Semenya says she will fight the IOC's new gender-testing policy for the female category, which she argues undermines women's rights. The policy, involving cheek swab tests for the SRY gene, is intended to become a universal rule. Semenya, who has a Difference of Sexual Development (DSD), disputes the scientific basis for excluding athletes like her, stating athletic success comes from hard work.

articles.title: A Gaza mother waits in agony: Did Israel kill her daughter, or arrest her? | Israel-Palestine conflict News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Tahrir Abu Mady holds a death certificate for her daughter Malak, issued after remains were found in their bombed home in Khan Younis. However, a list from released detainees suggests Malak might be alive in Israeli custody, with her status listed as 'No information available'. Human rights groups estimate thousands are forcibly disappeared in Gaza, leaving families in a state of unresolved grief and uncertainty.

articles.title: Drones strike Russia’s Ust-Luga port again as EU officials visit Ukraine | Russia-Ukraine war News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Ukrainian drones struck Russia's Ust-Luga oil port for the fifth time in ten days, part of escalated attacks on Russian oil infrastructure. The regional governor reported injuries and building damage. Meanwhile, Ukraine said its air defences shot down most of a large Russian drone barrage overnight, and EU officials visited Kyiv to mark the anniversary of the Bucha massacre, reaffirming support.

articles.title: Hundreds rally in West Bank against Israeli death penalty for Palestinians | Israel-Palestine conflict News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Hundreds protested across the West Bank against a new Israeli law allowing the death penalty for Palestinians convicted of deadly attacks. The law, passed by the Knesset, has been condemned by human rights groups and EU officials as discriminatory and a breach of international law. Demonstrators included families of prisoners, civil society groups, and political factions.

articles.title: Pakistan cricketer Fakhar Zaman handed two-match PSL ban for ball tampering | Cricket News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Pakistan batsman Fakhar Zaman received a two-match ban after being found guilty of ball tampering during a Pakistan Super League match. Video footage showed him altering the ball's condition. His team, Lahore Qalandars, was penalized five runs and lost the match. Team captain Shaheen Shah Afridi was also fined for breaching tournament security protocols.

articles.title: What’s Israel’s death penalty law that only applies to Palestinians? | Occupied West Bank News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article examines Israel's new death penalty law, which rights groups condemn as discriminatory because it primarily applies to Palestinians, not Israeli Jews. The law's passage was celebrated by far-right lawmakers. European countries have expressed concern over its discriminatory nature, with critics arguing it entrenches a two-tiered justice system under apartheid.

articles.title: Will China join Pakistan-led efforts to mediate US-Iran peace? | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Pakistan's Deputy PM Ishaq Dar visited China to discuss regional developments, following Pakistan's hosting of a quadrilateral meeting aimed at mediating US-Iran peace talks. China has expressed support for Pakistan's mediation efforts. Analysts suggest Dar's trip seeks to determine if China will move beyond rhetorical support to play a more active role in the diplomatic push.

articles.title: Cancer drug facility, religious site hit in Israeli-US strikes on Iran | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Israeli-US strikes damaged a major Iranian pharmaceutical company that produces cancer drugs and anaesthetics, according to the Iranian government. Separate attacks hit a Shia religious site in Zanjan. Strikes were also reported in Isfahan and Kermanshah provinces, with casualties reported. The attacks occurred as diplomatic efforts have stalled.

articles.title: War on Iran: US’s history of making other nations pay for conflicts | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The White House said President Donald Trump is considering asking Arab nations to cover the costs of the US-Israel war on Iran, similar to allied funding during the 1990 Gulf War. An analyst argued Israel should bear the cost, as Gulf states did not advocate for this war. The article notes the US has a history of getting other countries to pay for its conflicts.

articles.title: Where to invest during Iran war? Ask – or don’t – Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Iran's parliamentary speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf has used social media to offer financial advice and critique US market influence during the war. His posts, blending humour and threats, are seen as part of Iran's asymmetric warfare strategy to exert economic pressure. Analysts suggest this aims to exploit economic vulnerabilities to force a US diplomatic retreat.

articles.title: Iran will play World Cup games in US despite war, FIFA’s Infantino says | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: FIFA President Gianni Infantino stated Iran will play its 2026 World Cup matches in the United States as scheduled, despite the ongoing war and Iran's request to relocate games to Mexico. Infantino, attending an Iran friendly in Turkiye, expressed confidence in Iran's participation. This follows conflicting statements from US President Trump about the team's safety.

articles.title: The attack on the right to protest in the UK is not just about Palestine | Protests | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article discusses a trial of UK pro-Palestinian activists, seen as a test case for protest rights. It reveals close ties between police and Zionist groups, alleging police imposed protest conditions under pressure. The defence argues the right to protest was not facilitated properly, highlighting concerns about shrinking space for dissent in Britain beyond the Palestine issue.

articles.title: Venezuela’s ‘Chavismo’ movement faces a crossroads after US attack | US-Venezuela Tensions News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Following a US attack that abducted President Nicolas Maduro, Venezuela's Chavismo movement is divided between resisting US demands and cooperating for potential economic gains. The article profiles a supporter who views Maduro's capture as a kidnapping, while analysts note the movement is adapting pragmatically to maintain power amidst this crisis.

articles.title: Cuba crisis explained: Who holds power, and could Diaz-Canel be replaced? | Donald Trump News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Cuba is experiencing severe blackouts and fuel shortages due to a de facto US oil blockade, straining all aspects of life. US President Trump has threatened military action. The article examines the crisis's impact and questions about leadership stability, noting one recent Russian oil shipment is insufficient to resolve the infrastructure collapse.

articles.title: Qatar says Iran’s attacks on neighbours crossed ‘many red lines’ | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Qatar's foreign ministry spokesperson said Iran's attacks on neighbouring countries, including Qatar, have crossed 'many red lines' and are damaging bilateral relations. Qatar called for de-escalation and warned against attacks on nuclear or energy infrastructure. While not directly involved in Pakistan's mediation, Qatar supports those peace efforts.

overall_summary: The batch covers two distinct stories from the last six hours. One is a political report about the Pentagon denying allegations of improper financial activity by the defense secretary's broker. The other is a sports article detailing a dominant NBA victory by the Los Angeles Lakers.

articles.title: Pentagon denies that Hegseth’s broker sought investment before Iran war | Business and Economy News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The Pentagon has demanded an immediate retraction of a Financial Times report alleging a broker for Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth sought a multimillion-dollar investment in a defense-related fund before the war on Iran. A Pentagon spokesman called the allegation entirely false and fabricated. The article notes Al Jazeera could not independently confirm the report and that the mentioned ETF has fallen in value since the war began.

articles.title: LeBron James records triple-double as Lakers dominate Wizards | Basketball News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: LeBron James recorded his 125th regular-season triple-double with 21 points, 12 assists, and 10 rebounds to lead the Los Angeles Lakers to a 120-101 win over the Washington Wizards. The victory was the Lakers' 12th in their past 13 games. The win did not immediately clinch a playoff berth due to a Phoenix Suns victory the same night.

overall_summary: The articles cover two major conflicts and a violent incident. The first three articles report on Israel's passage of a controversial death penalty law targeting Palestinians, which has drawn international condemnation. The next two articles detail developments in the US-led war against Iran, including diplomatic statements and funding discussions. A final article reports on a deadly gang attack in Haiti.

articles.title: Israel’s parliament passes death penalty bill targeting Palestinians | Israel-Palestine conflict News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Israel's parliament passed a bill instructing military courts to impose the death penalty on Palestinians convicted of killing Israelis in acts of 'terror', but it does not apply the same penalty to Jewish Israelis convicted of killing Palestinians. The law, championed by far-right National Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir, was approved by 62 lawmakers. The Palestinian Authority called it a 'war crime' and a breach of the Fourth Geneva Convention.

articles.title: The war in Iran: Key takeaways from Al Jazeera’s interview with Marco Rubio | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: In an interview, US Secretary of State Marco Rubio said talks with Iran are ongoing through intermediaries and that the US military campaign will continue until Tehran abandons its nuclear and missile programs. He warned the Strait of Hormuz will be kept open 'one way or another' and said war objectives could be achieved 'in weeks, not months'. Rubio also stated the US would welcome political change in Iran but it is not an official objective.

articles.title: 'Dangerous escalation': World reacts to Israel passing death penalty law | Israel-Palestine conflict News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Human rights groups and Palestinian leaders have condemned Israel's passage of a death penalty law for Palestinians convicted of deadly attacks, calling it a violation of international law and inherently discriminatory. The Palestinian Ministry of Foreign Affairs called it a 'dangerous escalation', while Hamas said it threatens the lives of Palestinian prisoners. Far-right minister Itamar Ben-Gvir celebrated the law's passage, rejecting international pressure.

articles.title: US says Trump 'interested' in asking Arab countries to pay for war on Iran | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: White House spokesperson Karoline Leavitt suggested President Donald Trump is interested in asking Arab countries to cover the costs of the US war against Iran, estimated to be tens of billions of dollars. She said it is 'an idea that I know that he has'. The article notes the US is seeking additional military funding from Congress and that the war has sent global energy prices soaring.

articles.title: At least 70 killed, 30 wounded in Haiti gang attack, rights group says | Crime News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A human rights group says at least 70 people were killed and 30 injured in a gang attack in Haiti's Artibonite region, a figure significantly higher than official estimates of about 16 dead. The attack, which began Sunday and continued into Monday, involved gang members storming rural communities and setting homes on fire. The rights group estimated 6,000 people were displaced, and police said gang members fled the area before their arrival.

overall_summary: This batch of articles covers a range of international news from the last six hours, including severe weather fatalities in South Asia, ongoing conflict in the Middle East, arrests and activism in the UK and France, and policy statements from the US and G7. The reports detail casualties, geopolitical tensions, and domestic incidents.

articles.title: Heavy rain, floods kill at least 45 people in Afghanistan, Pakistan | Weather News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Heavy rainfall over five days has caused severe flooding and landslides, killing at least 45 people in Afghanistan and Pakistan. Afghan authorities report 28 deaths and over 100 homes destroyed, with unstable weather conditions continuing. In Pakistan's Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province, 17 people were killed and 56 wounded.

articles.title: We dug up medics in Gaza. A year later, international law remains buried | Israel-Palestine conflict | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A former UN official recounts the killing of medics in Gaza a year ago, describing how they were attacked despite clear markings and coordination. The article states Israeli forces denied access and that an investigation led only to disciplinary actions, not criminal charges. It argues a coordination system was twisted to enable attacks on aid workers.

articles.title: UK pro-Palestine activist arrested weeks after being released on bail | Prison News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Pro-Palestine activist Qesser Zuhrah was arrested again, with campaigners believing it was over an Instagram post. She is part of a group alleged to have raided an Elbit Systems factory in 2024. Zuhrah had been released on bail last month after 15 months in remand, during which she participated in a hunger strike.

articles.title: Fire contained after missile attack on oil refinery in Israel’s Haifa | Israel attacks Lebanon News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: An oil refinery in Haifa, Israel, was struck in a missile barrage reportedly from Iran and Lebanon, causing a fire. Israeli authorities said the incident was contained with no casualties or public danger. The attack is the second on the facility since the US-Israeli war on Iran began, amid broader regional strikes.

articles.title: G7 ready to take ‘necessary measures’ to ensure energy market stability | Oil and Gas News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: G7 finance ministers vowed to take necessary measures to stabilize energy markets amid the US-Israeli war on Iran, which has driven oil prices higher. They called on countries to avoid unjustified export restrictions and noted the International Energy Agency's suggestions. The statement emphasized central banks' commitment to price stability.

articles.title: ICE announces death of another Mexican detainee in US immigration custody | Migration News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A Mexican immigrant died in ICE custody in California, raising the death toll of detainees this year. Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum said her country would take steps to protest the death. ICE stated the detainee had pre-existing medical conditions and received care, but rights advocates question medical treatment in facilities.

articles.title: Two more UN peacekeepers killed in southern Lebanon: UNIFIL | Israel attacks Lebanon News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Two UN peacekeepers were killed in southern Lebanon when an explosion destroyed their vehicle, with two others wounded. This follows another incident where a peacekeeper was killed by a projectile at a UN position. The deaths occur amid Israel's expanding ground invasion of Lebanon, which has caused widespread displacement.

articles.title: US airport lines shorten as TSA workers get paid | Aviation News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Airport security wait times in the US are shortening after President Trump ordered payment to TSA workers, ending a period of long lines. However, delays persist at some airports like LaGuardia, and over 500 officers have left the agency recently due to funding instability. The action came amid a partial government shutdown.

articles.title: French police make two more arrests over foiled attack on Bank of America | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: French police arrested two more people over a foiled attack on Bank of America's Paris headquarters, bringing the total in custody to five. Authorities are investigating a suspected link to the Iran war, noting similarities to other attempted attacks in Europe claimed by a pro-Iran group. The initial suspect allegedly placed a homemade explosive device.

articles.title: Rubio denies US actions punitive, blames Cuba for economic failures | Donald Trump News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio denied the US has taken punitive actions against Cuba, blaming the island's economic system and government for its crises. He cited the end of Venezuelan oil subsidies as the key change, not US policy. Rubio dismissed links between US pressure and recent blackouts in Cuba, attributing them to outdated infrastructure.

overall_summary: This batch of articles covers escalating tensions in the US-Israel war on Iran, including diplomatic efforts, military threats, and civilian impacts. It also includes separate stories on international diplomatic expulsions, a doping ban, a human rights report on the World Cup, and a feature on a mother's struggle with her son's cancer in Mexico.

articles.title: Schools, water, industry: What civilian targets have US, Israel, Iran hit? | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article details civilian infrastructure targeted by the US, Israel, and Iran since the war began on February 28. It states US-Israeli strikes have hit thousands of sites, including schools, hospitals, and residential buildings, with Iran reporting over 2,000 deaths. In retaliation, Iran has struck civilian and energy facilities in Israel and Gulf nations hosting US bases.

articles.title: Russia’s FSB orders British diplomat’s expulsion, UK rejects ‘intimidation’ | News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Russia's FSB intelligence agency has ordered a British diplomat to leave the country within two weeks, accusing him of economic espionage. The UK rejected the accusations as 'completely unacceptable' and stated it would not tolerate intimidation of its embassy staff. The incident occurs amid tensions over Russia's war on Ukraine.

articles.title: Trump ‘pretty sure’ of Iran deal, but can Pakistan-led efforts end the war? | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Pakistan, Saudi Arabia, Turkiye, and Egypt have formed a diplomatic team for backchannel talks between the US and Iran. Pakistan's foreign minister confirmed both sides expressed confidence in Pakistan to facilitate direct talks 'in the coming days'. However, the article notes a tension between these efforts and President Trump's simultaneous threats, including about seizing Iranian oil.

articles.title: Why have the US and Israel bombed more than 75 Iranian police facilities? | Armed Groups News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: An Al Jazeera investigation has verified that at least 75 internal security sites in Iran, including police stations and Basij paramilitary checkpoints, were destroyed or damaged by US-Israeli strikes from February 28 to March 10. The investigation suggests the strikes deliberately targeted urban law enforcement infrastructure in densely populated western cities, with over 40% of hits in the capital, Tehran.

articles.title: Spain closes airspace to US planes involved in war on Iran | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Spain has closed its airspace to US planes involved in the war on Iran, confirming its opposition to the conflict. Defence Minister Margarita Robles stated that neither the use of jointly operated military bases nor Spanish airspace is authorized for actions related to the war, which she called 'profoundly illegal and profoundly unjust'. Spain had previously refused the US use of its bases for the war.

articles.title: ‘I’m always watching’: A mother’s vigil in Mexico’s cancer system | Women | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article profiles Luisa Corral, a 24-year-old mother in Mexico whose eight-month-old son, Juan de Jesus, has bilateral retinoblastoma, a rare eye cancer. Doctors have said his right eye must be removed and are trying to save vision in his left. Luisa constantly monitors her son for changes while navigating long trips to Mexico City for treatment and staying at a nonprofit cancer shelter.

articles.title: Kenya’s former New York marathon champion Korir gets five-year doping ban | Athletics News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Kenyan marathon runner Albert Korir, the 2021 New York City Marathon champion, has been banned for five years for doping. The Athletics Integrity Unit stated Korir tested positive for synthetic EPO in out-of-competition tests and received a reduced ban due to an early admission. This sanction is part of a series of doping cases involving Kenyan distance runners.

articles.title: Trump wants to ‘take Iran’s oil’: Can he, and what would that mean? | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: President Donald Trump has stated his preference to 'take the oil' in Iran and suggested US forces could seize Kharg Island, which processes 90% of Iran's oil exports. The article notes Iran holds the world's third-largest crude oil reserves. It draws a comparison to the US operation in Venezuela, where Trump claims control over that country's oil exports has been achieved.

articles.title: Rubio says Strait of Hormuz to reopen ‘one way or another’ | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: In an interview, US Secretary of State Marco Rubio told Al Jazeera the Strait of Hormuz will 'reopen one way or another' after the war. He stated President Trump prefers diplomacy and confirmed there have been messages and some talks with Iran, primarily through intermediaries. Rubio also expressed a US preference for regime change in Iran and called on the country to end its nuclear and missile programmes.

articles.title: ‘Loyalty campaign’: Iraqi armed groups in Iran as US talks of ground war | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Paramilitary Iraqi forces from the Iran-aligned Hashd al-Shaabi (Popular Mobilisation Forces) have entered Iran with what Iranian media describes as a humanitarian aid convoy. Iranian state media called it a 'loyalty campaign' carrying food and medical supplies. The convoy's arrival comes amid speculation about a potential US ground assault and longstanding ties between Tehran and Iraqi armed factions.

articles.title: Is the US ready to invade Iran? What Trump’s moves tell us so far | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article examines the possibility of a US ground invasion of Iran, reporting that the Pentagon is preparing for limited ground operations, including potential raids on Kharg Island. It notes a significant US military buildup in the region since the war began. Iran's parliament speaker warned US troops would face fierce resistance if they attempted an invasion.

articles.title: Trump threatens to ‘blow up’ all desalination plants in Iran | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: President Trump has threatened to destroy Iran's desalination plants, along with its electrical grid and oil infrastructure, if a deal is not reached and the Strait of Hormuz remains closed. Legal experts cited in the article state that such an action would constitute illegal collective punishment under international humanitarian law. Trump has set an April 6 deadline related to these threats.

articles.title: FIFA World Cup will be held amid ‘human rights crisis’ in the US: Amnesty | World Cup 2026 News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Amnesty International warns the upcoming FIFA World Cup in the US, Canada, and Mexico will be held during an 'acute human rights crisis' in the US, posing risks to fans and communities. The report criticizes US immigration crackdowns, protest restrictions, and deaths in custody. It states the US, which will host most matches, is demonstrating a pattern of authoritarian practices under the Trump administration.

overall_summary: The articles cover escalating military conflicts in the Middle East, particularly the US-Israel war on Iran and its regional spillover into Lebanon and the Gulf. They also report on diplomatic tensions, a sports result, and features on the Israel-Palestine conflict and Syrian reconstruction.

articles.title: US-Israel war on Iran: What’s happening on day 31 of attacks | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: On day 31 of the conflict, US-Israeli strikes hit Tehran and other Iranian cities, targeting power infrastructure and causing a temporary blackout. US President Donald Trump stated a desire to 'take the oil in Iran' while also mentioning a potential deal, as Iran accused the US of planning a ground invasion. Regional diplomatic efforts continued, with Pakistan hosting talks, and the conflict has resulted in significant casualties and damage since February 28.

articles.title: Did Israel miscalculate in launching the war on Iran? | US-Israel war on Iran | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: This opinion piece argues Israel launched the war on Iran without a realistic political plan, and a narrative of potential defeat is emerging in Israeli media. It details reported tensions within Israel's leadership over the war's objectives and notes the Israeli public's continued support despite economic disruption and frequent shelter alerts. The article suggests uncomfortable questions are being raised about Israel's assumptions for the war.

articles.title: UN peacekeeper killed in southern Lebanon as Israeli invasion intensifies | Israel attacks Lebanon News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A UNIFIL peacekeeper from Indonesia was killed and another critically injured by a projectile in southern Lebanon amid intensifying fighting between Israel and Hezbollah. UNIFIL stated it does not know the origin of the projectile and has launched an investigation. The report notes Israel has expanded its ground offensive in southern Lebanon, with fighting escalating since the US-Israel war on Iran began.

articles.title: Trump wants to invade Iran to seize oil, calls US objectors ‘stupid people’ | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump stated he wants to 'take the oil in Iran,' specifically mentioning the possible seizure of the key oil export hub Kharg Island, and dismissed US opponents of the plan as 'stupid people.' The report states the Trump administration has deployed US Marines to the region and discussed the seizure plan internally.
