Iran's parliament speaker warned that any such attempt would be met with attacks on the infrastructure of assisting regional countries.

articles.title: France beat Colombia 3-1 to showcase firepower and squad depth | World Cup 2026 News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: France defeated Colombia 3-1 in a friendly match, with goals from Desire Doue (two) and Marcus Thuram. The victory, achieved with a heavily changed lineup and a late substitute appearance for Kylian Mbappe, is described as showcasing the team's attacking firepower and depth ahead of the World Cup squad selection.

articles.title: Iraq risks Arab ties as Iran-aligned groups strike Gulf nations | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Six Arab nations jointly condemned cross-border attacks launched from Iraq by Iran-aligned armed groups against Gulf infrastructure, holding the Iraqi government responsible. Iraq's foreign ministry rejected the use of its territory for such attacks and expressed readiness to address evidence. Analysts warn Baghdad's inability to control these factions is eroding its sovereignty and damaging rebuilt regional ties.

articles.title: Land Day in Gaza: Between memory and the fight for what remains | Israel-Palestine conflict | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: This feature focuses on a Palestinian woman in Gaza, Sawsan al-Jadba, who has lost most of her agricultural land due to Israel's war and a military demarcation line. It describes how Land Day, which commemorates historical land rights, now highlights the struggle for survival and the loss of land during the recent conflict. The article connects the current reality to the historical events of 1976.

articles.title: Palestine aIly Ireland under fire for allowing weapons transfers to Israel | Israel-Palestine conflict News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Activists in Ireland are protesting and facing criminal charges for actions at Shannon Airport, which they say is complicit in Israel's war on Gaza due to its use by the US military. Data indicates numerous US military flights near the airport, including some to or from Israel. The article notes Ireland's historic support for Palestine but highlights criticism over its continued allowance of US military transit.

articles.title: Green and Yellow: Two lines that separate me from my land | Israel-Palestine conflict | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: This personal opinion piece reflects on Land Day by recounting the author's family history of displacement during the Nakba and subsequent Israeli wars on Gaza. It describes how the Green Line of 1949 and current Israeli demarcations separate Palestinians from their land. The author asserts a determination to maintain their right to the land despite ongoing trauma and displacement.

articles.title: Syria’s al-Sharaa visits Germany to talk reconstruction, return of refugees | News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Interim Syrian President Ahmed al-Sharaa visited Germany for talks on reconstruction and the potential return of refugees. German officials expressed support for rebuilding a stable Syria and suggested Syrians who fled to Germany could contribute. The visit comes as Germany's government has stepped up efforts to limit irregular immigration and has resumed some deportations to Syria.

overall_summary: This batch of articles covers a range of global news from sports controversies and achievements to economic policy and geopolitical issues. The stories include a major resignation in African football, a dominant MotoGP victory, an Australian fuel tax cut, a cricket ball-tampering charge, and features on Palestinian Land Day, alongside speculation about a football star's future.

articles.title: CAF general secretary quits amid AFCON final controversy | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The Confederation of African Football's general secretary, Veron Mosengo-Omba, has resigned. His departure comes amid a crisis of confidence in CAF's leadership and fallout over the decision to strip Senegal of the Africa Cup of Nations title. Mosengo-Omba stated he was retiring to pursue personal projects, though sources indicate he may run for a football federation presidency in the Democratic Republic of the Congo.

articles.title: Marco Bezzecchi wins USA MotoGP to extend perfect start to season | Motorsports News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Aprilia rider Marco Bezzecchi won the United States Grand Prix in Austin, leading every lap. This victory marks his fifth consecutive Grand Prix win and his third win of the current season, giving him the lead in the championship standings. Bezzecchi finished ahead of teammate Jorge Martin, with Pedro Acosta completing the podium.

articles.title: Australia to halve fuel tax as global energy crisis deepens | Oil and Gas News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese announced the government will halve the fuel excise tax from April 1 to June 30 due to rising fuel prices linked to the US-Israel war on Iran. The cut is intended to reduce financial stress, saving motorists about 26.3 Australian cents per litre. However, a motorists' association spokesperson argued the tax cut will have little impact as the price surge is driven by global oil prices, not taxes.

articles.title: Fakhar Zaman charged with ball‑tampering by Pakistan board in PSL | Cricket News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Lahore Qalandars batter Fakhar Zaman has been charged with ball-tampering by the Pakistan Cricket Board following an incident in a Pakistan Super League match. Umpires awarded five penalty runs to the opposing team, Karachi Kings, after inspecting the ball. Fakhar Zaman has denied the charge, and a disciplinary hearing is set to conclude within 48 hours, with a potential match ban if found guilty.

articles.title: By the 50th anniversary of Land Day, Palestinians lose most of their land | Features | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article reports on the ongoing loss of Palestinian land due to illegal Israeli settlement expansion and land confiscations, focusing on a case south of Jenin. It describes how a Palestinian landowner was forced to cut down his olive trees after an Israeli decision to confiscate his land for a settlement road. The piece is published as Palestinians commemorate the 50th anniversary of Land Day, which marks protests against land confiscations in 1976.

articles.title: Land Day: What happened in Palestine on March 30, 1976? | Israel-Palestine conflict News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Land Day commemorates the events of March 30, 1976, when Israeli forces killed six unarmed Palestinians during protests against Israel's confiscation of Palestinian land in the Galilee. The article notes that land confiscations and settlement expansion have continued since then, with recent Israeli cabinet measures expanding powers in the West Bank. Palestinians mark the day with protests and tree-planting to reaffirm their connection to the land.

articles.title: Mohamed Salah warned against US move as MLS clubs eye Egyptian footballer | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Egypt's national team director Ibrahim Hassan has cautioned Mohamed Salah that a move to Major League Soccer after leaving Liverpool could cause him to fade from the spotlight. Hassan suggested Salah should stay in Europe or consider the Saudi Pro League instead. Meanwhile, San Diego FC owner Mohamed Mansour, an Egyptian compatriot, stated that Salah would be an asset to any team or league he joins, though he did not confirm active recruitment efforts.

overall_summary: The articles report escalating tensions in the US-Israel conflict with Iran, including Iranian accusations of a planned US ground attack and retaliatory strikes affecting Kuwait. European nations criticize proposed Israeli legislation, and the conflict drives a significant surge in global oil prices.

articles.title: Iran accuses US of plotting ground attack, as Israel steps up bombardment | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Iran's parliament speaker accused the United States of plotting a ground attack while publicly pushing for negotiations, warning that Iranian forces are waiting for US soldiers. This comes as Iran experienced power outages in Tehran and surrounding areas following attacks on electricity facilities, which authorities are working to restore. The Israeli military stated it dropped over 120 munitions on weaponry-related sites in Tehran, and Iran's health ministry reported over 2,000 war-related deaths.

articles.title: European nations criticise Israel’s death penalty plans | Israel-Palestine conflict News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The foreign ministers of France, Germany, Italy, and the United Kingdom expressed deep concern over an Israeli bill to extend the application of the death penalty, warning of its 'de facto discriminatory character' against Palestinians. They stated the bill risks undermining Israel's democratic commitments. The legislation is scheduled for parliamentary readings and faces potential legal challenges, with critics like Amnesty International arguing it would become a discriminatory tool within Israel's system.

articles.title: Iranian attack damages Kuwait power and desalination plant, kills worker | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: An Iranian attack on a power and water desalination plant in Kuwait killed an Indian worker and caused significant material damage, according to Kuwaiti authorities. Kuwait's Defence Ministry had previously reported detecting missiles and drones in its airspace, with some targeting a military camp and injuring servicemen. The attack is part of regional escalations since the start of the US-Israeli war on Iran, which has included Iran effectively blocking the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Oil tops $116 a barrel as Iran accuses US of preparing invasion | Oil and Gas News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Oil prices surged, with Brent crude rising over 3% to top $116 a barrel, reaching its highest point in nearly two weeks amid escalating conflict. The increase follows Iranian warnings about a potential US ground invasion and a deepening of hostilities, including Houthi missile launches at Israel. Iran's effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz has disrupted about one-fifth of global oil and LNG supplies, contributing to what is described as the world's biggest energy crisis in decades.

overall_summary: The articles cover escalating military actions in the Middle East, diplomatic efforts in the Gulf, U.S. domestic political debate over war powers, and separate conflicts in Sudan and Lebanon. Key themes include the expansion of the Israel-Lebanon conflict, Ukraine's security outreach, and ongoing humanitarian crises.

articles.title: Netanyahu orders military to expand invasion of southern Lebanon | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has ordered a further expansion of military operations in southern Lebanon, aiming to push towards the Litani River to drive out Hezbollah. The announcement came as fighting intensified, with Israeli forces reaching a tributary near the Litani, and as funerals were held for three journalists killed in an Israeli air strike. Lebanon's Health Ministry reports at least 1,238 people killed and over 1.2 million displaced since the country was drawn into the broader conflict in early March.

articles.title: Zelenskyy arrives in Jordan to bolster security ties | Russia-Ukraine war News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has arrived in Jordan as part of a tour to strengthen defence ties with Gulf states, following similar agreements with Qatar, Saudi Arabia, and the UAE. The visit comes as Ukraine continues its war with Russia, recently conducting drone strikes on Russian infrastructure like the Ust-Luga port. Ukrainian anti-drone experts have reportedly been deployed to several Gulf countries, and a Ukrainian team is also said to be in Jordan.

articles.title: Republican Mace says Congress must approve any US troop deployment to Iran | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: U.S. Representative Nancy Mace, a Republican, stated that Congress should authorize any decision to deploy American troops to Iran for ground operations, highlighting divisions within the party. Her comments follow a report that the Pentagon is preparing for limited ground operations in Iran, such as raids on Kharg Island. The White House press secretary stated that the Pentagon prepares various options for the president but that no decision has been made, while President Trump has kept all options on the table.

articles.title: RSF attack on Sudan’s South Kordofan kills at least 14, including children | Sudan war News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: An attack by the Rapid Support Forces (RSF) and their allies on the city of Dilling in Sudan's South Kordofan province has killed at least 14 people, including five children and two women, according to a medical group. The Sudan Doctors Network reported hours of shelling on residential areas, wounding at least 23 others, and warned of a potential catastrophic scenario similar to the RSF's prior attack on el-Fasher. The war between the Sudanese army and the RSF has created a major humanitarian crisis, displacing over 12 million people.

articles.title: Lebanon’s Catholics observe Palm Sunday under looming threat of war | Israel attacks Lebanon News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Lebanese Christians observed Palm Sunday amid the escalating conflict between Israel and Hezbollah, with worshippers in Beirut and Tyre gathering for prayers despite the threat of war. Attendees stressed that all communities in Lebanon are suffering the consequences of the conflict, with one noting, 'no one is safe from this.' The observances occurred as Israeli troops advance in a ground invasion and the health ministry reports over 1,200 killed since March 2.

overall_summary: The articles cover a range of international developments from the last 6 hours. Major themes include regional diplomatic efforts to de-escalate the US-Israel war on Iran, criticism of new Olympic gender verification policies, and various impacts of the ongoing conflict. Other stories involve sports controversies, ICC governance, and restrictions on religious worship.

articles.title: Olympic gender test ‘a disrespect for women’, South Africa’s Semenya says | Olympics News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: South African sprinter Caster Semenya criticizes the International Olympic Committee's reinstatement of gender verification tests for the 2028 Los Angeles Games, calling it 'a disrespect for women'. She expressed disappointment that the measure was taken under new IOC President Kirsty Coventry. The IOC stated only 'biological females' will be allowed in women's events, preventing transgender women from competing.

articles.title: Pakistan hosts four-nation bid to encourage US, Iran towards diplomacy | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Foreign ministers of Pakistan, Egypt, Saudi Arabia, and Turkiye began two-day consultations in Islamabad to push for de-escalation in the US-Israel war on Iran. The meeting aims to align regional positions and prepare the ground for possible direct US-Iran talks. Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian, in a call with Pakistan's Prime Minister, stressed the need for confidence-building measures before Tehran considers direct dialogue.

articles.title: The US-Israeli war on humanity | US-Israel war on Iran | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: An opinion piece argues the US-Israeli war represents a 'war on humanity' and a coordinated assault on international systems, with Palestine as the epicenter. It states that despite an October ceasefire in Gaza, deadly violence and severe aid restrictions continue. The article claims this moment is the apex of a plan for 'Greater Israel' and is enabled by decades of diplomatic and military cover from the US and European states.

articles.title: ICC states should not ignore judicial experts’ conclusions in Khan’s case | ICC | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: An opinion piece addresses the disciplinary case against ICC prosecutor Karim Khan regarding alleged sexual misconduct. It reports that judicial experts unanimously concluded no misconduct by Khan could be established under the legal framework. The article warns that if the Assembly of States Parties bureau ignores this expert advice and substitutes its own conclusions, it would damage the credibility of both the states and the ICC's governance framework.

articles.title: Iran ‘hits’ US AWACS, air tankers: What else has it targeted in past month? | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Iran reportedly damaged or destroyed US assets, including KC-135 refueling tankers and an E-3 Sentry AWACS aircraft, in an attack on the Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia. Iranian military spokesperson Ebrahim Zolfaghari claimed the attack destroyed one refueling aircraft and damaged three others. The article notes Iranian attacks over the past month have targeted radar systems, missile defense systems, and drones at US bases in several Gulf countries.

articles.title: Israeli footballer’s role in assault on southern Lebanon sparks outrage | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Israeli Premier League player Menashe Zalka was seen in a verified video opening fire with Israeli soldiers during an assault in southern Lebanon. This has prompted calls from football officials and fans for FIFA to take action against him and the Israeli Football Association. The Palestinian Football Association's vice president termed Zalka's involvement 'unacceptable', renewing criticism of FIFA's failure to suspend Israel despite sanctioning Russia in 2022.

articles.title: ‘Nowhere is really safe’: Iranian dissidents grapple with US war in Iran | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Iranian dissidents in the US express fear for their safety and concerns about growing anti-immigrant sentiment since the US joined the war on Iran. Activist Roozbeh Farahanipour describes facing threats even in exile. Scholar Negar Razavi describes an atmosphere of 'dual fear', noting dissidents feel unsafe both in the US and Iran, exacerbated by the Trump administration's deportation of Iranians, including refugees and asylum seekers.

articles.title: Pakistan maintains ‘delicate balancing act’ as it hosts Iran talks | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Foreign ministers from Turkiye, Saudi Arabia, and Egypt are meeting in Islamabad with Pakistani officials for talks aimed at de-escalating the US-Israel war on Iran. As an initial confidence-building measure, Iran agreed to allow 20 Pakistani-flagged ships to pass through the Strait of Hormuz. The diplomatic push comes as US President Donald Trump extended a deadline for Iran to reopen the strait, which Tehran has choked off, causing a global energy crisis.

articles.title: African football chief will ‘respect’ CAS decision on AFCON final row | Africa Cup of Nations News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: CAF President Patrice Motsepe says he will respect the Court of Arbitration for Sport's decision on Senegal's appeal against being stripped of their Africa Cup of Nations title. The appeal follows a CAF appeals body overturning Senegal's 1-0 win over Morocco in the final and awarding Morocco a 3-0 victory. Motsepe also announced the retirement of general secretary Veron Mosengo-Omba and said CAF would implement changes to its statutes and regulations to prevent similar incidents.

articles.title: Houthis open new front in Iran war: Will Yemeni group block Bab al-Mandeb? | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Yemen's Houthis have entered the Iran war by launching strikes on Israel, raising concerns they could blockade the Bab al-Mandeb strait. A Houthi military spokesperson announced a second military operation against Israel using cruise missiles and drones. Analysts note that while the Houthis are Iranian allies, their decision-making is largely independent, and their entry into the conflict could open a new regional front, though some describe their current participation as 'token'.

articles.title: Israeli police block Catholic cardinal from Holy Sepulchre on Palm Sunday | Religion News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Israeli police prevented Cardinal Pierbattista Pizzaballa, the Latin patriarch of Jerusalem, from entering the Church of the Holy Sepulchre to mark Palm Sunday Mass. Israeli police said all holy sites in Jerusalem were closed due to safety concerns amid the US-Israel war on Iran. The Catholic Church condemned the action as a 'grave precedent', and Italian and French leaders criticized the decision as an offence to religious freedom.

overall_summary: This batch of articles covers international sports results, escalating military conflict in the Middle East, and diplomatic efforts to de-escalate tensions. The main themes include football friendlies ahead of the 2026 World Cup, a new F1 championship leader, Iranian attacks on Gulf facilities, and high-stakes diplomatic talks in Pakistan regarding the US-Israel war on Iran.

articles.title: Belgium thrash USA 5-2; Mexico vs Portugal ends 0-0 in football friendlies | World Cup 2026 News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Belgium defeated the United States 5-2 in a football friendly, a result described as a wake-up call for the US team 75 days before co-hosting the 2026 World Cup. In another friendly, Mexico and Portugal played to a 0-0 draw at the reopened Azteca Stadium, where a man died after falling from the stands before the match. Authorities stated the man was intoxicated and attempted to jump between seating levels.

articles.title: Kimi Antonelli wins Japanese GP to become youngest F1 title leader | Motorsports News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Mercedes driver Kimi Antonelli, aged 19, won the Japanese Grand Prix to become the youngest driver to lead the Formula One world championship standings. He took advantage of a mid-race safety car to move into the lead after a poor start from pole position. The race saw Haas driver Ollie Bearman crash, resulting in a knee contusion, though initial X-rays showed no fractures.

articles.title: Iran’s IRGC claims attacks on UAE, Bahrain aluminium facilities | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps says it carried out missile and drone attacks on aluminium facilities in Bahrain and the United Arab Emirates, stating the targeted sites were linked to the US military. Aluminium Bahrain reported two employees injured, while Emirates Global Aluminium in Abu Dhabi reported significant damage and six injuries. The IRGC stated the strikes were retaliation for a US-Israeli attack on Iranian industrial infrastructure launched from bases in Gulf states.

articles.title: Pakistan hosts top Saudi, Turkish, Egyptian diplomats over war in Iran | News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Top diplomats from Saudi Arabia, Egypt, and Turkiye have gathered in Islamabad for two-day talks with Pakistan aimed at de-escalating the US-Israel war on Iran. Pakistani Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar announced Iran has allowed more Pakistani-flagged ships to pass through the Strait of Hormuz. The report states Pakistan has been acting as a key interlocutor, passing messages between the US and Iran as part of mediation efforts.

articles.title: Living in the dark: Gaza’s struggle for electricity | Israel-Palestine conflict News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article profiles the daily struggle for electricity in Gaza, focusing on Abdel Karim Salman, a displaced civil engineer in Deir el-Balah. He uses his and his wife's phone torches for light at night, walking daily to pay for charging at private points, which costs his family a significant portion of their limited resources. With municipality electricity absent for two years, alternatives like solar lamps and systems have become prohibitively expensive for most residents.

articles.title: US-Israel war on Iran: What’s happening on day 30 of attacks? | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: On the 30th day of the US-Israel war on Iran, diplomatic talks involving foreign ministers from Pakistan, Turkiye, Egypt, and Saudi Arabia are underway in Islamabad. The conflict has escalated, with Yemen's Houthis joining by firing missiles at Israel. The article lists recent attacks, including explosions in Tehran that caused casualties, strikes on civilian infrastructure in Iran, and Iranian retaliatory strikes targeting sites in Israel.

articles.title: Pentagon readies for weeks of US ground operations in Iran: Report | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A Washington Post report, citing US officials, states the Pentagon is preparing for weeks of limited ground operations in Iran, potentially including raids on Kharg Island and coastal sites near the Strait of Hormuz. The plans could involve special operations and conventional infantry troops but fall short of a full invasion. The White House press secretary stated such preparations are the Pentagon's job to provide options, not an indication of a presidential decision.

overall_summary: The articles cover major sports results from the last six hours. They report on Aryna Sabalenka's victory in the Miami Open tennis final and Joe Pyfer's knockout win over Israel Adesanya in a UFC main event.

articles.title: Sabalenka defeats Gauff to win second straight Miami Open title | Tennis News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Aryna Sabalenka defeated Coco Gauff 6-2, 4-6, 6-3 to win the Miami Open final, securing her second consecutive title at the event. This victory completes the 'Sunshine Double', as Sabalenka also won the Indian Wells tournament, making her only the fifth woman to achieve this back-to-back feat. Sabalenka is also the first player to win back-to-back Miami titles since Ash Barty.

articles.title: Israel Adesanya knocked out by Joe Pyfer at UFC Fight Night in Seattle | Mixed Martial Arts News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Joe Pyfer defeated former UFC middleweight champion Israel Adesanya by technical knockout in the second round of their main event fight. This marks Adesanya's fourth consecutive loss, though he stated he has no plans to retire. The article also notes other results from the event, including a win by Alexa Grasso and a retirement victory for Michael Chiesa.

overall_summary: The articles cover developments on the 29th day of the US-Israel war on Iran, focusing on regional spillover and political reactions. Key themes include a new shipping deal through the Strait of Hormuz, attacks expanding into Iraq and Yemen, and domestic US political commentary related to the conflict.

articles.title: Pakistan secures Iran deal to send 20 ships through Strait of Hormuz | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Iran has agreed to allow 20 Pakistani-flagged vessels to transit the Strait of Hormuz, which has been largely shut since the war began. Pakistan's foreign minister described the deal as a meaningful step and a 'harbinger of peace,' addressing his announcement to senior US and Iranian officials. The strait's closure has stranded thousands of vessels and driven up oil prices, with Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps controlling passage through a checkpoint system.

articles.title: Reza Pahlavi pledges to ‘make Iran great again’ at 2026 CPAC conference | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Reza Pahlavi, the son of Iran's former shah, spoke at the CPAC conference in Texas, urging the US not to cut a deal with Iran and to seek regime change. He received a standing ovation after stating his intention to 'make Iran great again.' The article notes that while Pahlavi has a base of support in the diaspora, President Trump has previously downplayed his potential role in Iran's future.

articles.title: Air strikes in Iraq kill three PMF fighters, two police | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Air strikes targeting Iraq's Popular Mobilisation Forces (PMF) killed three fighters and two Iraqi police officers, with the PMF blaming the US and Israel. The report states Iraq is becoming an 'expanding battleground' in the war, with Baghdad caught between its ties to both the US and Iran. Separate drone attacks were also reported near Erbil airport and on the home of the Kurdish region's president.

articles.title: Vice President JD Vance tops CPAC’s straw poll to be US president in 2028 | Elections News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: US Vice President JD Vance won CPAC's 2028 presidential straw poll for the second year, receiving 53% of the vote. Secretary of State Marco Rubio placed second with 35%, a significant rise from his previous showing. The article notes the poll is an informal bellwether and comes as President Trump's approval ratings have slumped, partly due to the war in Iran.

articles.title: As war on Iran enters second month, Yemen’s Houthis open new front | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Yemen's Houthis have attacked Israel for the first time since the war began, opening a new front with missile and drone strikes. The Iran-aligned group pledged continued support for regional 'resistance fronts,' raising fears it may also disrupt Red Sea shipping. The report states the conflict continues with US-Israeli bombardments in Iran, including strikes on a university and a residential area.

overall_summary: The articles cover a range of international news from the last 6 hours, focusing on the ongoing US-Israeli war on Iran and its regional fallout, domestic political responses, and unrelated events in sports and European protests. Key themes include legislative inaction in the US, attacks on journalists and medics in Lebanon, and diplomatic moves by Ukraine.

articles.title: One month in, disapproval high but US lawmakers take no action on Iran war | Donald Trump News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: One month into the US-Israeli war on Iran, US lawmakers have taken little action to curtail the conflict despite high public disapproval. A War Powers resolution failed in the Senate along party lines, and House Democratic leadership has reportedly backed away from holding a vote. Polls show about 61% of Americans disapprove of the war, and the Trump administration has not articulated a clear endgame.

articles.title: Three journalists killed in Israeli strike on marked press car in Lebanon | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: An Israeli strike on a clearly marked press vehicle in southern Lebanon killed three journalists from Al Mayadeen and Al-Manar. The Israeli military acknowledged the strike, claiming one of the journalists was embedded with Hezbollah, a characterization his employer rejected. The same day, the World Health Organization reported nine paramedics were killed in separate attacks in southern Lebanon.

articles.title: Senegal parade AFCON trophy, despite title being awarded to Morocco | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Senegal paraded the Africa Cup of Nations trophy ahead of a friendly in Paris, despite having been stripped of the title by the Confederation of African Football (CAF). CAF's appeals panel overturned Senegal's January final win and awarded the title to Morocco. Senegal plans to appeal the decision to CAF and the Court of Arbitration for Sport.

articles.title: Ukraine’s Zelenskyy signs air defence deals with UAE, Qatar on Gulf tour | News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy signed defence agreements with Qatar and the UAE during a Gulf tour, focusing on countering missile and drone threats. Ukraine is leveraging its battlefield expertise in downing drones to offer cheaper defensive solutions to Gulf nations. The agreements involve collaboration in technology, joint investments, and expertise exchange.

articles.title: France opens probe into suspected attack on Bank of America in Paris | Banks News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: French authorities have opened a terrorism investigation after police arrested a suspect allegedly trying to ignite an explosive device outside Bank of America's Paris headquarters. A second individual fled the scene. The Interior Minister stated vigilance remains high in the current international context, with security increased around US and Jewish community sites.

articles.title: As war rages, Iranian politicians push for exit from nuclear weapons treaty | Nuclear Energy News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Iranian politicians are pushing for the country to withdraw from the Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty (NPT) as US-Israeli attacks target Iranian nuclear sites and infrastructure. Lawmakers argue the treaty has provided no benefit and have drafted legislation for exit, which would also revoke laws linked to the 2015 nuclear deal. Iranian officials continue to accuse the IAEA of being complicit in attacks.

articles.title: ‘No Kings’ protests erupt across the US, with a Minnesota focus | Protests News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Demonstrators across the US held 'No Kings' protests, the third such round since President Trump's second term began and the first since the war on Iran started. The main event was in Minnesota, focusing on outrage over a federal immigration operation that resulted in two civilian deaths. Organizers said thousands of events were planned, with many occurring outside major city centers.

articles.title: Thousands march through London in stand against the far right | Protests News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Tens of thousands of people marched in London in what organizers called the largest ever demonstration against the far right in British history. The Together Alliance event brought together trade unions and anti-racism groups, with organizers claiming half a million participants, though police estimated a lower figure. Protesters said they were resisting the rise of hate and division in politics.

articles.title: Palestine Action supporters arrested as London’s Met Police reverse policy | Israel-Palestine conflict News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: London's Metropolitan Police arrested 18 supporters of Palestine Action for holding signs, days after reversing a policy and resuming arrests under terrorism legislation. The police had paused arrests following a High Court ruling that banning the group was unlawful, but resumed them pending a government appeal. Critics say the U-turn defies the court ruling.

overall_summary: The articles cover a range of international news from the last six hours, with a focus on escalating conflict in the Middle East and sports controversies. Key themes include new attacks in the Russia-Ukraine and US-Iran wars, diplomatic tensions, and several major sports stories involving disputes, tributes, and competitions.

articles.title: Russian strikes on Ukraine kill 4 as deal to end war dims | Russia-Ukraine war News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Russian strikes on Odesa and Kryvyi Rih killed at least four people and wounded more than a dozen, damaging residential areas, a maternity hospital, and an industrial site. The attacks come as diplomatic efforts to reach a ceasefire remain entangled, with no talks currently underway between Russia and Ukraine. US Secretary of State Marco Rubio accused Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy of lying about US demands regarding the Donbas region and voiced openness to diverting weapons to support the US-Israel attack on Iran.

articles.title: How the US and Israel are making the Islamic republic stronger | US-Israel war on Iran | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: This opinion piece argues that the US-Israel war against Iran is feeding into a theological narrative of martyrdom and sacrifice that helps maintain the Islamic Republic's internal cohesion. It suggests external violence may not weaken the regime as expected but could instead reactivate the symbolic and moral grammar through which it has sustained itself. The article states that the regime's ideological core is shaped by a Shia political theology that sanctifies sacrifice and resistance against imperialism.

articles.title: Diop debut for Morocco adds latest twist in Senegal post-AFCON dispute | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Fulham defender Issa Diop made his debut for Morocco in a friendly against Ecuador, shortly after receiving FIFA clearance to switch nationality. Diop, whose father is Senegalese and mother is Moroccan, had previously rejected overtures from both countries while hoping to play for France. His debut adds a new layer to the ongoing dispute after Senegal was stripped of the Africa Cup of Nations title and Morocco was declared winner, a decision Senegal is contesting at the Court of Arbitration for Sport.

articles.title: Iran footballers pay tribute to victims of school strike at start of war | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The Iran men's national football team wore black armbands and posed with school rucksacks before a friendly against Nigeria to remember victims of a strike on a primary school in Minab. The air strike on February 28 killed at least 170 people, including students and teachers, on the day the US and Israel launched the war with attacks across Iran. Iran has qualified for the World Cup but has asked for its matches to be moved from the US to Mexico over safety concerns after a warning from US President Donald Trump.

articles.title: At least 15 US troops wounded in Iran strike on Saudi airbase: Reports | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: At least 15 American soldiers were wounded, with five in serious condition, after Iran launched a missile and drone attack on the Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia, according to news reports. The attack reportedly destroyed one refuelling aircraft and damaged three others. This is part of Iran's retaliatory attacks on Gulf nations it accuses of serving as launchpads for US strikes, as the conflict enters its second month.

articles.title: Pakistan’s Naseem Shah under fire after criticism of politician’s PSL visit | Cricket News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The Pakistan Cricket Board has issued a show-cause notice to fast bowler Naseem Shah for criticising the presence of Punjab Chief Minister Maryam Nawaz at the opening game of the Pakistan Super League. Shah had posted on social media questioning why she was 'treated like the queen' at the stadium, which is hosting games behind closed doors due to government travel restrictions. Shah later deleted the post and claimed his account was hacked, but the PCB is investigating a potential violation of his central contract and media policy.

articles.title: Verstappen calls his Red Bull ‘undriveable’ after more F1 woes at Japan GP | Motorsports News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Max Verstappen qualified 11th for the Japanese Grand Prix, calling his Red Bull car 'undriveable' due to handling problems with oversteer and unpredictability. Meanwhile, Mercedes secured a 1-2 in qualifying with Kimi Antonelli taking pole position ahead of teammate George Russell. Verstappen, a four-time world champion, has had a difficult start to the season under new Formula One regulations and crashed out in qualifying at the previous race in Australia.

articles.title: Iran warns neighbours not to let ‘enemies run the war’ from their land | News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian warned neighbouring nations not to let Tehran's 'enemies run the war' from their territory, reiterating calls for countries hosting US military bases to distance themselves. The warning comes amid reports of attacks in the region, including drone strikes on Kuwait's international airport that damaged its radar system and strikes in Abu Dhabi that injured six people. Iran's military also claimed it struck a Ukrainian anti-drone system depot in Dubai, alleging it supported US forces, a claim Ukraine denied.

articles.title: Defending champion Aryna Sabalenka and Coco Gauff meet in Miami Open final | Tennis News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Top-seeded Aryna Sabalenka and Coco Gauff will face each other in the Miami Open final, continuing their growing rivalry with their head-to-head record tied at 6-6. Sabalenka is attempting to win the 'Sunshine Double' after winning Indian Wells earlier this month, while Gauff is making her first Miami Open final appearance in her home state of Florida. Both players acknowledged the physical challenge of their matches and anticipated a great battle in the final.

articles.title: At least 22 migrants die off Greece after 6 days at sea | Migration News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: At least 22 migrants died after six days at sea in a rubber boat that left Libya for Greece, with survivors stating the bodies were thrown overboard on a smuggler's orders. Two survivors were hospitalized in Crete, and Greek authorities arrested two South Sudanese men suspected of being the smugglers. The incident highlights the ongoing risks of Mediterranean crossings, with thousands attempting the journey from Libya to Europe each year despite tighter restrictions.

articles.title: Morocco claims AFCON case closed, despite Senegal appeals to CAF and CAS | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Morocco believes the case of the Africa Cup of Nations title is closed despite Senegal's legal appeals, after the Confederation of African Football awarded Morocco a 3-0 victory due to Senegal players leaving the pitch in protest during the final. Morocco played a friendly draw against Ecuador, with players and fans focused on the future and the upcoming World Cup rather than the controversy. Senegal continues to contest the decision and displayed the AFCON trophy before a recent friendly match.

overall_summary: This batch of articles covers a diverse range of international news from the last 6 hours. The stories include a personal feature on a family's struggle with anorexia, several sports updates on football, basketball, and golf, and significant geopolitical developments concerning the US-Israel war on Iran and its regional and global repercussions. Additional reports cover political arrests in Nepal and economic protests in the Philippines.

articles.title: ‘She left her body’: A mother’s fight against her daughter’s anorexia | Women | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article details the story of Dalila Brancaccio and her family in Italy after she was diagnosed with anorexia nervosa in 2018. Her mother, Rita, describes the family's fear and confusion as Dalila's health deteriorated to a critical weight of 31kg. The family's life became centered around her strict treatment plan, with Rita taking daily measures to care for her daughter's physical pain and extreme sensitivity to cold.

articles.title: White is hero and villain on England return, Germany’s Wirtz destroys Swiss | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: In pre-World Cup friendlies, England drew 1-1 with Uruguay after Ben White scored following his controversial recall but then conceded a stoppage-time penalty. Meanwhile, Germany defeated Switzerland 4-3, with Florian Wirtz playing a starring role by scoring two goals and providing two assists to help his team come from behind twice.

articles.title: Doncic faces one-game ban after technical foul in Lakers win against Nets | Basketball News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Luka Doncic scored 41 points in the Los Angeles Lakers' win over the Brooklyn Nets but received his 16th technical foul of the season, triggering an automatic one-game suspension. The technical occurred after an altercation with Nets player Ziaire Williams. The article also notes LeBron James and his son Bronny playing together, with LeBron recording the first father-son assist in NBA history.

articles.title: Yemen’s Houthis launch missile attack on Israel as war with Iran intensifies | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Yemen's Houthi rebels announced they fired ballistic missiles at what they described as sensitive Israeli military sites in southern Israel, marking their first such attack since the US-Israeli war on Iran began. The Israeli military said it intercepted one missile. A Houthi official stated that closing the Bab al-Mandeb strait to Israel-linked shipping is among their options.

articles.title: US-Israel war on Iran: What’s happening on day 29 of attacks? | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article reports on escalating tensions on the 29th day of the US-Israel war on Iran. It states that Iran warned of a 'heavy price' after Israeli attacks on nuclear and industrial sites, while US President Donald Trump criticized NATO's response. Other developments include a US envoy expecting talks with Iran, Saudi Arabia intercepting a missile, and analysts suggesting Russia may be providing Iran with satellite intelligence.

articles.title: Philippine transport strikers say Marcos Jr failing to control oil prices | Energy News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Transport workers in the Philippines are striking, blaming soaring fuel costs on the government's inaction and the economic fallout from the US-Israel war on Iran. Protesters marched to the presidential palace demanding price controls and the scrapping of fuel taxes. President Ferdinand Marcos Jr recently declared a state of national energy emergency, which allows for faster fuel procurement and action against profiteering.

articles.title: Tiger Woods released on bail hours after arrest on suspicion of DUI | Golf News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Golfer Tiger Woods was released on bail after being arrested on suspicion of driving under the influence following a rollover crash in Florida. Authorities say he showed signs of impairment, possibly from medication, but a breath test showed no alcohol. This is Woods's second DUI arrest not involving alcohol; he was previously arrested in 2017 after a mix of painkillers.

articles.title: Nepal’s ex-PM Oli detained over alleged role in deadly protest crackdown | Politics News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Nepal's former Prime Minister KP Sharma Oli and ex-Home Minister Ramesh Lekhak have been arrested over their alleged involvement in a deadly crackdown on protesters in September 2025. The new Home Minister stated the arrest marks the 'beginning of justice'. A government-backed commission had previously recommended prosecution, though Oli has denied ordering security forces to open fire.

overall_summary: This batch of articles from Al Jazeera covers the first month of a US-Israeli war on Iran and its regional consequences. The reports detail the unfolding military conflict, including significant strikes and casualties, and examine the severe humanitarian impact on Lebanon, where Israeli attacks have caused mass displacement.

articles.title: How the US-Israel war on Iran unfolded in its first four weeks | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article recounts major developments in the first month of a US-Israeli war on Iran, which began with large-scale strikes on February 28. The initial attack killed Iran's Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and other top officials, leading to Iranian missile and drone retaliation, the blocking of the Strait of Hormuz, and regional escalation including Hezbollah's entry into the conflict. The conflict has resulted in high casualties, soaring energy prices, and intensified violence across the Middle East.

articles.title: Israel’s unending attacks in Lebanon push country’s population to the brink | Israel attacks Lebanon News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article reports on the severe humanitarian impact of Israeli attacks on Lebanon, now in its fourth week as part of the broader US-Israeli war on Iran. Israeli forced evacuation orders have displaced about a quarter of Lebanon's population, creating a crisis for at least 1.2 million people. The attacks intensified after Hezbollah retaliated for the assassination of Iran's Supreme Leader, with Israel stating its intent to occupy southern Lebanon, worsening conditions for vulnerable groups including migrants and refugees.

overall_summary: The articles cover escalating military conflict in the Middle East, a search operation in the Caribbean, economic repercussions of the war, and sports news. The dominant theme is the intensifying US-Israel war on Iran, now in its fifth week, with reports on strikes, regional warnings, and domestic security measures.

articles.title: Israel launches strikes on nuclear sites as Iran warns of retaliation | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Israel confirmed strikes on Iranian nuclear and industrial sites, including a uranium processing facility in Yazd, the Khondab Heavy Water Complex, and major steel plants. Iran's Foreign Minister warned of retaliation for the attacks, which Iranian officials said have killed over 1,900 people and damaged cultural sites since the war began on February 28. An IRGC commander warned the conflict was entering new territory, urging employees of US and Israeli-linked companies in the region to vacate workplaces.

articles.title: Mexico’s navy searches for two missing boats involved in Cuba aid convoy | Humanitarian Crises News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Mexico's Navy and the US Coast Guard are searching for two missing sailboats carrying nine people that were part of a humanitarian convoy from Mexico to Cuba. The convoy, organized by the Nuestra America Convoy, is a response to worsening conditions in Cuba after the US cut off foreign oil supplies. Cuban President Miguel Diaz-Canel expressed concern and said Cuba is involved in search efforts, while the US Coast Guard retracted an earlier statement that the boats had been found.

articles.title: Guns in the streets as US, Israel intensify month-long attacks across Iran | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Heavily armed state forces, including IRGC paramilitaries, maintain checkpoints and patrols on Iranian streets as the war continues. A senior IRGC official in Tehran stated on state television that children over 12 years old can now join armed patrols and checkpoints. The article describes state-backed gatherings in support of the government occurring alongside US and Israeli calls for Iranians to stay home.

articles.title: What is the TACO trade and are investors profiting amid war on Iran? | Business and Economy News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article discusses market volatility and a trading strategy dubbed 'TACO' (Trump Always Chickens Out), where investors bet on US President Donald Trump backing down from threats. It notes oil markets rallied after Trump extended deadlines for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. However, an economist suggests markets have been less responsive to these policy reversals in the Iran conflict compared to past tariff disputes due to more complex players and objectives involved.

articles.title: Oyarzabal double seals Spain win against Serbia in World Cup warm-up | World Cup 2026 News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Spain defeated Serbia 3-0 in a friendly match, with striker Mikel Oyarzabal scoring two first-half goals and substitute Victor Munoz scoring on his senior international debut. The match served as a warm-up for the 2026 World Cup, where Spain is in Group H. Spain also announced it will play Peru in its final World Cup warm-up game.

articles.title: Tiger Woods charged with DUI after rollover crash in Florida | Golf News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Golfer Tiger Woods was arrested on a DUI charge following a rollover crash in Florida where he struck a truck trailer and his vehicle rolled over. Authorities said he showed signs of impairment, refused a urine test, and was charged with driving under the influence with property damage and refusal to submit to a test. This is at least the third time Woods has been involved in a car crash, with a prior DUI arrest in 2017.

articles.title: Houthis warn ‘fingers on the trigger’ as US-Israel war on Iran continues | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Yemen's Houthi movement warned it is prepared for direct military intervention if other countries join the US and Israel in the war against Iran, or if the Red Sea is used for hostile operations against Iran. The group's military spokesperson said their 'fingers are on the trigger' and warned against any tightening of the blockade on Yemen. The warning raises the prospect of a broader regional war, given the Houthis' ability to disrupt shipping lanes.

overall_summary: This batch of articles covers a range of international political and security developments. Stories include Russia designating a documentary filmmaker as a foreign agent, a cyberattack on the FBI director, US diplomatic statements on Iran and settler violence, Canadian immigration law changes, a foiled assassination plot against a Palestinian activist in the US, and a US legislative standoff over airport worker pay.

articles.title: Russia declares protagonist of Oscar-winning documentary a ‘foreign agent’ | Russia-Ukraine war News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Russia has declared Pavel Talankin, the teacher and main protagonist of the Oscar-winning documentary 'Mr Nobody Against Putin,' a foreign agent. The justice ministry stated he disseminated inaccurate information about Russia's leadership and spoke against the war in Ukraine. A Russian court also banned the documentary from several streaming platforms, saying it promoted negative attitudes about the government and the war.

articles.title: FBI director Kash Patel’s emails, photos hacked by Iran-linked group | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: An Iran-linked hacking group called the Handala Hack Team claims to have successfully accessed the personal emails of FBI Director Kash Patel, sharing photographs and documents online. The breach, confirmed by Reuters and CNN, appears to have released documents over a decade old, including travel correspondence and personal photos. The group, described as pro-Palestinian hacking vigilantes linked to Iranian cyberintelligence, also claimed credit for a recent cyberattack on medical device company Stryker as retaliation for a US-Israeli strike in Iran.

articles.title: US diplomat Marco Rubio denounces settler violence, tolls in Hormuz strait | Donald Trump News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio, departing from a G7 meeting, denounced Iran's reported plans to implement a tolling system in the Strait of Hormuz, calling it illegal and dangerous. He reiterated President Donald Trump's call for other countries to participate in securing the vital waterway for global oil supply. Rubio stated the US is prepared to be part of a plan to confront this, urging G7 members and Asian countries to contribute.

articles.title: Canada’s Bill C-12 an ‘attack on refugee, migrant rights’: Advocates | Migration News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: More than two dozen human rights organizations in Canada have condemned the newly passed Bill C-12, calling it a significant attack on refugee and migrant rights. They warn the law will put thousands at risk by limiting access to refugee protection, enabling mass cancellation of immigration documents, and facilitating broad sharing of personal information. The government defends the legislation as an effort to reduce pressure on a strained immigration system and bolster border security.

articles.title: US Palestinian activist says FBI foiled assassination plot against her | Crime News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: US law enforcement agencies announced they foiled a plot against Palestinian activist Nerdeen Kiswani, arresting a suspect for allegedly planning to throw Molotov cocktails at her New York home. Kiswani said the FBI Joint Terrorism Task Force informed her of the threat, which was thwarted by an undercover operation. The suspect, identified as Andrew Heifler, was charged with making and possessing destructive devices.

articles.title: House Republicans shoot down bill that would pay federal airport workers | Donald Trump News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Republican leaders in the US House of Representatives rejected a Senate-passed bill that would have resumed funding for Department of Homeland Security agencies like the TSA, whose workers have gone unpaid during a partial government shutdown. House Speaker Mike Johnson called the bill a 'joke' because it withheld funding from border patrol and ICE, suggesting the House would advance its own bill. Separately, President Donald Trump signed a memo directing DHS to find a way to pay TSA employees, blaming Democrats for the impasse.

overall_summary: This batch of articles covers a range of global events from the last 6 hours, with a strong focus on the ongoing US-Israel war on Iran and its regional consequences. Other stories include political developments in Nepal and Austria, sports policy debates, and humanitarian crises in Lebanon and El Salvador.

articles.title: What is LNG and what is it used for? | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article explains what Liquefied Natural Gas (LNG) is and its global uses, from power generation to industrial applications. It states that the US-Israeli war on Iran has severely disrupted LNG supplies moving through the Strait of Hormuz, causing significant global energy market disruptions. The text details the technical process of creating, transporting, and regasifying LNG.

articles.title: Nepal’s youngest premier sworn in after releasing new rap song about unity | News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Balendra Shah, a 35-year-old rapper-turned-politician, has been sworn in as Nepal's youngest prime minister. His party, the Rastriya Swatantra Party, won a landslide victory in elections following Gen-Z-led protests. A day before being sworn in, Shah released a rap song about unity which has garnered millions of views online.

articles.title: Yemenis fear economic consequences of being dragged into US-Iran conflict | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article reports that Yemenis fear the economic fallout if the Iran-allied Houthi rebels get involved in the US-Israel war on Iran. Analysts warn that Houthi involvement, such as blocking the Bab al-Mandeb Strait, could reignite Yemen's internal conflict and have devastating humanitarian consequences. The Houthis have so far not participated in the current conflict despite their capabilities.

articles.title: ‘We will no longer stand by’: Austria plans social media ban for under-14s | News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Austrian officials have announced plans to ban children under the age of 14 from using social media platforms, citing addiction and health risks. The government says draft legislation will be prepared by June, targeting platforms based on addictive algorithms and harmful content. This move follows similar considerations and actions in other European countries.

articles.title: France calls IOC sex testing a ‘step backwards’ while Trump praises move | Olympics News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: France's sports minister has criticized the International Olympic Committee's decision to introduce genetic testing to determine eligibility for women's events, calling it a 'step backwards' that raises ethical and scientific concerns. In contrast, US President Donald Trump praised the move, which essentially bars transgender athletes from female categories, aligning with his earlier executive order.

articles.title: Ukraine announces ‘mutually beneficial’ defence deal with Saudi Arabia | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has announced a defence agreement with Saudi Arabia, focusing on technological cooperation and investment. The deal comes as Gulf countries face Iranian drone and missile attacks, and Ukraine seeks to share its expertise in countering drones gained from its war with Russia. Saudi Arabia has not publicly confirmed the pact.

articles.title: Qatari PM and US officials discuss strategic ties amid Iran war | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Qatar's Prime Minister met with senior US officials in Washington to discuss strengthening strategic and defence cooperation amid the ongoing war on Iran. Both sides stressed the importance of ensuring sustainable energy supplies and the continued flow of Qatari LNG to global markets. The talks occurred against a backdrop of regional tension and Iranian attacks on Gulf infrastructure.

articles.title: Saudi, UAE, Iraq: Can three pipelines help oil escape Strait of Hormuz? | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article examines whether three major oil pipelines in the Middle East can compensate for the severe disruption to shipping through the Strait of Hormuz caused by the US-Israel war on Iran. It notes that traffic through the strait has plunged by over 95%, forcing countries to seek alternative routes. The piece specifically mentions Saudi Arabia's East-West Petroline as one pipeline being used, though it lacks the capacity to fully replace the strait.

articles.title: ‘Raising 10 red flags’: Is Israel’s army exhausted? | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Israel's army chief has warned the cabinet that the military is on the brink of collapse due to exhaustion from prolonged conflicts. He urged the government to pass new conscription and reserve duty laws to sustain the force. The article notes that the army, heavily reliant on reservists, has been involved in multiple campaigns across Gaza, the West Bank, Lebanon, and Syria.

articles.title: Norway to add more than $11bn to defence budget over 10 years | Business and Economy News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Norway's government has announced a plan to increase defence spending by 115 billion kroner ($11.84bn) over the next decade, aligning with NATO commitments. The increase is attributed to rising equipment costs and lessons from the war in Ukraine, and will include support for Ukraine. The funding will go towards new submarines, frigates, and other military capabilities, though some procurement projects will be delayed.

articles.title: Nixon to Trump: Pakistan’s long record as backchannel between rival powers | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article details Pakistan's historical role as a diplomatic backchannel, currently facilitating messages between the US and Iran amid their war. Pakistani officials have confirmed relaying a US ceasefire proposal to Tehran. This role echoes past Pakistani mediation, such as during the US-China rapprochement in the 1970s.

articles.title: Lebanon faces ‘humanitarian catastrophe’ under Israeli assault: UN | Israel attacks Lebanon News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A UN Refugee Agency official has warned that Lebanon faces a 'humanitarian catastrophe' due to Israel's expanding bombardment and ground invasion. Over 1.2 million people have been displaced, with strikes hitting central Beirut and shelters becoming overwhelmed. The assault began after Hezbollah fired rockets following the assassination of Iran's Supreme Leader.

articles.title: Meet the children left without parents under El Salvador’s emergency decree | Child Rights News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article reports on the fourth anniversary of El Salvador's state of emergency, highlighting how mass arrests have left thousands of children without parental support. Advocacy groups estimate tens of thousands of children have been affected, suffering psychological trauma. The emergency decree, initially a temporary anti-gang measure, has been renewed 48 times since 2022.

overall_summary: This batch of articles covers a range of global news from the last six hours. The dominant theme is the ongoing US-Israel war on Iran, detailing military actions, diplomatic efforts, and regional impacts. Other stories cover World Cup football playoffs, a new political leader in Nepal, the Russia-Ukraine war, an oil spill in Mexico, and India's economic response to the energy crisis.

articles.title: US-Israel war on Iran: What’s happening on day 28 of attacks? | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: On day 28 of the war, US President Donald Trump has delayed planned attacks on Iran's energy infrastructure by 10 days, claiming peace talks are going well, while Iranian officials call a US proposal 'one-sided and unfair'. Diplomatic efforts involving Pakistan, Turkiye, and Egypt are intensifying to prevent a wider conflict. The article reports ongoing military strikes, with over 1,900 people killed in Iran, and details attacks and interceptions in Gulf states.

articles.title: Italy to play Bosnia in 2026 World Cup playoff final, Kosovo face Turkiye | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Italy beat Northern Ireland 2-0 and will travel to play Bosnia and Herzegovina in a final for a spot in the 2026 World Cup. Kosovo advanced to a playoff final against Turkiye after a 4-3 win over Slovakia. The article also notes wins for Sweden, Poland, the Czech Republic, and Denmark in their European playoff semifinals.

articles.title: Iran bans sports teams from travelling to ‘hostile’ countries amid war | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Iran has banned its national and club sports teams from traveling to countries it deems 'hostile', citing security concerns for athletes. The ban was announced before a club football match in Saudi Arabia and requires the football federation to notify the Asian Football Confederation to relocate games. The article notes this comes amid the war and mentions Iran's separate negotiations with FIFA to move its World Cup matches from the US.

articles.title: Israeli opposition leader rails against ‘multi-front war without strategy’ | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Israeli opposition leader Yair Lapid has warned the military is 'stretched to the limit' and accused the government of waging a 'multi-front war without a strategy', steering towards a 'security disaster'. His comments echo a leaked warning from the military chief. The article reports on Israel's plan to create a buffer zone in south Lebanon and ongoing clashes with Hezbollah.

articles.title: Ukraine fends off increased attacks, strikes Russian oil revenue | Russia-Ukraine war News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Ukraine has responded to increased Russian assaults, potentially the start of a spring offensive, by striking Russian oil export terminals at Ust-Luga and Primorsk. These strikes have severed an estimated 40% of Russia's oil export revenue. The article details the attacks on oil infrastructure and increased ground assaults by Russian forces in eastern Ukraine.

articles.title: Now in power, Nepal’s rapper-politician Balen Shah faces new challenge | Politics | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Rapper-turned-politician Balendra (Balen) Shah has been sworn in as Nepal's prime minister after his Rastriya Swatantra Party won a landslide victory. Analysts say Shah has a historic opportunity to deliver on the aspirations of young Nepalis but faces the challenge of extremely high public expectations. An immediate test will be implementing the findings of a commission investigating last year's Gen Z-led uprising.

articles.title: How extensive is Russia’s military aid to Iran? | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article examines the extent of Russia's military aid to Iran, noting US President Trump's comment that Moscow might be helping 'a bit'. It reports that Russia is likely providing Iran with satellite and intelligence data, specifically from its Liana spy satellite system, to track US military assets. The piece also notes the long history of mutual arms supplies between the two countries.

articles.title: Iran emergency workers search for survivors after deadly US-Israel attacks | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Emergency workers in Iran are searching for survivors after US-Israeli strikes on Tehran and Qom, where an air raid killed six people. A deputy health official reported the war has killed at least 1,937 people in Iran. The Norwegian Refugee Council warned that millions of Iranians are displaced or living in fear, with civilians paying the highest price.

articles.title: India lowers fuel taxes, says rumours of lockdown ‘completely false’ | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: India has slashed fuel taxes to protect consumers from rising global energy prices caused by the war on Iran. The government cut petrol duties and removed diesel duties, taking a significant fiscal hit. Petroleum Minister Hardeep Singh Puri also denied rumours of an impending lockdown due to the energy crisis, calling them 'completely false'.

articles.title: Wildlife killed, reefs damaged in ‘active’ Gulf of Mexico oil spill | Climate Crisis News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: An active oil spill in the Gulf of Mexico from an unidentified vessel and two natural seeps has spread over 600 km, affecting seven nature reserves. Mexican authorities confirmed the spill has killed sea turtles, a manatee, and fish, and damaged about 17 reefs. Environmental groups have criticized the government's response and communication about the incident.

articles.title: ‘My heart burns with pain’: Iranian mother tells UN of Minab school attack | News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Mohaddeseh Fallahat, whose two children were killed in a US missile strike on a girls' school in Minab, Iran, addressed the UN Human Rights Council, describing her grief. Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi told the council the attack was 'deliberate and intentional', not a miscalculation. A UN special rapporteur suggested the attack may have resulted from a mistake using outdated intelligence, which is under investigation.

overall_summary: The articles cover diverse global issues from the last six hours. They report on Australia's new hate speech laws facing criticism, a precedent-setting move to feature President Trump's signature on US currency, and Malaysia securing passage for its ships through the Strait of Hormuz. Other stories detail the economic impact of the Iran conflict on Sri Lanka, analyze the strategic importance of the Strait of Hormuz, and examine Nigeria's e-waste crisis fueled by imports from developed nations.

articles.title: Australia’s post-Bondi crackdown accused of targeting pro-Palestinian voices
articles.summary: Human rights groups have criticized Australia's new hate speech laws, saying they will have a 'chilling effect' on support for the Palestinian cause. The laws, rushed through after a deadly attack in Bondi Beach, specifically target speech considered anti-Semitic and carry penalties of up to two years imprisonment. A senior lawyer expressed concern that the broad legislation could suppress legitimate criticism of Israel's actions in Gaza and be used against human rights groups.

articles.title: Trump’s signature to appear on US currency in first for sitting president
articles.summary: President Donald Trump's signature will appear on US currency to mark the 250th anniversary of Independence Day, a first for a sitting president. The Treasury Secretary said the change recognizes Trump's 'historic achievements' and the country's economic path. The move has drawn criticism from some who liken it to the behavior of dictators, coming a week after approval of a commemorative gold coin bearing Trump's image.

articles.title: Malaysia’s ships allowed to pass Strait of Hormuz, PM Anwar says
articles.summary: Malaysian Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim says Iran has granted Malaysian ships clearance to pass through the Strait of Hormuz, which Iran has effectively closed. Anwar thanked Iran's president for the 'early clearance' and said Malaysia is securing the release of oil tankers and workers. The government is introducing fuel conservation measures, including reducing subsidized petrol quotas, as the global energy crunch affects the country despite its status as a net energy exporter.

articles.title: Sri Lanka braces for new economic crisis as war on Iran continues
articles.summary: Sri Lanka has reintroduced a QR-based fuel rationing system similar to one used during its 2022 economic crisis, due to disruptions from the war on Iran closing the Strait of Hormuz. The country imports 60% of its energy needs through the strait and has raised fuel prices by approximately 33%. Experts warn of significant food price increases as nearly half the world's urea fertilizer also passes through the strait, creating major challenges for the current government.

articles.title: The Strait of Hormuz is not just an oil chokepoint
articles.summary: The article argues the Strait of Hormuz is the 'aortic valve of globalised production' and a single point of failure for global order, not merely an oil chokepoint. It carries vital materials including urea for fertilizers, aluminum, helium for semiconductors, and petrochemicals. The piece provides historical context, noting how control of the strait has been contested for centuries by various powers including Persians, Portuguese, British, and now Iran.

articles.title: ‘Truly junk’: E-waste from rich nations floods local markets in Nigeria
articles.summary: Nigeria faces a growing e-waste crisis as developed countries export discarded, near-end-of-life electronics to its markets. Around 60,000 tonnes of used electronics enter Nigeria annually through key ports, with many items already damaged or toxic. The trade, largely from European countries and the US, often violates international restrictions like the Basel Convention, worsening environmental and health hazards across West Africa.

overall_summary: The articles cover a range of global events from the last 6 hours, including a deadly migrant shipwreck off Djibouti, a critical WTO meeting on trade rules, and escalating tensions in the US-Iran conflict. Other stories detail Argentina's terrorist designation for a cartel, economic impacts in the Philippines, a football friendly, a US deportation agreement, and an analysis of Myanmar's civil war.

articles.title: At least nine people dead, dozens missing as boat sinks off Djibouti coast | Migration News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A boat carrying over 300 migrants and refugees sank off the coast of Djibouti, leaving at least nine dead and 45 missing. The Djiboutian coastguard rescued 266 survivors, all Ethiopian nationals, who are receiving support. The IOM warns this could be the first of many such incidents this year as the hot season brings rougher seas to the perilous migration route.

articles.title: WTO holds crunch meeting amid growing uncertainty over multilateral system | International Trade News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The WTO is holding a critical ministerial conference in Cameroon, described as a make-or-break moment for the organization. Director-General Ngozi Okonjo-Iweala stated the old world order is not returning amid global trade disruptions. Key divisions exist, with the US criticizing the 'most-favoured nation' principle while China defends it, and the EU seeks a more flexible framework.

articles.title: Trump postpones US strikes on Iranian power grid to April 6 amid talks | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump has postponed a threatened attack on Iran's power grid to April 6, citing progress in negotiations. The threat is aimed at pressuring Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. This is the second such delay, and rights groups like Amnesty International have denounced the plan as a threat to commit war crimes.

articles.title: Argentina declares Jalisco New Generation Cartel a ‘terrorist’ group | Crime News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Argentina has designated the Mexico-based Jalisco New Generation Cartel as a terrorist organization, aligning with similar US and Canadian designations. The announcement from President Javier Milei's office stated it strengthens international security cooperation. This reflects a broader policy shift in the hemisphere, where the Trump administration has expanded the use of the 'terrorist' label to justify military actions against criminal groups.

articles.title: Manila’s streets empty as fuel prices surge amid Strait of Hormuz crisis | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Streets in Metro Manila are unusually empty as soaring fuel prices, linked to the Iran war and Strait of Hormuz crisis, keep vehicles off the road. This has severely impacted the incomes of workers like parking attendants and vendors. The economic strain threatens the Philippines' GDP growth forecasts and has exposed the insufficiency of the public transport system as commuters crowd railway stations.

articles.title: Mbappe nets for 10-man France in win against Brazil in World Cup warm-up | World Cup 2026 News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: France defeated Brazil 2-1 in a friendly match in the United States, a potential warm-up for the 2026 World Cup. Kylian Mbappe, returning from a knee injury, scored an early goal, with Hugo Ekitike adding another. The match proceeded despite prior disputes over security costs and was played on a temporary grass field at Gillette Stadium.

articles.title: Costa Rica to accept 25 deportees per week under Trump deportation effort | Donald Trump News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Costa Rica has agreed to accept up to 25 migrants deported from the US per week as part of a 'third-country' agreement with the Trump administration. The government describes the pact as non-binding and reserves the right to accept or reject transfers. Such agreements have been criticized for leaving vulnerable migrants in a legal 'black hole' in foreign countries.

articles.title: Who is fighting in Myanmar’s multi-front civil war? | Conflict News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Myanmar is in the sixth year of a civil war triggered by the military's 2021 coup. The conflict involves the military regime, various ethnic armed groups, and pro-democracy forces, with alliances described as fluid. The military, bolstered by arms from China and Russia, has regained confidence, while the conflict has resulted in tens of thousands of deaths and millions displaced.

overall_summary: This batch of articles covers significant international developments across sports, conflict zones, legal matters, and technology. Major themes include a new IOC policy banning transgender athletes from women's Olympic events, escalating tensions and displacement in Lebanon due to Israeli military actions, and diplomatic maneuvers regarding conflicts in Gaza and Iran. Additional stories involve a legal dispute over funding for Venezuela's former president, a Dutch court ruling against an AI tool, and an incident involving London police and journalists.

articles.title: Olympics align with Trump as transgender athletes banned from women’s games | Olympics News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The International Olympic Committee (IOC) has adopted a new policy banning transgender women from competing in women's events at the Olympics, aligning with a U.S. executive order. The policy, which will be enforced starting with the 2028 Los Angeles Games, limits eligibility to 'biological females' as determined by a mandatory gene test.
