IOC President Kirsty Coventry stated the move aims to protect fairness in the female category, and the policy also restricts athletes with differences in sex development (DSD).

articles.title: Details revealed of Board of Peace plan for Gaza disarmament | Israel-Palestine conflict News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A plan submitted by Board of Peace Director General Nickolay Mladenov details a proposed eight-month, multiphase process for the disarmament of Hamas and other Palestinian groups in Gaza. The plan calls for disarmament to proceed in parallel with Israel fulfilling obligations, including allowing reconstruction materials and increased humanitarian aid into Gaza, and transferring administration to a national committee. Hamas has historically refused to disarm while Israeli occupation continues, and Palestinian experts have suggested the plan amounts to the 'political surrender' of Hamas.

articles.title: London police officer filmed in crowd intimidating Al Jazeera journalists | Police News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The Metropolitan Police confirmed an off-duty officer was involved in an incident where a group of residents surrounded and intimidated Al Jazeera journalists reporting on a suspected arson attack in North London. Video footage shows men shouting at the crew, with one identified as a police officer using derogatory language. The police stated they are reviewing the footage to identify any offences and emphasized that journalists must be able to work without intimidation.

articles.title: More than one million displaced by Israel’s evacuations in Lebanon | Israel attacks Lebanon News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Israel has issued expanded evacuation orders for Lebanese residents south of the Zahrani River, threatening those who stay. The orders, covering about 14% of Lebanon's territory, have displaced over one million people—more than 18% of the population—within two weeks. The humanitarian crisis is severe, with many in overcrowded shelters or sleeping outdoors, and over 250,000 people have fled the country, including thousands of Lebanese citizens crossing into Syria.

articles.title: Trump says Iran ‘begging’ for deal to end war as Tehran issues new demands | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump claimed Iran is 'begging to make a deal' to end the war, while Iran has formally responded to a U.S. proposal by asserting its rights over the Strait of Hormuz and issuing new demands. Iran's conditions, reported by the Tasnim news agency, include an end to assassinations, war reparations, and cessation of hostilities from regional resistance groups. A U.S. special envoy suggested Iran was seeking an 'off-ramp' for negotiations, but an Iranian source accused the U.S. of deceptive tactics.

articles.title: US judge weighs Trump decision to bar Venezuelan funds for Maduro’s defence | Nicolas Maduro News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A U.S. judge declined to dismiss drug-trafficking and weapons charges against former Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro and his wife but questioned the Trump administration's decision to bar Venezuela from funding their legal defence. Judge Alvin Hellerstein challenged prosecutors' national security argument, noting Maduro poses no threat while imprisoned and emphasizing the constitutional right to a vigorous defence. Maduro and his wife pleaded not guilty in January after being taken into U.S. custody.

articles.title: Dutch court bans xAI’s Grok from generating nonconsensual nude images | Technology News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A Dutch court ordered Elon Musk's xAI to stop its Grok AI tool from generating and distributing nonconsensual nude images in the Netherlands, threatening daily fines for noncompliance. The ruling came in a civil suit brought by a monitoring center, which demonstrated the tool could still create such imagery despite company measures. The court placed the burden on xAI to prevent its tools from being used to create sexualized deepfakes.

articles.title: Israel sends more troops into southern Lebanon as ground invasion expands | Israel attacks Lebanon News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Israel is deploying more troops from Division 162 into southern Lebanon to expand a 'buffer zone,' joining two other divisions already operating there. The move follows Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's statement about creating a larger zone to counter Hezbollah's missile threat. Lebanese Prime Minister Nawaf Salam warned the invasion threatens Lebanon's sovereignty and violates international law, stating his government would submit a complaint to the UN Security Council.

overall_summary: The articles cover a range of global developments, with a primary focus on the escalating US-Israel war on Iran and its regional and global consequences. Other major stories include a landmark social media liability verdict, a diplomatic treaty in North Korea, and a major sports controversy in Africa.

articles.title: Iranian naval commander Alireza Tangsiri killed in attack, Israel says | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Israel's defense minister announced that an Israeli airstrike killed Iranian naval commander Alireza Tangsiri, holding him responsible for blocking the Strait of Hormuz. The attack reportedly occurred in Bandar Abbas and targeted other senior naval officers. There has been no official Iranian confirmation of the killing, which would represent another major loss for Iran's military leadership.

articles.title: Germany warns of world economic ‘catastrophe’; OECD cuts UK growth forecast | News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: German Defense Minister Boris Pistorius labeled the US-Israel war on Iran an economic 'catastrophe' for the world. Separately, the OECD sharply downgraded its 2026 growth forecast for the UK to 0.7%, citing planned fiscal tightening and higher energy prices. Pistorius stated Germany was not consulted before the war and urged a ceasefire.

articles.title: Iran to Lebanon: Four million people displaced by US-Israeli war | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The UN estimates 3.2 million people have been displaced within Iran due to the war, while over a million have been displaced in Lebanon due to Israeli evacuation orders. In Iran, over 85,000 civilian sites have been damaged, including homes, schools, and healthcare facilities. Aid agencies and neighboring countries are bracing for a potential refugee crisis.

articles.title: Venezuela’s Maduro set to again appear in US court: How strong is the case? | Donald Trump News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Former Venezuelan leader Nicolas Maduro is set for his second US court appearance since his abduction by US forces in January. Maduro's defense has argued he is a 'prisoner of war' and that his capture was illegal. Legal experts describe the prosecution of a former head of state as largely uncharted territory, with many questions about evidence and legal strategy unanswered.

articles.title: Jury finds Meta, YouTube liable for social media addiction: What we know | Social Media News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A Los Angeles jury found Alphabet's Google and Meta liable in a landmark civil trial over youth social media addiction. The plaintiff, a 20-year-old woman, testified that addictive platform features exacerbated her depression, suicidal thoughts, and body dysmorphia. The jury recommended the companies pay $6m in damages, though TikTok and Snapchat had previously settled their parts in the case.

articles.title: North Korea’s Kim meets Lukashenko, slams ‘pressure ​on Belarus from West’ | News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un and Belarusian President Alexander Lukashenko signed a friendship treaty during a visit to Pyongyang. Lukashenko said relations were entering a 'fundamentally new stage', while Kim stated they opposed 'undue pressure on Belarus from the West'. Both nations are close allies of Russia and have backed its war in Ukraine.

articles.title: As a Palestinian, I stand in solidarity with the Iranian people. Here’s why | US-Israel war on Iran | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: This opinion piece expresses Palestinian solidarity with the Iranian people, arguing that Western promises of freedom are empty and that imperialism seeks control, not liberation. The author draws parallels between Palestinian and Iranian experiences of war, citing a specific attack on a girls' school in Iran that used a 'double-tap' strike tactic familiar in Gaza.

articles.title: Senegal pledges to fight ‘robbery’ after CAF strips it of AFCON title | Africa Cup of Nations News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Senegal's football federation has pledged to fight what it calls an 'administrative robbery' after the Confederation of African Football stripped it of the African Cup of Nations title. The decision followed Senegal walking off the pitch to protest a penalty awarded to Morocco in the final. Senegal's legal team has appealed to the Court of Arbitration for Sport, arguing the ruling violates the principle that refereeing decisions are final.

articles.title: ‘My phone is a brick’: Russians scramble for information as data blocked | Cybersecurity News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Widespread mobile internet outages in major Russian cities like Moscow and St. Petersburg are disrupting daily life, with authorities citing security concerns over Ukrainian drone attacks. Digital rights experts suggest the blackouts may be a test for a government 'whitelist' of approved websites. The outages have proven unpopular and are estimated to have caused significant economic losses.

articles.title: Russian officials meet US counterparts as Moscow denies aiding Iran | Russia-Ukraine war News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A Russian delegation has arrived in the US for meetings, marking the first such trip since relations soured over the war in Ukraine. The Kremlin hopes it will revive bilateral engagement. The visit comes as Moscow denies a report alleging it is close to completing a drone shipment to Iran, dismissing it as media lies.

articles.title: Tehran’s’tollbooth’: How Iran picks who to let through Strait of Hormuz | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Iran's de facto blockade of the Strait of Hormuz has caused a major energy crisis, with nearly 2,000 vessels stranded. Reports indicate Iran's parliament is preparing legislation to collect tolls for ships transiting the strait, arguing it ensures security. The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps has already imposed a 'toll booth' system, controlling vessel traffic and contributing to a global surge in oil prices.

overall_summary: This batch of articles covers a range of global news from the last 6 hours, including sports developments, legal disputes, and the expanding regional impacts of the US-Israel war on Iran. Major themes include civilian casualties from the conflict, geopolitical tensions, and significant events in international sports.

articles.title: NBA owners vote to explore Seattle, Las Vegas expansion bids | Basketball News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: NBA owners have unanimously voted to explore bids for expansion teams in Seattle and Las Vegas, with the 2028-29 season as a potential entry point. The vote allows the league to formally explore these opportunities, with bids expected to cost between $7bn and $10bn per team. If expansion proceeds, it would be the NBA's most significant since 2004, and a Western Conference team would likely move east to balance the leagues.

articles.title: Senegal appeal to CAS against handing over of AFCON title to Morocco | Africa Cup of Nations News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Senegal has appealed to the Court of Arbitration for Sport (CAS) against the decision to strip them of the Africa Cup of Nations title and award it to Morocco. The Confederation of African Football (CAF) made the decision after Senegalese players walked off the pitch to protest a penalty award during the final. CAS has confirmed receipt of the appeal and states it will conduct proceedings swiftly.

articles.title: Deaths and debts: Missiles in Gulf shake millions of South Asian families | US-Israel war on Iran | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article details the impact of the US-Israel war on Iran on South Asian migrant workers in the Gulf, focusing on a family in India whose son died in Qatar, reportedly from a heart attack after hearing missiles. It states that most of the civilian deaths in Gulf countries from Iranian attacks have been South Asian nationals. The piece highlights the economic reliance of millions of South Asian families on remittances from these workers, which is now under threat.

articles.title: In U-turn, UK police say Palestine Action protesters will be arrested again | News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: London's Metropolitan Police has reversed its stance, warning that anyone showing support for the group Palestine Action is now likely to be arrested. This follows a High Court ruling that found the government's ban on the group unlawful, but the ruling's effect is suspended pending a government appeal. Police state they must enforce the law as it currently stands, which still criminalizes support for the proscribed group.

articles.title: US-Israel war on Iran: What’s happening on day 27 of attacks? | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The US-Israel war on Iran is intensifying, with strikes reported to be increasing in number and intensity. US President Donald Trump claims Iran is engaged in peace talks, while Iran rejects negotiations and vows to continue resisting. The article lists various developments, including civilian casualties, Iranian retaliatory strikes, and military actions reported by Gulf states like Saudi Arabia and the UAE.

articles.title: Two teenagers killed in Shiraz, two dead in Abu Dhabi as Iran war expands | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Two teenage boys were killed in a US-Israeli strike on a residential area in Shiraz, Iran. Separately, two people were reported killed in Abu Dhabi from falling debris of an intercepted missile. Israel announced extensive strikes on Isfahan, and the article describes an expansion in the geographic scope of the attacks within Iran, alongside continued missile barrages and injuries in Israel.

articles.title: US seeks Hamas ‘political surrender’ in new Gaza plan | Armed Groups News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: US envoys have presented a written demand for the complete disarmament of Hamas and allied Palestinian factions in Gaza as part of a new plan. Analysts describe the proposal as seeking the 'political surrender' of Hamas, linking reconstruction and Israeli troop withdrawal to the surrender of weapons. The plan is viewed by Palestinian observers as a coercive ultimatum that abandons previous agreements.

articles.title: Helium hitch: Why US-Israel war on Iran could cause MRI scan delays | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The war has disrupted about one-third of global helium supplies, critical for MRI scanners and semiconductors, largely due to shipping restrictions through the Strait of Hormuz and halted production in Qatar. Qatar, a major producer, exports most of its helium via the Strait, where traffic has nearly halted following Iranian warnings. The shortage is expected to have ripple effects across medical and high-tech industries.

articles.title: ‘He will call me Mama’: The Gaza ‘grandmother’ raising an orphaned baby | Women | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article profiles Maha al-Rubaie, a 56-year-old woman in Gaza who is raising her orphaned grandnephew, Hamza. Hamza's parents and siblings were killed in Israeli airstrikes during the war, and he was delivered via emergency caesarean after his mother's death. Maha, who had previously raised Hamza's father after he was orphaned, now cares for the baby in a former classroom, referring to herself as his 'Mama'.

articles.title: Venezuela’s Maduro set to appear in US court months after abduction | News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Former Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro is set to appear in a US court as he seeks to have his drug trafficking indictment dismissed. Maduro and his wife, who were abducted by US forces in January and remain jailed, plead not guilty to all charges, which they deny as part of an imperialist plot. The article notes that while Maduro's party remains in control, he has been sidelined within the government led by acting President Delcy Rodriguez.

articles.title: Cuban president says Raul Castro involved in US talks amid oil blockade | News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Cuban President Miguel Diaz-Canel says former leader Raul Castro is involved in early-stage talks with the United States, amid soaring tensions and a US oil blockade that has caused nationwide blackouts. The announcement follows a threat from US President Donald Trump about taking over Cuba. UN officials have warned of a humanitarian crisis and a collapsing health system on the island due to the energy shortage.

articles.title: Brazil vs France: When and where is the match? Will Mbappe, Vinicius play? | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Brazil and France are set to play a glamour friendly in Boston, USA, as both teams prepare for the upcoming FIFA World Cup. The match will feature stars like Kylian Mbappe and Vinicius Junior, though Neymar is absent from the Brazil squad due to what coach Carlo Ancelotti cited as a physical issue. This is the first meeting between the teams in 11 years.

articles.title: Amid US-Israel war, Iran football team trains in Turkiye before World Cup | World Cup 2026 News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Iran's national football team is training in Turkiye for two friendly matches ahead of the World Cup, against a backdrop of the US-Israel war on Iran. The camp has restricted media access to avoid distractions. The federation is in discussions with FIFA about moving its World Cup matches from the US to Mexico over safety concerns, and striker Sardar Azmoun was omitted from the squad for a perceived act of disloyalty.

articles.title: Israel hinders repair of damaged water infra deepening Gaza’s health crisis | Gaza | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Palestinians in Gaza struggle to access clean water as Israel's military offensive has decimated water infrastructure and hinders repairs. Israeli policies, including a blockade on 'dual-use' equipment and control over areas like the 'Yellow Line', prevent the restoration of water systems. The UN estimates 70% of Gaza City's water supply infrastructure is disrupted, deepening a public health crisis.

articles.title: Dozens dead in Bangladesh as passenger bus plunges into Padma River | News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: At least 24 people, including children, died after a bus carrying 40 passengers plunged into the Padma River in central Bangladesh. The bus lost control while attempting to board a ferry. Rescue efforts recovered bodies from the submerged bus, with officials fearing more may be missing. Road accidents are common in Bangladesh due to poor infrastructure and reckless driving.

overall_summary: The articles cover a range of international economic and political developments. They report on Malaysia tightening expatriate rules, Australia banning visitors from Iran, US lawmakers proposing a pause on AI data centers, and rising oil prices following Iran's denial of talks with the US.

articles.title: Malaysia moves to tighten rules for expats, raising fears of talent flight | Business and Economy News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Malaysia's government plans to reduce reliance on foreign workers by raising the minimum salary threshold for visas and capping length of stay, aiming to encourage local hiring and boost incomes. The move has created uncertainty for long-term expatriates, including highly-paid professionals, who contribute significantly to the economy. The policy is part of a national strategy citing that reliance on low-skilled foreign labor has hampered technology adoption and wage growth.

articles.title: Australia bans visitors from Iran amid war in the Middle East | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Australia has imposed a six-month ban on Iranian visitors, citing the conflict in Iran increases the risk they may not depart when their visas expire. The ban applies to Iranian citizens outside Australia, even with existing visas, with exceptions for family members of citizens and permanent residents. The government describes the move as in the national interest, while a refugee support group criticizes it for blocking a pathway to safety.

articles.title: US lawmakers push for pause in data centres until AI safeguards in place | Technology News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: US lawmakers Bernie Sanders and Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez have introduced legislation calling for a moratorium on new AI data center construction until national safeguards are established. They argue a pause is needed to address potential harms to workers, civil liberties, and the environment from the rapid AI rollout. The proposal follows grassroots opposition to data centers over environmental and economic concerns, though the bill faces a challenging path in Congress.

articles.title: Oil prices rise higher as Iran denies US talks, dimming deescalation hopes | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Oil prices increased, with Brent crude surpassing $104 a barrel, after Iran denied reports of direct talks with the US, reducing hopes for de-escalation in the conflict. The price rise follows a previous easing on reports of a US peace plan. Iran's closure of the Strait of Hormuz and attacks on energy facilities have driven prices up over 40% since the war began, with shipping transits through the strait drastically reduced.

overall_summary: This batch of articles covers a range of international news from the last 6 hours, including sports disciplinary actions, US military operations, major sports investments, and geopolitical tensions. Key themes involve racism in football, US foreign policy in the Caribbean and towards Cuba, significant business deals in Indian cricket, and diplomatic warnings from Iran.

articles.title: Benfica fined for fans’ racism at Real Madrid game with UEFA investigation | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: UEFA has fined Portuguese club Benfica 40,000 euros for racist chants and gestures by two supporters during a Champions League game against Real Madrid. The club must also serve a one-year probation period to avoid a partial stadium closure. A separate UEFA investigation is ongoing into an allegation that Benfica player Gianluca Prestianni racially abused Real Madrid's Vinicius Junior during the same match.

articles.title: Four killed in latest US attack on alleged drug-smuggling boat in Caribbean | Donald Trump News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The United States has conducted another lethal strike on an alleged drug-trafficking vessel in the Caribbean, killing four people. US Southern Command stated the vessel was operated by a designated terrorist organization and was engaged in narco-trafficking. The attack is part of Operation Southern Spear, a boat-bombing campaign that has reportedly killed approximately 163 people since September and has been criticized by legal experts as extrajudicial killing.

articles.title: US investors bet big on Indian cricket with record billion-dollar IPL deals | Sport News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: US investors have made two record-breaking acquisitions of Indian Premier League cricket teams on the same day. A consortium including former Walmart chairman Rob Walton bought the Rajasthan Royals for $1.63 billion, and a group led by David Blitzer's Bolt Ventures and Blackstone bought the Royal Challengers Bengaluru for $1.78 billion. These deals highlight the growing international investment appeal of India's premier cricket league.

articles.title: Barcelona hammer Real Madrid in Women’s Champions League quarterfinals | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Barcelona's women's team secured a commanding 6-2 victory over rivals Real Madrid in the first leg of their Women's Champions League quarterfinal. Barcelona, now unbeaten in 25 games, built a strong lead with goals from Ewa Pajor, Esmee Brugts, Irene Paredes, Vicky Lopez, and a penalty from Alexia Putellas. The result puts Barcelona in a dominant position ahead of the second leg next week.

articles.title: Mexico will continue accepting Cuban medical workers despite US pressure | Donald Trump News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum has stated that Mexico will continue its agreement to receive Cuban medical workers, praising their service in underserved rural areas. This comes despite US pressure, including a new law that could lead to sanctions on countries participating in Cuba's medical programme, which the US describes as forced labour. Sheinbaum emphasized the bilateral agreement has been very beneficial for Mexico.

articles.title: Migrants march in southern Mexico to denounce immigration restrictions | Migration News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: An estimated 500 migrants and asylum seekers have begun a march from Tapachula in southern Mexico to protest difficulties in the local immigration system. Participants cite long lines, movement restrictions, and allegations of being charged for legally free documentation as major obstacles. The march occurs amid reports and court questions in the US about a potential secret agreement for Mexico to accept deported Cubans.

articles.title: Sarah Mullally enthroned as first female archbishop of Canterbury | Religion News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Sarah Mullally has been formally installed as the Archbishop of Canterbury, becoming the first woman to lead the Church of England. The ceremony at Canterbury Cathedral was attended by 2,000 guests, including Prince William and Prime Minister Keir Starmer. In her inaugural sermon, she prayed for peace in conflict zones and acknowledged past church safeguarding failures.

articles.title: Tehran issues warning to regional neighbour if Iranian island occupied | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Iran's parliament speaker, Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, has warned that intelligence suggests 'Iran's enemies' plan to occupy an Iranian island with support from an unnamed regional country. He stated any such attempt would be met with continuous attacks on that country's vital infrastructure. This warning comes as the US denies Iranian claims of peace talks and threatens further military action while deploying additional troops to the region.

overall_summary: The articles cover a diverse range of international news from the last six hours. Topics include World Cup qualification playoffs, analysis of the US-Israel war on Iran, legal actions by pro-Palestine activists, forced displacements in East Jerusalem, a UN resolution on the transatlantic slave trade, a US social media liability case, US political developments involving Donald Trump, and a rescheduled diplomatic visit to China.

articles.title: Which teams can still qualify for the World Cup, and when are the playoffs? | World Cup 2026 News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article details the upcoming FIFA interconfederation playoff tournament, which will determine the final two teams to qualify for the 2026 World Cup. It explains the match schedule, with semifinals featuring Bolivia, New Caledonia, Jamaica, and Suriname, and finals against the higher-ranked DRC and Iraq. The games will be hosted in Mexico, and the DRC and Iraq are noted as the favorites to qualify.

articles.title: What it would take to end the Iran war | US-Israel war on Iran | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: This opinion piece analyzes the conditions needed to terminate the US-Israeli war on Iran, arguing that while a mutually hurting stalemate is emerging, a viable bargaining architecture is absent. It states that deep structural problems, particularly a near-total erosion of trust and a commitment problem, are significant barriers to a durable peace. The article cites military and economic costs on both sides but questions the feasibility of reaching a lasting agreement.

articles.title: Palestine Action hunger strikers to sue prisons over alleged mistreatment | Prison News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Four pro-Palestine activists, released on bail after a months-long hunger strike in UK prisons, plan to take legal action against the prisons for alleged medical neglect and mistreatment. The activists described suffering lasting medical conditions, physical assault, solitary confinement, and being denied proper electrolytes during their strike. They were held in connection with a 2024 raid on an Elbit Systems factory.

articles.title: Israel forcibly displaces more Palestinian families in East Jerusalem | Israel-Palestine conflict News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Israeli forces have forcibly displaced about a dozen Palestinian families from their homes in the Batn al-Hawa area of Silwan in occupied East Jerusalem, according to rights group B'Tselem. The Norwegian Refugee Council states the homes are expected to be transferred to an Israeli settler organization and that over 1,000 more Palestinians in East Jerusalem are at risk of forced eviction. The evictions are based on a discriminatory Israeli law from 1970.

articles.title: UN passes resolution naming slave trade ‘gravest crime against humanity’ | Slavery News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The UN General Assembly has adopted a non-binding resolution, proposed by Ghana, that recognizes the transatlantic slave trade as the 'gravest crime against humanity' and calls for reparations. The resolution passed with 123 votes in favor, 3 against (including the US and Israel), and 52 abstentions (including the UK and EU countries). It urges member states to engage in dialogue on reparatory justice, including formal apologies and financial compensation.

articles.title: US jury finds Meta, Alphabet liable in landmark social media addiction case | Social Media News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A California jury found Alphabet's Google and Meta liable and awarded $3 million in damages in a lawsuit alleging their platforms' addictive designs harmed a young plaintiff. The plaintiff, who began using YouTube and Instagram as a child, claimed the features exacerbated her mental health issues. The companies argued her issues had other causes, but the jury found social media was a 'substantial factor' in the harm.

articles.title: Democrat Emily Gregory wins Florida seat in Trump’s Mar-a-Lago district | Donald Trump News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Democratic Representative Jamie Raskin has sent a letter to the Attorney General alleging that newly released records show former President Donald Trump mishandled highly sensitive classified documents, potentially for personal business interests. Raskin claims the documents were so sensitive that only six people in the US government had access and that Trump may have 'sold out our national security'. The Department of Justice dismissed the allegations as a 'cheap political stunt'.

articles.title: Trump to visit Xi Jinping in China on May 14 and 15 after Iran war delay | Xi Jinping News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump's visit to China for a meeting with President Xi Jinping has been rescheduled to May 14-15, delayed from late March due to the ongoing war against Iran. The White House stated that President Xi understood the need for Trump to be present during combat operations. China has called for an end to the war, which has disrupted global trade through the Strait of Hormuz.

overall_summary: This batch of articles covers escalating tensions in the Middle East, including the US-Israel war on Iran and Israeli attacks on Lebanon, alongside European political developments, financial scrutiny, and sports news. Key themes include diplomatic efforts, military actions, and regional spillover effects.

articles.title: Spain’s PM says Israel to inflict on Lebanon same destruction as in Gaza | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez stated that Israel seeks to inflict the same level of destruction on Lebanon as it has on Gaza. He also condemned the US-Israeli war on Iran, calling it a scenario far worse than the 2003 Iraq invasion. Sanchez highlighted Spain's arms embargo on Israel and the withdrawal of its ambassador as signs of diplomatic tension.

articles.title: Denmark’s PM resigns after failing to secure majority in general election | News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Danish Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen submitted her government's resignation after her coalition suffered a large defeat in a general election. Her Social Democratic Party had its worst election result since 1903, winning only 38 seats. Both left and right blocs fell short of a majority, leaving the centrist Moderates Party as potential kingmakers in coalition talks.

articles.title: Large Polymarket, Wall Street bets on Trump’s war news under scrutiny | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Traders have made hundreds of millions of dollars in bets on the US-Israeli war on Iran with suspiciously well-timed trades, suggesting possible insider knowledge. The cryptocurrency platform Polymarket has been linked to such activity, with researchers identifying accounts that netted over $2 million by correctly predicting the start of the war. Analysts note red flags like wallet splitting and accounts with no prior history placing successful bets.

articles.title: Estonia, Latvia report drone incursions from Russian airspace | Russia-Ukraine war News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Estonia and Latvia reported drones entering their airspace from Russia, raising concerns about spillover from the Ukraine war. A drone struck a power station chimney in Estonia, causing no casualties, while another crashed in Latvia without damage. Latvian authorities said the drone might be Ukrainian and are investigating.

articles.title: US-Iran mediation: What are each side’s demands – and is a deal possible? | Energy News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump has sent a 15-point ceasefire plan to Iran via Pakistan, though Iran denies talks are happening. The war has disrupted global energy markets and shipping, with Iran closing the Strait of Hormuz to most traffic. Mediators are pushing for potential in-person talks in Islamabad, but the sides' demands remain far apart.

articles.title: Asian Champions League quarterfinal sends Iran’s Tractor FC to Saudi Arabia | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Iranian club Tractor FC will travel to Saudi Arabia to play a playoff against Dubai's Shabab Al-Ahli in the Asian Champions League Elite. The AFC rescheduled postponed western zone playoffs for April in Jeddah due to the Middle East war. Organizers hope for calm to complete the fixtures as planned.

articles.title: Why is Mohamed Salah leaving Liverpool, and which club will he join next? | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Liverpool forward Mohamed Salah announced he will leave the club at the end of the season on a free transfer. Salah cited a nonexistent relationship with manager Arne Slot and feeling made a scapegoat. Potential next destinations include Saudi Pro League clubs or Major League Soccer in the US.

articles.title: Democrat captures Florida House seat in Trump’s Mar-a-Lago district | Elections News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Democrat Emily Gregory won a special election for a Florida state House seat that includes Trump's Mar-a-Lago resort, defeating a Trump-endorsed opponent. Democrats hailed the victory as a sign of voter discontent with Republicans ahead of midterm elections, citing issues like the cost of living. This marks another Democratic upset in a traditionally Republican area.

articles.title: Iranian attacks amount to violation of sovereignty, Gulf states tell UN | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Gulf states told the UN Human Rights Council that Iranian attacks on their countries violate sovereignty and international law. They demanded reparations and condemned Iran's strikes, which have targeted infrastructure and closed the Strait of Hormuz. The council passed a non-binding resolution demanding Iran cease 'unprovoked attacks'.

articles.title: Mohamed Salah: Is Liverpool exit the end? Can World Cup spur Egypt star? | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Mohamed Salah's departure from Liverpool raises questions about his future, with a move to another Premier League club seen as unlikely due to sentimentality and financial constraints. The most probable option is a transfer to the Saudi Pro League, which has shown past interest. His exit marks the end of an era for one of the Premier League's greats.

articles.title: Hezbollah chief urges unity, no talks with Israel amid Lebanon attacks | Israel attacks Lebanon News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Hezbollah chief Naim Qassem called for national unity in Lebanon and rejected negotiations with Israel while under attack. He stated the choices are surrender or resistance, vowing to fight 'without limits' against Israeli assaults. The conflict has killed over 1,000 people in Lebanon and drawn global condemnation.

articles.title: Anthropic’s case against the Pentagon could open space for AI regulation | Business and Economy News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A California judge suggested the US Defense Department may be illegally trying to punish AI company Anthropic for advocating restrictions on AI weapons and surveillance. The company was designated a 'supply chain risk,' which could block government contracts. The case could set a precedent for AI regulation and government-company relations.

articles.title: Missiles overhead, silence below: Israel’s home front holds firm | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Daily life in Israel is constrained by missile attacks, school closures, and police restrictions, stifling dissent against the war on Iran. Activist groups report that exhaustion and safety concerns make protests nearly impossible. While public support for the war remains strong, societal fractures are anticipated as resentment grows.

articles.title: Iran calls US proposal to end war ‘maximalist, unreasonable’ | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Iran has received a 15-point US ceasefire plan but describes it as 'extremely maximalist and unreasonable.' A diplomatic source said there have been no direct talks since the war began, only messages via mediators. Mediators, including Pakistan, Egypt, and Turkiye, are pushing for potential in-person negotiations.

articles.title: War on Iran: What troops is the US moving to the Gulf? | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The US is amassing thousands of troops in the Middle East, signaling a potential shift from an air campaign to ground operations in the war on Iran. Reinforcements include soldiers from the 82nd Airborne Division and Marine units. The conflict has involved strikes on Iranian targets and Iranian counterattacks, with the Strait of Hormuz as a key strategic point.

overall_summary: The articles cover escalating military and diplomatic developments in the US-Israel war on Iran, including conflicting claims about negotiations and attacks across the region. Other stories detail impacts on global energy markets, personnel changes in Iran, and separate conflicts in Ukraine and Gaza.

articles.title: US-Israel war on Iran: What’s happening on day 26 of attacks? | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The US-Israeli war against Iran continues into its 26th day with reported strikes in Iran, Israel, and the Gulf. US President Trump claims negotiations with Iran are underway, but Iranian officials deny this, and there are reports of a US-delivered 15-point peace plan. The conflict is disrupting global energy markets, particularly due to issues in the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Iran’s new security boss Mohammad Zolghadr: Why his appointment matters | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Iran has appointed former IRGC commander Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr as the new head of the Supreme National Security Council, replacing Ali Larijani who was killed in an airstrike. Zolghadr, a heavyweight security figure with a strong military background, is seen as someone suited to handle Iran's current war footing and complex security challenges amid external pressure and internal unrest.

articles.title: US talking to itself, says Iran as Trump claims wheels of diplomacy turning | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: An Iranian military spokesperson mocked US claims of diplomacy, stating the US is 'negotiating with itself' to save face after failing in the war. This contradicts President Trump's assertions that productive talks with Iran are ongoing. Reports indicate the US has sent a 15-point ceasefire proposal to Iran, but Al Jazeera's correspondent in Tehran describes 'total confusion' over the status of any negotiations.

articles.title: Russia, Ukraine tit-for-tat attacks knock out power for over half a million | Russia-Ukraine war News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Reciprocal attacks on energy infrastructure have left around 450,000 people without power in Russia's Belgorod region and 150,000 in Ukraine's Chernihiv region. The attacks have caused disruptions to heating and water supplies, with repair work expected to take days. Separate incidents include a Ukrainian drone attack sparking a fire at a Russian oil export hub and a Russian attack killing one person in Ukraine's Odesa region.

articles.title: Dreams of survival: How war has restructured Gaza’s job market | Israel-Palestine conflict News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The war has devastated Gaza's traditional economy, forcing professionals like artist Abdulrahman al-Awadi to abandon their careers for makeshift jobs like running a mobile phone charging station. Widespread destruction and the collapse of basic services have created a 'survival economy' where skilled graduates must adapt to small-scale work to secure a bare-minimum income. This shift illustrates how the conflict has erased traditional professions and created new ones shaped by war and shortages.

articles.title: Is Iran’s negotiating position stronger than when US-Israeli war started? | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Analysts suggest Iran's negotiating position may have hardened since the war began, with Tehran seeking to end the conflict on its own terms and extract major concessions. Iran aims for a post-war order that restores deterrence and provides long-term economic and security guarantees, potentially including fees for Strait of Hormuz passage. Despite US claims of degraded Iranian capabilities, Iran has demonstrated it can still launch precise attacks and maintains significant leverage through its control of the strategic waterway.

articles.title: Belarus’s Lukashenko makes first visit to North Korea in bid to deepen ties | Russia-Ukraine war News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Belarusian President Alexander Lukashenko has begun his first official visit to North Korea to strengthen ties, with plans to sign a treaty of friendship and several agreements. The two countries are united by their support for Russia's war in Ukraine and face Western sanctions. The visit follows increased cooperation, including North Korea sending soldiers and weapons to Russia in exchange for aid, which has reduced Pyongyang's dependence on China.

articles.title: ‘A heinous crime’: Air strikes kill seven fighters in Iraq’s Anbar | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Aerial attacks on a military base in Iraq's Anbar province killed seven fighters and wounded 13, with Iraq's Defence Ministry condemning it as a 'heinous crime'. An Iraqi police source told Al Jazeera the strikes targeted positions of the Iran-aligned Popular Mobilisation Forces (PMF), marking an escalation as Iraq is increasingly drawn into the US-Israeli war on Iran. Iraq has denounced the attack and reserves the right to respond, following a deadly strike on the same base the previous day.

articles.title: Iran’s closure of the Strait of Hormuz is an international crisis | US-Israel war on Iran | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The disruption of maritime traffic through the Strait of Hormuz for over two weeks has created a global crisis, as the strait is a critical chokepoint for about 20% of the world's petroleum and a significant portion of LNG. Major energy producers like Iraq, Saudi Arabia, Qatar, and the UAE have had to cut production, invoke force majeure, or reroute exports, causing oil prices to soar to nearly $120 per barrel. The article argues this situation requires global action due to its severe impact on energy markets and potential violation of international law.

overall_summary: This batch of articles covers geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, legal action against tech companies, and global economic impacts. Key themes include Iran's stance on Strait of Hormuz shipping, regional conflicts affecting Lebanon and global fuel prices, and corporate developments in tech and sports.

articles.title: Iran says ‘non-hostile’ ships can pass safely through Strait of Hormuz | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Iran has stated that 'non-hostile' ships may transit the Strait of Hormuz safely if they do not participate in or support acts of aggression against Iran and comply with safety regulations. This comes amid a collapse in maritime traffic through the key waterway, which has triggered a global energy crisis. The statement was issued as US President Donald Trump claimed negotiations are underway to end the US-Israel war on Iran, though traffic remains a fraction of pre-conflict levels.

articles.title: US jury orders Meta to pay $375m for endangering children | Technology News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A jury in New Mexico has ordered Meta to pay $375 million for harming children's mental health and making them vulnerable to sexual exploitation. The verdict marks the first successful US state lawsuit against Meta over child safety issues, with jurors finding the company violated state law by prioritizing profits over safety. Meta says it disagrees with the verdict and will appeal, while a second phase of proceedings to determine additional penalties and platform changes is scheduled for May.

articles.title: Canada tells Israel that Lebanon’s sovereignty ‘must not be violated’ | Crimes Against Humanity News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Canada has strongly condemned Israel's plans to occupy southern Lebanon, warning that Lebanese sovereignty and territorial integrity 'must not be violated.' This statement comes amid Israeli preparations for a major ground invasion, which has already caused significant casualties and displacement in Lebanon. France has also urged Israel to refrain from such operations, citing major humanitarian consequences.

articles.title: New Zealand footballers willing to play Iran World Cup match outside US | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: New Zealand football players have expressed willingness to play their FIFA World Cup 2026 opener against Iran outside the United States if necessary, due to geopolitical tensions. Iran has threatened to boycott its group matches on US soil because of the US-Israel war on Iran. While New Zealand is preparing as if the scheduled match in Los Angeles will proceed, players stated they could accommodate a venue switch to locations like Mexico or Canada.

articles.title: OpenAI pulls AI video app Sora as concerns grow on deepfake videos | Social Media News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: OpenAI is shutting down its social media app Sora, which allowed users to share short-form AI-generated videos but raised concerns about deepfakes and nonconsensual content. The company announced the decision abruptly, blindsiding partners like Disney, with whom it had a $1 billion deal. This move represents OpenAI's first major step to refocus its business on potentially more lucrative areas like coding tools and corporate customers.

articles.title: From Pakistan to Egypt, Iran war drives up fuel prices in the Global South | Business and Economy News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Developing economies in Asia, Africa, and the Middle East are bearing the brunt of surging energy costs due to the US-Israel war on Iran and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. Countries like Pakistan, Bangladesh, and Sri Lanka, which are heavily dependent on imported energy, are implementing measures such as fuel rationing, public holidays, and work-from-home orders to conserve dwindling reserves. Governments are attempting to cushion the public from price shocks with subsidies, but analysts warn the situation could worsen if the war continues.

articles.title: Amid US-Israeli attacks, people in Iran struggle to survive ailing economy | US-Israel war on Iran | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Iranians are grappling with the economic fallout and a prolonged internet shutdown amid the US-Israeli war on their country. Business activity has significantly declined, with vendors reporting sales at about one-third of usual levels during the Persian New Year period. The population faces high inflation, reduced purchasing power, and job scarcity, exacerbated by years of sanctions and mismanagement, with some families temporarily displaced from major cities.

overall_summary: The articles cover a range of international developments from the last 6 hours. Major themes include a significant shift in NASA's space exploration strategy, escalating diplomatic and military tensions in the Middle East related to the US-Israel war on Iran, a crisis in US air travel due to unpaid TSA workers, and the global ripple effects of the Iran conflict impacting energy supplies and regional conflicts.

articles.title: NASA to spend $20bn on moon base, nuclear-powered Mars spacecraft | Science and Technology News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: NASA has announced a major overhaul of its Artemis program, scrapping plans for a lunar-orbit space station and committing $20bn over seven years to build a surface base on the moon. The agency also plans to launch a nuclear-powered spacecraft called Space Reactor 1 Freedom by 2028 to demonstrate propulsion technology for Mars missions. These changes reshape contracts and partnerships, shifting focus from the Lunar Gateway to surface infrastructure as the US faces competition from China's lunar ambitions.

articles.title: Iraq to summon US, Iran envoys over deadly attacks: PM’s office | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Iraq's government plans to summon the US and Iranian envoys to deliver formal protests over recent deadly attacks on Iraqi territory linked to the US-Israel war on Iran. The attacks include strikes on the Popular Mobilisation Forces in Anbar province and the Kurdistan Regional Guard in Erbil. Iraq's National Security Council also stated it will lodge a formal complaint with the UN Security Council concerning these acts of aggression.

articles.title: Long lines, unpaid TSA workers: Experts say US air travel system in crisis | Travel News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Unpaid Transportation Security Administration (TSA) workers, a result of a partial government shutdown, have led to mass resignations, staffing shortages, and hours-long security lines at major US airports. The Trump administration has deployed ICE agents to fill gaps, a move criticized due to their lack of training. Analysts warn the situation, compounded by travel complications from the war on Iran, creates an image of systemic dysfunction and questions the safety of US air travel.

articles.title: Trump keeps up claims of talks with ‘the right people’ in Iran | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump maintains that negotiations to end the war on Iran are underway, claiming Tehran wants a deal and alluding to a 'present' related to oil and gas. His claims coincide with media reports of a US 15-point peace plan relayed to Iran, but fighting continues and Iran had previously dismissed such talk as 'fake news'. An analyst suggests Iran would want to end the war on its own terms, seeking deterrence and potential economic gains, such as fees for Strait of Hormuz passage.

articles.title: Russian weapons, tactics seen in Ukraine are shaping Myanmar’s civil war | Military News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Russian-supplied airpower, intelligence, and battlefield tactics from the Ukraine war are helping Myanmar's military government gain an edge in its civil war. Analysts note the use of Russian-made jets and drones, as well as the adoption of Russian 'meat assault' tactics using waves of conscripted infantry. The 2021 coup and Russia's 2022 invasion of Ukraine have brought the two sanctioned countries closer, with Myanmar providing military assistance to Russia and receiving weapons and support in return.

articles.title: Philippine president declares energy emergency as impact of Iran war felt | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. has declared a one-year national energy emergency, citing the 'imminent danger' to the country's energy supply from the US-Israel war on Iran. The declaration empowers the government to procure fuel, combat hoarding, and ensure the supply of essential goods. Transport unions have criticized the move as a 'superficial band-aid,' and the government is reportedly seeking US exemptions to potentially buy oil from sanctioned countries like Iran and Venezuela.

overall_summary: This batch of articles covers a range of international news from the last six hours. Major themes include sports controversies and announcements, escalating geopolitical conflicts, and significant legal and diplomatic developments.

articles.title: Palestinian FA calls for Israeli ban after FIFA discrimination report | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The Palestinian Football Association is calling for the Israeli Football Association to be banned from international institutions. This follows a FIFA report that found 'multiple breaches' of anti-discrimination obligations, for which the IFA was fined. The PFA chief cited the IFA's 'racist nature', alleged criminal activity by some officials, and the inclusion of clubs from Israeli West Bank settlements as grounds for the ban.

articles.title: When are UEFA’s World Cup 2026 playoffs, and which nations are involved? | Sport News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article details the final UEFA qualification playoffs for the 2026 FIFA World Cup. It lists the 16 European teams still competing for the final four European spots. The teams are divided into four paths, with single-leg semifinals on March 27 and pathway finals on March 31, the winners of which will qualify.

articles.title: Anthropic challenges US Pentagon’s ban in San Francisco court showdown | Business and Economy News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The AI company Anthropic is in court challenging the US Defense Department's decision to cut ties with it. The Pentagon banned Anthropic after it refused to remove safety guardrails preventing its Claude AI model from being used for autonomous weapons and mass surveillance. Anthropic's lawsuit claims the designation as a supply chain risk was unlawful retaliation for its advocacy on AI safety.

articles.title: Afghanistan frees detained US citizen Dennis Coyle as gesture of ‘goodwill’ | Prison News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Afghanistan's Taliban government has released US citizen Dennis Coyle, who was detained for over a year. The Foreign Ministry said the Supreme Court deemed his detention period sufficient and decided on his release as a humanitarian gesture for Eid al-Fitr, following a plea from his family. US Secretary of State Marco Rubio welcomed the release, thanking the UAE and Qatar for their support.

articles.title: Iran names successor to security chief killed in US-Israeli attack | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Iran has appointed former IRGC commander Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr as the new chief of the Supreme National Security Council, replacing Ali Larijani who was killed in a US-Israeli air strike. The appointment is seen as adding more military layers to Iran's national security establishment. Zolghadr's new role consolidates the IRGC's growing influence amid uncertainty in Iran's leadership following the succession of Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei.

articles.title: Aid flotilla vessel arrives in Cuba amid US-driven energy crisis | Donald Trump News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The first boat of a humanitarian aid flotilla has arrived in Cuba, bringing food, medicine, and solar panels. Activists describe the delivery as a small 'gesture of solidarity' meant to draw attention to a worsening US energy blockade, which Cuban authorities say has resulted in a nearly total ban on petroleum imports for three months. The convoy comes as US President Donald Trump has expressed a desire for regime change in Cuba.

articles.title: Egypt’s Mohamed Salah to leave Liverpool at end of season after 9-year stay | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Liverpool forward Mohamed Salah will leave the club at the end of the season. The club made the announcement, stating Salah wanted to provide transparency to supporters. The 33-year-old Egyptian international confirmed the news in a video message, expressing his deep connection to the club where he won multiple major trophies and became their third-highest all-time goalscorer.

articles.title: US says they’re talking, Iran says they’re not. Who’s telling the truth? | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article examines conflicting claims about negotiations to end the US-Israel war on Iran. US President Donald Trump insists 'productive' talks have occurred, while top Iranian officials like parliamentary speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf deny any negotiations are underway. The analysis suggests both sides have strategic reasons for their public statements, with Trump potentially seeking to calm financial markets and Iran wanting to maintain economic pressure on the US.

articles.title: Russia hits Ukraine with rare daytime barrage as new offensive begins | Russia-Ukraine war News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Russia has launched one of its largest aerial attacks on Ukraine, using 948 drones in a 24-hour period as it moves troops and equipment to the front line, signaling the start of a new offensive. The rare daytime strikes killed civilians and damaged a UNESCO World Heritage site in Lviv. Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy warned that Kyiv faces a deficit of air defence missiles while Washington is focused on the war on Iran.

overall_summary: This batch of articles covers escalating conflicts in the Middle East, particularly the US-Israel war on Iran and its regional consequences, including a major oil crisis and diplomatic tensions. Other stories report on international trade deals, environmental issues, human rights concerns, and sports news.

articles.title: How does the current global oil crisis compare with the 1973 oil embargo? | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article compares the current oil crisis caused by the US-Israel war on Iran to the 1973 oil embargo. It states the current closure of the Strait of Hormuz is stopping 20 million barrels per day, compared to 4.5 million barrels removed in 1973. The International Energy Agency has agreed to release strategic reserves and issued consumer guidance, but experts say these measures will do little if the situation persists.

articles.title: Lebanon declares Iranian ambassador persona non grata amid Israeli attacks | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Lebanon has withdrawn accreditation from the Iranian ambassador and declared him persona non grata, demanding his departure. The Foreign Ministry cited Tehran's violation of diplomatic norms. This decision comes as Israel continues attacks on Lebanon, which have reportedly killed over 1,000 people and displaced more than 1.5 million.

articles.title: Australia and EU seal trade deal to cut reliance on China’s rare earths | News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Australia and the European Union have signed a trade deal removing tariffs for nearly all exports of Australian critical minerals. EU Commission President Ursula von der Leyen said the deal is crucial to reduce over-dependence on China, which dominates global critical mineral supply chains. The agreement is also expected to remove over 99% of tariffs on EU goods exports to Australia.

articles.title: Palestinians struggle as Gaza endures severe fuel and gas shortages | Israel-Palestine conflict News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Gaza is facing severe fuel and gas shortages, with electricity prices surging up to ten times pre-war levels. Families receive minimal gas quantities every few months, and only a fraction of the agreed-upon fuel shipments have entered the territory since a ceasefire. The war has destroyed Gaza's power network, leaving most homes without reliable electricity.

articles.title: The US should end the war asap | US-Israel war on Iran | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: This opinion piece argues the US should end the war on Iran immediately to manage its fallout. The author states destroying Iran's energy infrastructure would cause an environmental disaster and create refugees who would hate America. It suggests a unilateral US ceasefire could lead Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, lowering global oil prices.

articles.title: IPL champions Bengaluru to keep 11 seats empty in honour of stampede dead | Cricket News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Royal Challengers Bengaluru will keep 11 seats empty at their stadium in memory of fans killed in a stampede during title celebrations last year. Eleven supporters died outside the stadium in June. The team will wear black armbands and tops sporting the number 11 for their opening match, and crowd management measures have been strengthened.

articles.title: What are Iran’s cluster munitions that are penetrating Israeli defences? | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article explains Iran's use of cluster munitions in the war, which disperse multiple bomblets over a wide area. These weapons reportedly killed two people in central Israel and can better evade defence systems. Analysts say cluster munitions are particularly dangerous for civilians and convert individual missiles into broader, harder-to-defend threats.

articles.title: After setbacks across Europe, is the populist far right losing ground? | The Far Right News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Recent elections in France, Slovenia, and Italy have dealt setbacks to populist far-right parties. Analysts caution against drawing long-term conclusions, suggesting the far right may have hit a 'hard ceiling' in major cities while expanding its base elsewhere. They note the results paint a mixed picture and warn against oversimplification.

articles.title: Pakistan ‘ready to host US-Iran talks’: Can latest peace push work? | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Pakistan has offered to host talks between the US and Iran, with its foreign ministry stating it advocates for dialogue. Multiple reports suggest Pakistan, Egypt, and Turkiye are serving as messengers between Washington and Tehran. However, Iran has denied engaging in any talks, and analysts describe the diplomatic movement as tentative and fragile.

articles.title: Pakistan world’s most polluted country in 2025: Report | Climate News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A report finds Pakistan was the world's most polluted country in 2025, with fine particulate levels up to 13 times higher than WHO guidelines. The report states only 13 countries met the WHO standard, and all of the world's top 25 most polluted cities were in China, India, and Pakistan. Data gaps from discontinued monitoring programs complicate some comparisons.

articles.title: Mothers of the Plaza de Mayo reflect on 50th anniversary of Argentina coup | Human Rights News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: On the 50th anniversary of Argentina's military coup, the Mothers of the Plaza de Mayo continue to demand justice for children abducted during the dictatorship. A surviving member, 95-year-old Taty Almeida, hopes to find her missing son. UN experts have warned of 'alarming setbacks' in Argentina's human rights progress under President Javier Milei.

articles.title: Why has India arrested US, Ukrainian nationals under ‘anti-terror’ laws? | Russia-Ukraine war News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: India has arrested six Ukrainian nationals and an American citizen for allegedly entering India's northeast without permits and crossing to Myanmar to train armed groups in drone warfare. They have been charged under anti-terror laws. Two additional American tourists were arrested for flying drones near a Coast Guard headquarters in Kochi.

articles.title: Is the US talking to Iran’s Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, and who is he? | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article examines US President Donald Trump's claims of talks with Iran and reports that US envoys are communicating with Iranian parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf. Both the Iranian government and Ghalibaf have denied negotiations are taking place. Ghalibaf is a former IRGC air force commander, police chief, and mayor of Tehran.

articles.title: QatarEnergy declares force majeure on some LNG contracts due to Iran war | Business and Economy News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: QatarEnergy has declared force majeure on some long-term LNG supply contracts due to production disruptions caused by the US-Israeli war on Iran. The CEO stated an Iranian attack damaged Qatar's gas facility, wiping out about 17% of its LNG export capacity. The closure of the Strait of Hormuz and attacks on energy infrastructure have spurred global concern.

articles.title: Keir Starmer’s policy on the Iran war is a recipe for catastrophe | US-Israel war on Iran | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: This opinion piece criticizes British Prime Minister Keir Starmer for allowing the US to use UK military bases for strikes on Iran, comparing it to the Iraq war. The author argues the UK has been dragged into an illegal conflict and that Starmer's claim of non-involvement is false. It calls for the UK to withdraw all support for the war.

articles.title: UN experts urge Israel to free Gaza doctor amid reports of ‘severe torture’ | Israel-Palestine conflict News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: UN experts are calling for the immediate release of Dr. Hussam Abu Safia, a Palestinian physician detained by Israel. They report he has been subjected to severe torture and systematically denied medical treatment, endangering his life. Abu Safia was arrested in December 2024 and has been held without charge.

articles.title: Iranian IRGC’s ties to Hezbollah deepen tensions in Lebanese politics | Israel attacks Lebanon News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Lebanon's prime minister has accused Iran's IRGC of commanding Hezbollah's operations against Israel, deepening tensions. Analysts agree the Iranian group is influential, noting longstanding ties between the IRGC and Hezbollah. The Lebanese government has banned Hezbollah's military activities, but this has had little impact on the ground.

overall_summary: The articles cover a range of global news from the last 6 hours, including ongoing military conflict between the US-Israel alliance and Iran, domestic US policy, international politics, sports updates, and legal developments. The batch is dominated by reports on the escalating war and its regional consequences.

articles.title: Tiger Woods eyes Masters return with competitive golf outing at TGL | Golf News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Tiger Woods is set to return to competitive golf in the Tomorrow's Golf League (TGL) finals, serving as a test run ahead of the Masters next month. He has been recovering from lumbar disc replacement surgery and an Achilles tendon rupture. His team, Jupiter Links Golf Club, trails Los Angeles Golf Club 1-0 in the best-of-three final series.

articles.title: US-Israel war on Iran: What’s happening on day 25 of attacks? | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Conflicting claims have emerged on the 25th day of the US-Israel war on Iran, with US President Donald Trump stating discussions are ongoing for a broader peace agreement. Iranian officials firmly denied any negotiations, calling the claims 'fake news'. Trump also ordered a five-day postponement of planned US strikes on Iranian power plants and energy infrastructure.

articles.title: Mbappe fit and ready to play every Real Madrid game before World Cup | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: French striker Kylian Mbappe says he has made a '100 percent recovery' from a lingering knee injury and is fully available for Real Madrid's remaining matches. He aims to play to build form for the upcoming FIFA World Cup 2026. Mbappe has been named in the France squad for friendlies against Brazil and Colombia in the US.

articles.title: Palestine weekly wrap: West Bank attacks surge, Israel restricts Gaza aid | Israel-Palestine conflict News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Palestinians in the occupied West Bank endured a surge of violence during Eid al-Fitr, marked by settler attacks, land seizures, and movement restrictions. Israeli settlers attacked multiple villages, torching homes and vehicles, in an apparent retaliation for a settler's death. Israeli authorities also emptied the Al-Aqsa Mosque compound of Muslim worshippers during Eid.

articles.title: ICE agents deployed to US airports: Which airports are affected? | Donald Trump News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: US Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) officers have been deployed to 14 major airports, including Atlanta and New York's JFK, to help ease security lines amid a partial government shutdown. The shutdown has left many TSA officers working without pay, leading to staff shortages and delays. The ICE agents will provide administrative support but will not conduct passenger screenings.

articles.title: Polls open in key Denmark elections clouded by Trump’s Greenland threat | Politics News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Polls have opened in Denmark's parliamentary election, with Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen seeking a third term. Her firm rejection of US President Donald Trump's push to take control of Greenland boosted her approval ratings. The campaign has focused on domestic issues like the cost of living, rather than the Greenland dispute.

articles.title: Where do reported US-Iran ‘negotiations’ leave Israel? | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Israeli analysts have described confusion and disappointment after US President Trump's claim of negotiations with Iran to wind down the war, which Iran denies. Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu stated the US believes military achievements can be leveraged for a deal, but analysts suggest Trump may be seeking an exit ramp, potentially leaving Israel isolated.

articles.title: Eritrea to end 18-year AFCON isolation by playing Eswatini in qualifier | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Eritrea will end an 18-year absence from Africa Cup of Nations qualifiers when they host Eswatini on Wednesday in Morocco. The match was moved because Eritrea lacks an international-standard venue. The team's long absence is widely believed to be due to players seeking asylum when playing abroad.

articles.title: Despite Trump’s peace talk claims, US-Israeli attacks continue to hit Iran | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: US-Israeli attacks hit several Iranian cities, including Tehran, Tabriz, and Isfahan, targeting gas facilities and a pipeline. This occurred despite US President Trump's claim of talks with Tehran to end the war, which Iran denies. Iranian media reported a professor and his two children were killed in an attack north of Tehran.

articles.title: Israel kills three in Beirut as it intensifies attacks across Lebanon | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: An Israeli strike on a residential apartment in Bchamoun, near Beirut, killed at least three people, including a three-year-old girl, and wounded five others. The Israeli army pressed ahead with attacks across Lebanon and issued a new evacuation threat for Burj Shemali. The military claimed it was targeting Hezbollah and IRGC infrastructure.

articles.title: Hong Kong grants police power to demand phone and computer passwords | News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Hong Kong police are now empowered to require anyone suspected of violating the national security law to hand over passwords to their electronic devices. Refusing to comply could lead to imprisonment and fines. The new amendments, which bypassed the legislature, also allow customs officers to seize items deemed to have 'seditious intention'.

articles.title: McCullum to continue as England’s all-format cricket head coach | Cricket News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Brendon McCullum will continue as England's head coach in all formats despite recent poor performances, including an Ashes series defeat and T20 World Cup exit. Ben Stokes will remain Test captain and Rob Key as managing director. The ECB leadership expressed commitment to learning from recent failures.

articles.title: Iran launches waves of missiles towards Israel | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Iran launched a round of missiles targeting Israel, causing damage and lightly injuring six people in Tel Aviv. A multistorey apartment building was damaged, and searches were conducted for people trapped. Meanwhile, the Israeli military said its jets carried out strikes in central Tehran, targeting command centres and ballistic missile sites.

articles.title: Supporter groups file lawsuit against FIFA over World Cup ticket prices | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Football Supporters Europe and Euroconsumers have filed a complaint with the European Commission against FIFA, alleging it abused its monopoly to impose excessive ticket prices for the 2026 World Cup final. They state the cheapest final tickets start at $4,185, far higher than for the 2022 final. Some resale tickets are being offered at astronomical prices.

articles.title: Amazon says AWS Bahrain region ‘disrupted’ following drone activity | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Amazon said its Amazon Web Services (AWS) region in Bahrain was 'disrupted' amid the Middle East conflict, following drone activity. This is the second time in a month AWS operations have been affected by the war. The company is helping customers migrate to alternate regions but did not specify if the facility was directly hit.

articles.title: Bill Cosby found guilty of 1972 sexual assault, victim awarded nearly $60m | News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A US jury found Bill Cosby liable for drugging and sexually assaulting Donna Motsinger in 1972, awarding her $59.25 million. Motsinger testified that Cosby gave her pills and wine, after which she woke up undressed at home. Cosby, who denies allegations from at least 60 women, did not testify at the trial.

overall_summary: The batch consists of a single sports report concerning a major coaching change in French football. It details a reported agreement for a legendary former player to take over the national team.

articles.title: Zinedine Zidane to take over as France coach this summer: Report | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A report from ESPN states that French football legend Zinedine Zidane has verbally agreed to become the head coach of the France national team after this summer's FIFA World Cup. He is set to replace Didier Deschamps, who has been in the role since 2012. Zidane previously managed Real Madrid and, as a player, helped France win the 1998 World Cup.

overall_summary: The articles report on escalating military actions and political rhetoric in the Israel-Lebanon conflict, alongside a UN expert's condemnation of Israeli treatment of Palestinians. One details calls for annexation and intensified assaults in Lebanon, while the other accuses Israel of state-sanctioned torture.

articles.title: Smotrich urges Israel to annex southern Lebanon as assault intensifies | Israel attacks Lebanon News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Israel's Finance Minister Bezalel Smotrich has called for Israel to annex southern Lebanon up to the Litani River, stating the bombardment needs to create a 'different reality'. His comments come as Israel intensifies air and ground attacks on Lebanon, destroying civilian infrastructure and displacing over 1.2 million people, with Lebanese officials fearing it is a prelude to a broader ground invasion.

articles.title: UN expert says world has given Israel ‘licence to torture Palestinians’ | Israel-Palestine conflict News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: UN Special Rapporteur Francesca Albanese states that torture has effectively become state policy in Israel, accusing the international community of giving it a 'licence to torture Palestinians'. Her report describes a regime of 'organized humiliation, pain and degradation' in the occupied Palestinian territories, linking it to genocidal violence. Israel's mission to the UN condemned the report and Albanese, calling her an 'agent of chaos'.

overall_summary: The articles cover a range of international news from the last six hours, focusing primarily on the ongoing US-Israel war with Iran and its global repercussions. Other stories include EU tensions with Hungary, a political defeat in Italy, and two separate aviation accidents.

articles.title: Trump can declare victory in Iran – and he should | US-Israel war on Iran | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: This opinion piece by a former Trump surrogate argues that President Trump's military strategy against Iran has been successful. It claims his approach of hitting hard and fast, rather than engaging in prolonged negotiations or nation-building, has led to a clear military victory. The author contends this has weakened Iran's military and nuclear ambitions, achieving the mission's objectives without an endless war.

articles.title: Why the oil and gas price shock from the Iran war won’t just fade away | Oil and Gas | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: This analysis argues the current energy price shock due to the war on Iran is more severe and lasting than the 2022 shock from the Russia-Ukraine war. It states the closure of the Strait of Hormuz and attacks on infrastructure have physically removed a significant portion of supply from the market, unlike the 2022 situation which involved rerouted flows. The International Energy Agency's assessment indicates this is the largest supply disruption in oil market history.

articles.title: Iran denies any talks with US after Trump claims ‘productive’ discussions | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
