articles.summary: Senior Iranian officials have denied holding talks with the United States, contradicting President Trump's claim of 'productive conversations.' Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf called the report 'fakenews' meant to manipulate markets. The denials come as the war continues, with Israel launching fresh attacks on Tehran and Iran keeping the Strait of Hormuz closed, which has driven up global energy prices.

articles.title: EU calls for clarification after Hungary accused of leaking info to Russia | European Union News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The European Commission has demanded clarification from Hungary following a report that its foreign minister leaked details of EU negotiations to Russia. Hungary's Foreign Minister Peter Szijjarto rejected the report as 'fake news.' The allegations come amid tense EU-Hungary relations, notably over Hungary blocking a loan to Ukraine, and Prime Minister Viktor Orban has ordered an investigation into alleged wiretapping of his minister.

articles.title: Italy’s Meloni concedes referendum defeat, calling it ‘a lost opportunity’ | Courts News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni has conceded defeat in a referendum on her government's justice reform package. She called the result 'a lost opportunity to modernise Italy' but confirmed she would not resign. The proposed reforms, which aimed to separate the roles of judges and prosecutors and change an oversight body, faced intense opposition from critics who saw it as a political power grab.

articles.title: Colombian military plane crash kills at least one | Military News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A Colombian military plane carrying 125 people crashed after takeoff in the south of the country, killing at least one person. President Gustavo Petro stated 77 were injured and the status of 43 others was unknown. The Air Force confirmed the Hercules C-130 was transporting troops, and authorities are investigating the cause of the crash.

articles.title: Air Canada crash at LaGuardia Airport: What happened, who were the victims? | Aviation News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Two people died after an Air Canada plane struck a fire truck while landing at New York's LaGuardia Airport. Authorities are investigating the cause, with early indications focusing on air traffic control coordination. Audio recordings reveal a controller urgently tried to stop the fire truck from crossing the runway seconds before the collision.

overall_summary: The articles cover a range of topics centered on the ongoing US-Israel war on Iran and its regional and global repercussions. Key themes include escalating military actions, diplomatic maneuvers, domestic policy shifts in the US, and related incidents of violence and investigation elsewhere.

articles.title: Threats over Iran war bare Trump efforts to transform free speech: Experts | Donald Trump News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article reports that FCC Chairman Brendan Carr, referencing the war on Iran, threatened to revoke broadcast licenses for 'hoaxes and news distortions' not in the 'public interest,' a statement cheered by President Trump. Free speech advocates argue the Trump administration is using regulators to transform constitutionally protected speech rights, with actions described as more robust than previous administrations.

articles.title: Iraq pulled into Iran war as US targets Iran-aligned groups | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article states that Iraq is increasingly caught in the regional conflict as the US carried out strikes against Iran-aligned Popular Mobilisation Forces (PMF) in Baghdad. It notes the attacks put Iraqi authorities in a difficult position balancing relations with the US and armed groups. Economic woes are deepening as Iraq declared force majeure on oilfields due to Strait of Hormuz disruptions, cutting off crucial revenue.

articles.title: Trump postpones military strikes on Iranian power plants for five days | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: President Trump announced he ordered the US military to postpone strikes on Iranian power plants for five days, citing 'very good and productive conversations' with Iran about resolving hostilities. He claimed Iran wants a deal and must give up its enriched uranium, and that the Strait of Hormuz would reopen if a deal happens. The announcement brought relief and caused oil prices to drop after earlier spikes.

articles.title: Has Iran brought down an ‘unkillable’ US F-35 jet? | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article reports that after a US F-35 fighter jet made an emergency landing in the Middle East, unnamed sources and Iranian state media claimed it was struck by Iran during the war. US Central Command confirmed the aircraft landed safely and the pilot was stable, stating the incident is under investigation. If confirmed, it would be the first time an F-35 has been struck by Iran in the conflict.

articles.title: UK police investigate Jewish charity ambulance arson as hate crime | News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: UK police are investigating the arson of four ambulances belonging to the Jewish charity Hatzola Northwest in London as an anti-Semitic hate crime. No one was injured, but gas cylinder explosions damaged nearby properties. Prime Minister Keir Starmer condemned the attack, stating antisemitism has no place in society, while police search for three suspects.

articles.title: Trump shares SNL skit mocking Starmer as he speaks to UK PM over Iran war | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: President Trump shared a video of a British SNL skit mocking UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer's apparent panic over a call with him. This occurred on the same evening the two leaders discussed the Iran war. Trump has previously criticized NATO allies, including the UK, for not supporting US efforts to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, calling them 'cowards.'

articles.title: ‘Substantial evidence’ of double-tap strike in killing of Gaza’s Hind Rajab | Israel-Palestine conflict News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A report by Avaaz claims there is 'substantial evidence' that the 2024 killing of five-year-old Hind Rajab and two Red Crescent paramedics in Gaza was a deliberate 'double-tap' attack by the Israeli army. The analysis suggests the strikes contravened international law and indicate a systematic tactic of targeting first responders. The group is urging the ICC to pursue justice for what it alleges is a war crime.

articles.title: ‘False flag attack’: Iran denies claims it fired missiles at Diego Garcia | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Iran has denied targeting the US-UK military base on Diego Garcia with missiles, dismissing the claim as an 'Israeli false flag' attack. Foreign Ministry spokesman Esmaeil Baghaei cited NATO's inability to confirm the claim as evidence of disinformation. If true, the attack would suggest Iran possesses missiles with a range exceeding 4,000km, a capability it has previously said it limits.

articles.title: Energy, water, bonds: What are Iran’s targets if Trump hits power plants? | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article examines the potential targets Iran might strike if the US attacks its power plants, following Trump's ultimatum over the Strait of Hormuz. It notes Trump has since paused such strikes for five days, claiming productive talks, which Iran denies. Iranian officials have threatened to retaliate against power plants in Israel and the Gulf, and warned of further Strait closures if attacks continue.

articles.title: Trump deploys ICE agents to US airports amid staffing issues, delays | Donald Trump News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The Department of Homeland Security has begun deploying hundreds of ICE agents to assist with security at US airports experiencing staffing shortages and delays due to a funding standoff. The deployment, which Trump supports, is intended to help TSA, but Democrats and observers warn it could fuel tensions. Many TSA agents have been working without pay due to the budget dispute.

articles.title: Which countries have strategic oil reserves – and how much? | Oil and Gas News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article explains that due to Iran's blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, International Energy Agency members have agreed to release 400 million barrels from strategic oil reserves—the largest such draw in history. It describes strategic petroleum reserves as emergency stockpiles used during crises like wars. The disruption has caused oil prices to surge above $100 a barrel, prompting this coordinated response.

overall_summary: The articles cover a major aviation incident in New York, a sports result, and escalating conflict in the Middle East. The batch is dominated by news of a widening US-Israel war on Iran and related regional violence, including new strikes and economic warnings.

articles.title: Two killed after Canada Air jet hits fire truck at NYC’s LaGuardia airport | Aviation News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: An Air Canada Express flight operated by Jazz Aviation struck a fire truck while landing at New York's LaGuardia Airport, killing two people. At least 13 others were injured, and the airport was closed, with flights diverted. The National Transportation Safety Board has sent a team to investigate the collision.

articles.title: Lionel Messi scores as Inter Miami rally past NYCFC in MLS | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Inter Miami came from behind to beat New York City FC 3-2 in a Major League Soccer match. Lionel Messi scored his 901st career goal, and Micael dos Santos Silva scored the winning header in the 74th minute. The victory gave Inter Miami its first MLS win since March 7.

articles.title: World in energy crisis worse than 1970s’ oil shocks combined, IEA head says | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The head of the International Energy Agency, Fatih Birol, stated the current energy crisis caused by the US-Israel war on Iran is worse than the 1970s oil shocks and the fallout from the Ukraine war combined. He said the closure of the Strait of Hormuz and attacks on facilities have cut global oil supplies by about 11 million barrels per day. Birol warned this poses a major threat to the global economy and called for measures to reduce consumption.

articles.title: Iran war: What’s happening on day 24 of US-Israel attacks? | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: On the 24th day of the US-Israel war on Iran, President Donald Trump's 48-hour deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz is set to expire. Iran threatened to strike Gulf neighbours' energy and water systems if its power plants are attacked. The article reports ongoing attacks across the region, including Israeli strikes on Tehran, missile launches toward Saudi Arabia and the UAE, and a helicopter crash in Qatar.

articles.title: ‘Unprecedented’: Israel, US carry out extensive strikes across Iran | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Israel and the US launched a new wave of extensive strikes on Iran, with the Israeli military targeting infrastructure in Tehran. Al Jazeera's correspondent described the explosions in the capital as 'unprecedented' in size. The report states strikes hit multiple Iranian cities, causing civilian casualties and damage, while Iran continued retaliatory missile strikes on Israel.

articles.title: Israeli settlers rampage through West Bank towns for second night in a row | Israel-Palestine conflict News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Israeli settlers attacked Palestinian towns in the occupied West Bank for a second consecutive night, injuring at least nine Palestinians. Settlers set fire to homes and cars and vandalized property in multiple communities. The violence occurred as the Israeli government advances plans to expand illegal settlements in the territory.

articles.title: Israel’s displacement of civilians in Lebanon is a possible war crime | Human Rights | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A Human Rights Watch opinion piece argues that Israel's attacks and evacuation orders in Lebanon, which have displaced over a million people, may constitute a war crime. It draws parallels to Israel's tactics of forced displacement in Gaza and the West Bank. The article states that under international law, forcing civilians to leave must be a last resort and temporary, conditions it alleges Israel has violated.

overall_summary: The articles cover a dominant MotoGP victory in Brazil and a significant political development in Pakistan. The sports story details a championship lead change, while the political report focuses on a major party's decision to join a governing coalition.

articles.title: Marco Bezzecchi dominates MotoGP Brazil for fourth straight victory | Motorsports News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Aprilia rider Marco Bezzecchi won the Brazilian MotoGP for his fourth consecutive victory, taking the championship lead. He started second on the grid and controlled the race, finishing ahead of teammate Jorge Martin. The race distance was shortened due to track conditions, marking MotoGP's return to Brazil for the first time since 2004.

overall_summary: The articles cover escalating geopolitical tensions affecting Cuba and global energy markets, key election results in Slovenia and France, and a major football match. The batch highlights international political instability and its economic repercussions.

articles.title: Emerging from latest blackout, Cuba says ready for any potential US attack
articles.summary: Cuba's Deputy Foreign Minister says the nation is prepared for any potential US military aggression, responding to threats from US President Trump. The statement comes as Cuba recovers from another nationwide blackout, which officials attribute to a failure at a thermoelectric plant. The country is facing a severe fuel shortage due to a US oil blockade imposed after Trump abducted Venezuela's President Maduro.

articles.title: Slovenia’s liberals and conservatives neck and neck in parliamentary vote
articles.summary: Slovenia's parliamentary election results show the governing centre-left Freedom Movement and the opposition right-wing Slovenian Democratic Party in a near tie. With neither main party securing a majority in the 90-seat parliament, future government formation will depend on negotiations with smaller parties. The election was preceded by controversy over allegations of foreign interference involving an Israeli spy firm.

articles.title: Vinicius strikes twice as Real Madrid edge Atletico 3-2 in pulsating derby
articles.summary: Real Madrid defeated Atletico Madrid 3-2 in a dramatic derby, with Vinicius Jr scoring two goals. Real came from behind twice to secure the win, keeping them within four points of La Liga leaders Barcelona. The match saw a red card for Real's Federico Valverde and a late Atletico shot hitting the post.

articles.title: Socialist Emmanuel Gregoire wins Paris mayoral race
articles.summary: Socialist candidate Emmanuel Gregoire has won the Paris mayoral election, leading a coalition of traditional left, Green, and Communist parties. The results from nationwide municipal elections also showed the far-right National Rally falling short in key southern cities like Marseille and Toulon, though it celebrated breakthroughs in other areas. The far-right did win in Nice, with former conservative Eric Ciotti, now an RN ally, taking the mayoralty.

articles.title: Asian stock markets plunge amid Trump’s ultimatum on Iran
articles.summary: Major Asian stock markets fell sharply following a US ultimatum demanding Iran reopen the Strait of Hormuz. President Trump threatened to destroy Iran's energy infrastructure if the strait is not reopened, with a deadline set for late Monday GMT. The threat has heightened fears of a wider energy crisis, causing oil price volatility and market declines across Japan, South Korea, Hong Kong, and Australia.

overall_summary: The articles cover escalating military conflicts in the Middle East, particularly between Iran and a US-Israel alliance, with strikes and threats exchanged. Other stories detail regional consequences like a fuel crisis affecting a Pakistani cricket league, warnings of a potential Israeli ground invasion in Lebanon, ongoing violence in Gaza, and a US domestic crisis over airport security funding.

articles.title: Iranian authorities taunt US, Israel, EU amid strikes and assassinations | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Iranian military and political authorities are projecting confidence amid escalating war with the US and Israel, citing recent joint air raids and assassination attempts in Iran. Top officials, including Parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, stated that Iranian missile strikes on Israel's Dimona mark a 'new stage of battle' where 'Israel’s skies are defenceless'. The article also reports on Iranian officials making taunting remarks towards the US, Israel, and the EU.

articles.title: Pakistan T20 cricket league to be held in empty stadiums amid oil crisis | Cricket News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The Pakistan Super League (PSL) will be held without crowds and only in two cities, Lahore and Karachi, due to a fuel shortage caused by the Middle East conflict. PCB chairman Mohsin Naqvi said the decision was made because the government has asked citizens to restrict movements amid soaring oil prices, and having crowds would be contradictory. The opening ceremony is cancelled, tickets will be refunded, and several foreign players have pulled out of the tournament.

articles.title: Did Israel miscalculate Iranian military capabilities? | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article questions if Israel underestimated Iran's military capabilities after Iranian missile strikes wounded at least 180 people near the Israeli nuclear facility in Dimona. It describes Iran's missile programme as the Middle East's largest and most varied, comprising ballistic and cruise missiles with different ranges. Analysts suggest these systems, developed over decades, give Tehran significant reach despite its lack of a modern air force.

articles.title: Ukraine’s Zelenskyy urges allies to pressure Russia ahead of US talks | Russia-Ukraine war News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has called for continued sanctions pressure on Russia, specifically targeting its 'shadow fleet' of oil tankers, ahead of a second day of US-Ukraine talks in Florida. He stated that Russia increased its crude oil sales over the past week, using revenues to finance its war. Zelenskyy emphasized that tankers serving Russia's war budget 'must be stopped and blocked' to maintain economic pressure.

articles.title: Lebanon’s Aoun warns Israeli attack on bridge ‘prelude to ground invasion’ | Israel attacks Lebanon News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Lebanese President Joseph Aoun has described an Israeli strike on the Qasmiyeh Bridge, a key crossing in southern Lebanon, as a 'prelude to ground invasion'. The attack followed an order from Israeli Defence Minister Israel Katz to destroy all crossings over the Litani River. The Israeli army chief stated that the operation against Hezbollah 'has only begun' and would be prolonged, involving prepared ground operations.

articles.title: Israeli air strikes on Gaza kill four Palestinians | Israel-Palestine conflict News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Israeli air strikes in Gaza have killed four Palestinians, including three local police members in the Nuseirat refugee camp. Gaza's Health Ministry says at least 680 Palestinians have been killed since a ceasefire took effect in October. The article also reports on intensified violence in the occupied West Bank, with the UN stating over 30,000 Palestinians have been forcibly displaced there recently.

articles.title: Trump sends US immigration agents to airports as shutdown chaos deepens | Migration News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump will deploy Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) agents to major airports starting Monday to address a security crisis caused by a funding standoff. The Department of Homeland Security lacks funding, leading to unpaid TSA screeners, resignations, and long passenger queues. The move follows a political dispute where Democrats refuse to pass a full DHS funding bill without ICE reforms.

overall_summary: This batch of articles covers escalating military actions and threats in the Middle East, primarily focusing on the US-Israel war on Iran and its regional consequences. It also includes one article on a domestic Canadian legal case concerning religious freedom.

articles.title: Aftermath of Iranian missile strikes near Israel’s nuclear facility | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Iranian missiles struck two communities in southern Israel, wounding at least 180 people and damaging buildings near Israel's main nuclear research centre. This was reportedly the first time Iranian missiles penetrated Israeli air defences in that area. The strikes came after Iran's Natanz nuclear facility was hit earlier the same day, an attack Israel denied responsibility for.

articles.title: Did Iran launch missiles at US-UK base on Diego Garcia? Here’s what to know | Explainer News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The United Kingdom condemned what it called an Iranian missile attack on the joint US-UK military base on Diego Garcia in the Indian Ocean, but an Iranian official denied responsibility. US media reported two ballistic missiles were launched, with one failing mid-flight and the other intercepted. An analyst suggested that if confirmed, the attack demonstrates a longer missile range than previously known for Iran.

articles.title: Hezbollah attack kills one in north Israel as assault on Lebanon continues | Israel attacks Lebanon News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A Hezbollah rocket attack from Lebanon killed one person in northern Israel, marking the first such death since the US-Israel war on Iran began over three weeks ago. The attack occurred as Israel sent more troops into southern Lebanon, where its ground invasion has reportedly killed over 1,000 people. Israel's defence minister ordered the military to speed up the demolition of Lebanese homes and destroy bridges over the Litani River.

articles.title: Iran says will hit region’s energy sites if US, Israel target power plants | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Iran's parliament speaker warned that critical energy infrastructure across the Middle East would be 'irreversibly destroyed' if the US or Israel targeted Iranian power plants. This threat was a response to US President Donald Trump's ultimatum to open the Strait of Hormuz or face attacks on Iran's power plants. Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps also stated it would completely shut the Strait of Hormuz if such threats were executed.

articles.title: Canada’s Supreme Court must strike down Quebec’s Bill 21 | Human Rights | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: This opinion piece argues that Canada's Supreme Court must strike down Quebec's Bill 21, a law that prohibits certain public sector workers from wearing visible religious symbols. The author contends the law, passed using a constitutional override clause, effectively excludes people of faith from public employment and sends a message of exclusion to young people. The case raises fundamental questions about religious freedom and the limits of state power in a democracy.

articles.title: Trump’s changing messages on Iran war: What does it say about US strategy? | Explainer News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article examines contradictory messages from the Trump administration regarding the US-Israel war on Iran, now in its fourth week. It notes President Trump recently threatened to 'obliterate' Iran's power plants, a day after suggesting the US was 'winding down' military operations. Analysts cited say Trump launched the war without a clear goal and misjudged Iran's response, which has included retaliatory attacks and a de facto blockade of the Strait of Hormuz.

overall_summary: This batch of articles covers a range of international news from the last six hours. Major themes include a military helicopter crash in Qatar, escalating conflict between the US-Israel alliance and Iran, and elections in Slovenia and Italy. Other stories detail a nationwide blackout in Cuba and a feature on homelessness in Delhi.

articles.title: ‘Can’t live like this’: Cuba hit by second nationwide blackout in a week
articles.summary: Cuba experienced its second nationwide blackout in less than a week, marking the third major grid collapse in March. The outage was caused by a failure at the Nuevitas thermoelectric plant, leading to a cascading effect. The article states the blackouts are exacerbated by a US-imposed oil blockade and the loss of Venezuela as a main oil supplier.

articles.title: ‘I count their breaths’: A homeless mother protects her children in Delhi
articles.summary: This feature profiles Abida Begum, a homeless mother living with her five children on a Delhi pavement. She describes a life of constant vigilance to protect her children from harassment, theft, and the elements, often going hungry so they can eat. The article details their daily struggles, including the lack of privacy, sanitation, and basic security.

articles.title: Six dead as Qatar military helicopter crashes after ‘technical malfunction’
articles.summary: A Qatari military helicopter crashed in the country's territorial waters due to a technical malfunction, killing six of the seven people on board. The deceased included Qatari military personnel, a Turkish soldier, and Turkish civilian collaborators. The crash occurred amid heightened regional tensions, with Qatar having recently faced Iranian attacks on its energy infrastructure.

articles.title: Iran war: What’s happening on day 23 of US-Israel attacks?
articles.summary: On the 23rd day of conflict, US President Donald Trump threatened to attack Iranian power plants if the Strait of Hormuz is not reopened within 48 hours. Israel launched new attacks on Tehran, while Iran continued retaliatory strikes, injuring nearly 100 people near an Israeli nuclear facility. The article also notes Saudi Arabia intercepted Iranian drones and expelled Iranian diplomatic staff.

articles.title: Slovenia’s governing liberals face right-wing populists in tight election
articles.summary: Slovenians are voting in a tight parliamentary election between incumbent liberal Prime Minister Robert Golob and right-wing populist Janez Jansa. Neither candidate is likely to win a parliamentary majority, making smaller coalition partners decisive. A victory for Jansa, an admirer of Donald Trump, could shift Slovenia's foreign policy, reversing moves like recognizing Palestine and imposing an arms embargo on Israel.

articles.title: Why the world should worry about Israel’s nuclear doctrine
articles.summary: This opinion piece argues that Israel's nuclear doctrine, based on 'nuclear opacity,' sets a dangerously low threshold for the use of nuclear weapons. It states that Israeli strategic thinking, shaped by a fear of existential threat, could consider nuclear use against a non-nuclear state if national survival is believed to be at risk. The author contends this risk is heightened by Israel's current multi-front conflicts.

articles.title: Italians vote in high-stakes justice referendum in key test for Meloni
articles.summary: Italians are voting in a two-day referendum on contested judicial reforms proposed by Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni's government. Meloni says the reforms will make the judiciary more independent, but critics argue they are a political power grab that weakens judicial independence. The vote is seen as a key political test for Meloni ahead of parliamentary elections next year.

overall_summary: The articles cover two distinct events from the last 6 hours. One reports on a series of violent attacks by Israeli settlers against Palestinians in the occupied West Bank. The other details a mixed martial arts fight result that sets up a future championship bout.

articles.title: Casualties as Israeli settlers set fire to homes and cars in West Bank | Occupied West Bank News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Israeli settlers set fire to homes and vehicles in at least two areas of the occupied West Bank near Jenin, wounding at least one person. The attacks, which occurred during Eid al-Fitr celebrations, also included incidents in Masafer Yatta and other villages involving assaults and stone-throwing. Settler violence in the West Bank has intensified since the war in Gaza began, with over 1,000 Palestinians killed in the territory according to UN figures.

articles.title: Evloev upsets Murphy, sets up featherweight title shot against Volkanovski | Mixed Martial Arts News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Russia's Movsar Evloev defeated the previously unbeaten Lerone Murphy by majority decision at UFC Fight Night 270 in London. Despite a point deduction for a foul, Evloev's performance, including a strong grappling attack in the final round, secured the win. The victory is expected to earn him a title fight against featherweight champion Alexander Volkanovski.

overall_summary: This batch of articles covers escalating military conflicts in the Middle East, including a major hospital attack in Sudan and a dangerous tit-for-tat exchange between Iran and Israel. It also reports on diplomatic expulsions, a cleared ICC prosecutor, and political warnings from Brazil's president.

articles.title: ‘Tears and grief’: Mother’s Day in Gaza marked by mourning | Israel-Palestine conflict News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article describes Mother's Day in Gaza as a day of grief and hardship, with mothers mourning children lost in the war and children mourning their mothers. It features personal accounts from displaced Palestinians, including a mother who lost three sons and a 14-year-old girl who lost her mother. An Amnesty International report cited in the article highlights the severe consequences for women and girls during the conflict.

articles.title: ICC Chief Prosecutor Khan cleared of sexual misconduct by judges: Report | ICC News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A panel of judges has cleared International Criminal Court (ICC) Chief Prosecutor Karim Khan of all wrongdoing following an investigation into alleged sexual misconduct, according to a Middle East Eye report. The report states the judges found the factual findings did not establish misconduct. Khan had taken voluntary leave from his position in May while awaiting the inquiry's results.

articles.title: WHO says attack on Sudan hospital killed 64, including 13 children | Sudan war News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The head of the World Health Organization (WHO) says an attack on a teaching hospital in Sudan's Darfur region killed at least 64 people, including 13 children, two nurses, and a doctor. The attack damaged key departments, rendering the facility non-functional. This incident brings the total number of fatalities linked to attacks on health facilities during Sudan's war to over 2,000.

articles.title: Iran strikes towns near Israel’s nuclear site, wounds over 100 | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Iranian missile strikes on the Israeli towns of Dimona and Arad wounded more than 100 people. Iranian state television framed the strikes as retaliation for an earlier attack on Iran's Natanz nuclear facility. The International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) said it received no indication of damage to the Dimona nuclear research center itself.

articles.title: Saudi Arabia expels Iran military attache, four embassy staff | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Saudi Arabia has given Iran's military attache and four other embassy staff 24 hours to leave the kingdom, citing 'repeated Iranian attacks' on its territory. The move follows attacks on Saudi energy facilities and a drone strike on the Red Sea port of Yanbu. The Saudi Foreign Ministry warned that continued attacks would have significant consequences for bilateral relations.

articles.title: ‘They want to colonise us’: Brazil’s Lula warns of foreign interference | Politics News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Brazilian President Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva criticized what he called a colonial approach towards developing nations, gesturing at actions by the United States without naming it directly. He referenced US policies towards Cuba, Venezuela, and the war on Iran, questioning their motives. Lula warned that foreign powers now want to control critical minerals and 'colonise us again'.

articles.title: Trump issues 48-hour Hormuz Strait ultimatum, threatens Iran power plants | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump threatened to attack Iran's power plants if freedom of navigation is not fully restored in the Strait of Hormuz within 48 hours. Iran responded by saying it would target all US energy infrastructure in the region if attacked. The ultimatum comes as shipping traffic through the vital waterway has virtually ground to a halt during the war.

overall_summary: This batch of articles covers a range of international news from the last 6 hours, including geopolitical analysis on energy in the Middle East, military incidents in Bahrain, US domestic politics, the death of a former FBI director, severe flooding in Hawaii, humanitarian conditions in Gaza, and EU energy security concerns. The stories are largely connected to the ongoing regional conflict and its wider repercussions.

articles.title: Arab states should beware of Israel’s hegemonic energy expansion | Energy | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: This opinion piece argues that Israel is expanding its energy control in the region, which could advance a colonialist agenda. It details how Egypt and Jordan, which are increasingly dependent on Israeli gas imports, have recently signed agreements to supply gas to Syria and Lebanon. The article notes conflicting reports about whether the gas being exported is of Israeli origin.

articles.title: Bahrain says Patriot system intercepted drone over homes | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A Bahraini official stated that a Patriot air defence system intercepted an Iranian drone over a residential area on March 9, preventing casualties. This account differs from the US military's statement on the same day, which said an Iranian drone struck a neighbourhood, injuring civilians. Bahrain's government has not addressed the discrepancy between the two accounts.

articles.title: Trump threatens to deploy ICE to airports amid Homeland Security shutdown | Donald Trump News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump threatened to deploy Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) agents to conduct airport security amid a partial government shutdown affecting the Department of Homeland Security. He blamed Democrats for the impasse and said ICE would focus on arresting undocumented immigrants, with 'heavy emphasis' on those from Somalia. Trump has repeatedly made derogatory statements about Somalis and Somali Americans.

articles.title: Former FBI chief Robert Mueller, known for Trump investigation, dead at 81 | Politics News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Robert Mueller, the former FBI director and special counsel who led the investigation into Russian interference in the 2016 US election, has died at age 81. His death was marked by an acrimonious post from former President Donald Trump, while Democratic figures remembered him as a public servant. Mueller's tenure included reforming the FBI after 9/11 but also overseeing controversial surveillance practices.

articles.title: Flash flooding swamps Hawaii, prompting evacuation orders for 5,500 people | Weather News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Heavy rains have caused the worst flooding in Hawaii in 20 years, prompting evacuation orders for about 5,500 people on Oahu's north shore. Officials warned of a high risk of road failure and expressed particular concern about the potential failure of the 120-year-old Wahiawa dam. No deaths have been reported, but at least 200 people have been rescued and 10 hospitalized with hypothermia.

articles.title: Eid without toys: Israeli restrictions drive up prices in Gaza | Israel-Palestine conflict News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A feature story describes how Israeli restrictions on imports into Gaza have led to severe toy shortages and exorbitant prices ahead of the Eid holiday. It focuses on a displaced mother, Rania al-Saudi, who cannot afford dolls for her daughters due to prices that have quadrupled since before the war. The article illustrates the war's impact on daily life and children's experiences in Gaza.

articles.title: EU urges members to start storing winter gas as Iran war causes price surge | Oil and Gas News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The European Union has urged member states to start early on filling gas storage for next winter after attacks on energy facilities in the Gulf caused prices to surge. Energy Commissioner Dan Jorgensen suggested lowering the storage target to 80% and starting early to mitigate price pressure. The call follows an Iranian attack on Qatar's LNG facility, which has reduced global supply and increased competition, though the EU's direct supply from Qatar is limited.

overall_summary: The articles cover a range of international news from the last six hours, including a sports eligibility ruling, a drone strike in Iraq, a US legal verdict against Elon Musk, an alleged attack on an Iranian nuclear facility, a major football final, and diplomatic unease following remarks by the US president.

articles.title: Lin Yu-ting cleared to return to boxing after sex eligibility review | Boxing News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Olympic gold medal-winning boxer Lin Yu-ting of Taiwan has been cleared to return to competition at the Asian Boxing Championships following a review of her sex eligibility by World Boxing. The sport's governing body implemented a policy requiring a genetic test, and Lin's national association stated that independent experts confirmed she has been female since birth. The Algerian boxer Imane Khelif, who was also scrutinized at the 2024 Paris Olympics, has not competed under the new policy but has indicated a desire to return.

articles.title: Drone strike near Iraqi intelligence headquarters in Baghdad kills officer | News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A drone strike by 'outlaw groups' targeted the headquarters of the Iraqi National Intelligence Service in Baghdad, killing one police officer. The attack occurred hours after other drone strikes targeted a US military complex at Baghdad International Airport. Iraq has been drawn into regional conflict following US-Israeli attacks on Iran, with frequent strikes on its territory and attacks on US interests by Iran-backed groups.

articles.title: US jury finds Elon Musk misled investors during Twitter purchase | Elon Musk News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A federal jury in California found that Elon Musk misled Twitter shareholders through false statements in two May 2022 tweets, which drove down the company's share price as he was poised to buy it. The verdict in the class action lawsuit means Musk could be ordered to pay billions in damages, with a lawyer for the plaintiffs estimating about $2.6 billion. Musk's lawyers said they will appeal the decision.

articles.title: Iran says US and Israel attacked Natanz nuclear facility | News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Iran's atomic energy organisation stated that the United States and Israel struck the Natanz uranium enrichment facility, reporting no leakage of radioactive materials and no danger to the nearby population. The International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) said Iran informed it of the attack and reported no increase in off-site radiation levels. The site, a key nuclear facility, was also targeted in a previous conflict in June 2025.

articles.title: Japan beat Australia to lift Women’s Asian Cup title | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Japan defeated tournament hosts Australia 1-0 in the Women's Asian Cup final in Sydney to claim their third title in four editions. Maika Hamano scored the decisive long-range goal in the 17th minute in front of a record-breaking crowd of 74,357 fans. The tournament, which also served as World Cup qualifying, saw Japan dominate, scoring 29 goals and conceding just one across six games.

articles.title: Unease in Japan after Trump cites Pearl Harbor to defend Iran war | News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: There has been embarrassment and unease in Japan after US President Donald Trump referenced Japan's 1941 attack on Pearl Harbor to justify his secrecy before launching the war on Iran. Trump made the remark during a news conference with Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi, leading to mixed reactions on social media and among analysts. Some criticized Takaichi for not speaking up in response, while others noted the complex strategic relationship between the two allies.

overall_summary: The articles cover a range of topics, primarily focused on the ongoing US-Israel war on Iran, including military developments and diplomatic maneuvers. They also include a UN report on the Israeli-Palestinian conflict, a sports preview, and a human interest story from Uganda.

articles.title: Trump hints at ‘winding down’ Iran war as US deploys more troops to region | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: President Donald Trump suggested the US is considering 'winding down' military operations in Iran, even as his administration deploys 2,500 more marines to the region and requests more war funding. The mixed messages came amid continued fighting, with reports of US-Israeli attacks on Tehran and Iranian missile strikes on the Diego Garcia base. The White House indicated the mission could take 4-6 weeks, but the three-week-old war shows no signs of abating.

articles.title: Iran says it will allow Japanese ships to transit the Strait of Hormuz | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi stated that Japanese ships will be allowed to transit the Strait of Hormuz, clarifying that the strait is only closed to ships from 'enemy' countries attacking Iran. Japan, which sources over 90% of its crude oil from the Middle East, is heavily dependent on the waterway. The report indicates Iran is pursuing a selective blockade and developing a vetting system, with a small number of other countries' ships also being allowed passage.

articles.title: Iran war: What’s happening on day 22 of US-Israel attacks? | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The war has entered its fourth week, with over 1,400 people reported killed in Iran. Iran has retaliated with attacks on Israel and US bases, including firing ballistic missiles at the Diego Garcia base. The article details ongoing regional hostilities, including Iranian attacks on Gulf states and interceptions by Saudi Arabia, Bahrain, and Kuwait. Millions of Iranians marked Eid al-Fitr and Nowruz under the threat of bombardment.

articles.title: Israel systematically torturing Palestinians in custody, says UN expert | Israel-Palestine conflict News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: UN Special Rapporteur Francesca Albanese released a report accusing Israel of systematically torturing Palestinians on a scale that 'suggests collective vengeance and destructive intent'. The report states that since October 7, 2023, Palestinians in custody have been subjected to 'exceptionally ruthless physical and psychological abuse', including beatings, sexual violence, and starvation. It claims more than 18,500 people have been arrested, with about 9,000 still in detention and over 4,000 subjected to enforced disappearance.

articles.title: Real Madrid vs Atletico Madrid: La Liga – team news, start, lineups | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article previews a La Liga match where Real Madrid will host rivals Atletico Madrid at the Santiago Bernabeu. Real Madrid aims to stay in touch with league leaders Barcelona, who are four points clear. The preview notes Real Madrid's recent Champions League victory over Manchester City and seeks vengeance for a 5-2 loss to Atletico earlier in the season.

articles.title: ‘I have fought for Aaron’: A Ugandan mother confronts disability and stigma | Women | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article profiles Martha Apolot, a 21-year-old mother in rural Uganda caring for her eight-year-old son Aaron, who has an undiagnosed disability and requires constant care. Martha, who became pregnant after being raped at age 13, was shunned by her family and community, facing stigma associated with disability. She receives support only from her brother Paul as she navigates the challenges of poverty and caring for Aaron while working in the fields.

overall_summary: The articles cover US military and legal actions in Latin America, a historic extradition from Costa Rica, a US investigation into Colombia's president, and a US court ruling on press freedom. The stories focus on drug enforcement, international justice, political tensions, and media rights.

articles.title: US says it struck alleged drug trafficking vessel in Pacific, killing two | Donald Trump News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The US military announced it attacked an alleged drug trafficking vessel in the eastern Pacific, killing two people and recovering one survivor. This strike is part of Operation Southern Spear, a campaign that has killed at least 159 people in 46 incidents since September 2025. The Trump administration argues the strikes deter drug trafficking, but international legal scholars have denounced them as extrajudicial killings.

articles.title: Ex-minister Gamboa targeted in Costa Rica’s first extradition to the US | Crime News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Costa Rica has extradited citizens to the US for the first time in recent history, including former Supreme Court justice Celso Gamboa, who is accused of using government connections to traffic cocaine. This follows a 2025 constitutional amendment that allowed extradition for drug-trafficking and terrorism suspects, with certain conditions. The extradition is seen as part of a broader regional trend of aggressive tactics to combat organized crime in Latin America.

articles.title: Colombia’s President Gustavo Petro under investigation in US for drug ties | Donald Trump News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Colombian President Gustavo Petro is named in two separate US criminal investigations examining whether he met with drug traffickers and solicited campaign donations from them, according to a New York Times report. Petro has denied the claims, stating he has never spoken with a drug trafficker and that the investigations will exonerate him. The report's timing is noted as significant, coming months before Colombia's presidential election, with experts suggesting it could influence the political outcome.

articles.title: US judge sides with New York Times against Pentagon journalism policies | Donald Trump News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A US federal judge blocked the Trump administration from enforcing a Pentagon policy that limited news reporters' access, siding with The New York Times which argued the rules were unlawful. Judge Paul Friedman ruled the policy violates journalists' constitutional rights to free speech and due process by restricting press credentials. The ruling is described as a rejection of efforts to impede freedom of the press and reporting on government actions.

overall_summary: The articles cover a range of international news from the last six hours. Major themes include escalating tensions in the US-Israel war on Iran, with political statements and regional impacts. Other stories involve sports commentary, US domestic lawsuits, a celebrity death, and diplomatic refusals related to the conflict.

articles.title: Guardiola sidesteps Arsenal League Cup final rivalry as ‘world collapses’ | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Manchester City manager Pep Guardiola refused to engage in pre-match rivalry talk with Arsenal's Mikel Arteta ahead of the League Cup final, stating that 'the world is going to collapse' and there are more important issues. He referenced past tensions where City players accused Arsenal of using 'dark arts' but praised Arsenal as an 'exceptional team'. Arteta said his professional relationship with Guardiola had to change due to their rivalry, but his personal admiration remains.

articles.title: ‘Cowards’: Trump slams NATO over lack of support in US–Israel war on Iran | News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump called NATO allies 'COWARDS' on social media for not supporting the US-Israel war on Iran and helping to secure the Strait of Hormuz, which Iran has effectively closed. He accused them of complaining about high oil prices while avoiding military help. The UK announced it is allowing the US to use its bases for 'collective self-defence' operations in the region, while NATO said it is 'adjusting' its mission in Iraq, relocating personnel.

articles.title: Iran’s Khamenei says enemy ‘defeated’ in written Nowruz message | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Iran's Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei, in a written Nowruz (Persian New Year) message, said the country's enemies are being 'defeated' due to national unity amid ongoing US and Israeli attacks. He claimed the enemy expected the government to fall quickly but miscalculated. Khamenei also denied Iran was responsible for attacks in Turkiye and Oman, calling them 'false flag' incidents, and called for an end to fighting between Afghanistan and Pakistan.

articles.title: Cuba ‘categorically’ rejects prospect of removing Diaz-Canel in US talks | Miguel Diaz-Canel News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Cuban Vice Foreign Minister Carlos Fernandez de Cossio categorically denied that Cuba is negotiating with the US over the removal of President Miguel Diaz-Canel, stating the political system and its officials are not subject to negotiation. This was a response to reports that the Trump administration is seeking Diaz-Canel's ouster. The article notes Trump has implemented measures like a fuel blockade to weaken Cuba's government.

articles.title: Trump administration seeks billions from Harvard in anti-Semitism lawsuit | Donald Trump News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The Trump administration has filed a lawsuit against Harvard University, seeking billions in restitution for allegedly allowing anti-Semitism to flourish and neglecting the civil rights of Jewish and Israeli students. Harvard rejected the suit as 'pretextual and retaliatory,' stating it has taken steps to address anti-Semitism. The lawsuit suggests seeking to recoup federal grants awarded since October 2023, when protests over the war in Gaza erupted.

articles.title: Iranians celebrate Persian New Year in first wartime Nowruz in decades | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Iranians celebrated Nowruz, the Persian New Year, during wartime for the first time since the 1980s Iran-Iraq war, despite ongoing US and Israeli bombardment. In Tehran, people queued for flowers and exchanged greetings, with some air defence batteries firing celebratory shots at the new year. Residents reported the city felt more crowded as some people returned home, though life remains disrupted by attacks and a near-total internet shutdown.

articles.title: Walker, Texas Ranger star and martial arts icon Chuck Norris dies at 86 | Obituaries News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Martial arts icon and actor Chuck Norris has died at age 86, according to a family statement. Norris was a six-time karate champion who rose to fame in 1980s action films and the TV series 'Walker, Texas Ranger.' His family described his death as sudden and said he was surrounded by family and at peace.

articles.title: Sri Lanka says it denied US request to land two aircraft at Mattala airport | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Sri Lanka's President Anura Kumara Dissanayake said his government denied a US request to land two combat aircraft at Mattala Rajapaksa International Airport in early March to maintain neutrality in the US-Israel war on Iran. The request was made on February 26, two days before the war began. He also said Sri Lanka denied a concurrent request from Iran for naval vessels to visit, to avoid taking sides.

articles.title: QatarEnergy CEO says warned US, industry officials against attack on energy | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: QatarEnergy CEO Saad al-Kaabi said he repeatedly warned US officials and energy executives about the 'negative consequences' of attacking Iranian energy infrastructure before Iran retaliated by striking Qatar's Ras Laffan LNG complex. He said the attack destroyed critical cooling units, impacting 17% of Qatar's export capacity, with full recovery taking years. A White House spokesperson said they were aware of potential short-term disruptions.

overall_summary: This batch of articles covers escalating military conflict between the US-Israel alliance and Iran, with significant regional and global repercussions. Key themes include strategic analysis of the Strait of Hormuz, political fallout in various countries, and economic impacts on energy markets. Several articles also cover related developments in Syria, Ukraine, and domestic politics in Slovenia and Switzerland.

articles.title: Iran does not need to close the Strait of Hormuz to disrupt it | US-Israel war on Iran | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article argues that Iran's most effective military option is not to physically close the Strait of Hormuz but to mine its approaches, creating a zone of uncertainty. This strategy would disrupt commercial shipping patterns in the wider Gulf of Oman approach zones using natural water currents to spread the threat. The author states this represents a technically sophisticated interdiction strategy rather than a crude blockade.

articles.title: Iran today, Africa tomorrow | US-Israel war on Iran | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The opinion piece argues that Africa should reject the US-Israeli war on Iran as illegal, citing civilian casualties including children. It states that instability in the Gulf historically causes fuel price increases and inflation across Africa. The author contends the conflict raises fundamental legal questions about the use of force, comparing it to past Western military campaigns that expanded beyond their stated purposes.

articles.title: Refugee Council questions Australia’s Iran strategy at Women’s Asian Cup | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Australia faces criticism over its handling of asylum claims by seven members of the Iranian women's football team during the Women's Asian Cup. Five of the women who initially accepted protection visas returned to Iran within days, with critics arguing the process was rushed and lacked independent legal advice. Refugee advocates expressed concern that publicity around the cases may have endangered the women and overshadowed their welfare.

articles.title: Israel says it hit Syrian army camps in the south after Druze ‘attacked’ | Syria's War News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Israel's military says it struck Syrian army camps overnight in response to what it claimed were attacks against the Druze community in Suwayda. The Syrian Observatory for Human Rights reported fighting between government forces and Druze factions in the area. This latest flare-up occurs as war continues in the region following the US-Israeli attack on Iran in late February.

articles.title: Slovenia heads to polls with diverging views on Israel in focus | Elections News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Slovenia's upcoming election features a close race between incumbent Prime Minister Robert Golob and right-wing former Prime Minister Janez Jansa. Foreign policy toward Israel has become a significant issue, with the current government being an outspoken critic of Israel's actions while Jansa is a staunch supporter. Slovenia has recognized Palestinian statehood, banned imports from occupied territories, and prohibited weapons trade with Israel.

articles.title: FIFA World Cup 2026: Security concerns grow in US as funding stalls | World Cup 2026 News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Security concerns are growing for the 2026 FIFA World Cup in the US as $625 million in federal security grants have been delayed. Intelligence briefings warn of potential extremist attacks targeting the event, with threats heightened since the start of the US-Israeli war on Iran. The delayed funding has complicated planning for states and cities hosting matches, though FEMA recently announced it had awarded the grants.

articles.title: Iran’s IRGC says spokesman Ali Mohammad Naini killed in US-Israeli attack | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps spokesperson Ali Mohammad Naini has been killed in overnight strikes carried out jointly by the US and Israel. His death is the latest in a series of high-profile assassinations that began with Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei on February 28. The Israeli military says it is carrying out strikes across eastern Tehran as the country marks Persian New Year and Eid al-Fitr.

articles.title: Who are the Gulf’s military allies, and how are they helping in Iran war? | Drone Strikes News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Gulf countries are facing increasing attacks from Iranian strikes as the US-Israeli war on Iran escalates. The article examines military partnerships Gulf states have with other countries, noting Qatar hosts the largest US military base in the region at Al Udeid. It details recent attacks including Iranian drones targeting Kuwait's Mina al-Ahmadi refinery and US security guarantees to Qatar following strikes on energy infrastructure.

articles.title: Zelenskyy says Ukraine wants timeline for next round of Russia talks | Russia-Ukraine war News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy says Ukraine wants clear dates for the next round of Russia talks, which have been paused amid the US-Israeli war on Iran. Ukrainian negotiators are set to hold talks in the US on Saturday regarding US-brokered attempts to reach an agreement to end the more than four-year war. The main sticking point remains territory, with Russia pushing for Ukraine to give up remaining parts of Donetsk.

articles.title: Will Russian oil be the biggest winner in the US-Israel war on Iran? | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Russian oil is emerging as a key beneficiary of the US-Israeli war on Iran as countries scramble for alternatives following Iran's closure of the Strait of Hormuz. US President Donald Trump has temporarily eased sanctions on Russian oil to address shortages, causing the price of Russian Urals crude to rise significantly. Analysts say Russia has earned hundreds of millions in additional oil sales since the war began due to the supply vacuum.

articles.title: Switzerland announces halt to weapons exports to US amid Iran war | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Switzerland says it will not issue licenses for companies to export weapons to the United States due to the ongoing war on Iran, citing the country's principle of neutrality. The government has also closed its airspace to US military flights directly linked to the Iran war. Existing US licenses will face regular review, and no new export licenses have been issued to the US since the war began on February 28.

articles.title: What US-Israeli targets reveal about Iran war goals three weeks in | Donald Trump News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article analyzes US-Israeli military targets in Iran over three weeks of fighting, suggesting multiple objectives but no clear endgame. Initial strikes targeted Iran's political and military leadership including Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, while subsequent phases focused on military institutions and internal security forces. Analysts note the approach offers several potential off-ramps for the US but may lead to prolonged escalation due to Iranian responses.

overall_summary: This batch of articles covers a range of international news from the last 6 hours, with a strong focus on the escalating US-Israel war on Iran and its regional consequences. Other stories include a football team's return to Iran, economic hardship in Gaza and the DRC, a US smuggling case, and global Eid al-Fitr celebrations under the shadow of conflict.

articles.title: Iran women’s football team feted in Tehran after asylum battle at Asian Cup | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Iran's national women's football team received a hero's welcome in Tehran after returning from Australia, where several players had initially sought asylum. Five of the players who applied for asylum later withdrew their claims and returned home; activists accuse Iranian authorities of pressuring their families. Two squad members have remained in Australia.

articles.title: Eid under siege: Little to celebrate in Gaza as Israel tightens chokehold | Opinions | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: An opinion piece argues that as world attention shifts to the Iran war, Israel has tightened restrictions on Gaza, worsening shortages and price hikes. Essential goods like tomatoes, cooking gas, and electricity have seen drastic price increases, turning Eid al-Fitr into a time of anxiety. The author states this economic crisis results from Israeli restrictions and some traders exploiting the situation.

articles.title: Iran war: What is happening on day 21 of US-Israel attacks? | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Iran has warned of 'zero restraint' if its energy facilities are attacked again, following Israeli strikes. The article reports new Iranian missile and drone attacks on US bases and Israeli cities, and notes a civilian toll of over 1,400 killed in Iran. Other developments include French President Macron consulting on securing the Strait of Hormuz, and Gulf states responding to missile attacks.

articles.title: Iran hits Kuwaiti oil refinery as Israel renews attacks on Tehran | Conflict News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Drones struck Kuwait's largest oil refinery for a second day as Iran launched a sweeping assault on Gulf energy infrastructure, while explosions were reported over Tehran. The attacks are retaliation for an Israeli strike on Iran's South Pars gasfield. The article also notes severe damage to Qatar's Ras Laffan LNG terminal and that Iran has closed the Strait of Hormuz, sending energy prices surging.

articles.title: Three charged in the US with smuggling AI chips into China | Science and Technology News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Three people associated with server maker Super Micro Computer have been charged with smuggling at least $2.5bn worth of US AI technology to China in violation of export laws. US prosecutors allege a complex scheme to send servers through Taiwan and Southeast Asia to China, using measures like removing serial numbers. Two individuals were arrested, while a third remains a fugitive.

articles.title: ‘It helps us survive’: Poverty forces children into mine work in DR Congo | Child Rights News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Following a deadly landslide at the Rubaya coltan mine that killed 70 children, the article profiles a 15-year-old survivor who says he must return to work because he has 'no other choice'. Child labour is common in the unregulated, informal mining sector in eastern DRC, which is also affected by violence from armed groups. The boy works to support his family after his father died in a previous mine collapse.

articles.title: Ukraine sends advisers to Gulf as it counterattacks Russian forces in south | Russia-Ukraine war News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Ukraine has sent over 200 experts to help Gulf countries defend against Iranian drones, the same type Russia uses, and is preparing to send more. President Zelenskyy said Ukraine has a high success rate shooting down these drones and can produce interceptors to supply allies. The article also notes Ukraine's increased offensive capability, including strikes on Russian energy infrastructure.

articles.title: Long before Trump: How US policy has harmed the environment for decades | Climate Crisis News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Health and environment groups are suing the US EPA over the Trump administration's decision to withdraw a key 2009 climate change ruling, the 'endangerment finding'. The article states that while Trump's actions are the latest, he is not the first US president to enact policies damaging to the environment. The endangerment finding, established under Obama, had formed the legal basis for many climate regulations.

articles.title: Five problems the Iran war could solve for Israel’s Netanyahu | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The article analyzes how the war with Iran could solve several problems for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu. It states he has succeeded in getting the US to join open-ended strikes against Iran, a long-term goal. The conflict may weaken Iran and its regional allies, potentially solidifying Israel's regional hegemony, despite the war's high human and economic costs.

articles.title: Eid celebrations dimmed by war and displacement across Middle East | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Eid al-Fitr celebrations are being dampened by war and economic crisis in Iran, Gaza, and Lebanon. In Lebanon, over a million people are displaced, with some sleeping in tents in Beirut. In Iran, people struggle with high prices and danger from bombing, while in Gaza, Israeli restrictions have driven up the cost of goods, making traditional celebrations difficult.

articles.title: Muslims celebrate Eid al-Fitr around the world amid shadow of war | Religion News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Muslims around the world celebrated Eid al-Fitr on Friday, marking the end of Ramadan, though the timing varied by location based on moon sightings. The festival, marked by congregational prayers and family gatherings, arrives this year against a backdrop of conflict and instability in regions like Iran, the Gulf, Lebanon, and Sudan.

articles.title: Iran developing a ‘vetting system’ for Strait of Hormuz transit: Report | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: According to a report from Lloyd's List, Iran is developing a new vetting and registration system for ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz as it moves to a 'selective' blockade. Several countries are in talks with Tehran, and ships approved for a 'safe corridor' must provide extensive details to the IRGC. Traffic through the Strait has plunged 95% since the war began.

overall_summary: This batch of articles covers sports news and a climate-related lawsuit. The sports stories include a record-breaking NBA performance, a preview of the Women's Asian Cup final, and a report about an Iranian footballer's expulsion from the national team. The non-sports article details a lawsuit by Pakistani farmers against German companies over the 2022 floods.

articles.title: Luka Doncic scores 60 points as Lakers rally to defeat Heat in NBA | Basketball News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Luka Doncic scored 60 points, setting a record for the most points ever scored against the Miami Heat, to lead the Los Angeles Lakers to a 134-126 road victory. The Lakers, who won their eighth straight game, also got a triple-double from LeBron James. The Heat, missing two key injured players, led early but were outscored in the second half.

articles.title: Why Pakistani farmers are suing two German companies for deadly 2022 floods | Climate Crisis | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Thirty-nine Pakistani farmers from Sindh province are suing two German companies, RWE and Heidelberg Materials, alleging their greenhouse gas emissions contributed to the catastrophic 2022 floods. The floods, described as a climate-induced disaster, killed over 1,700 people and caused an estimated $40bn in damages. The farmers' case relates to the companies being among major industrial producers of historical carbon emissions.

articles.title: Japan vs Australia: Women’s Asian Cup final – team news, start and lineups | AFC Asian Cup News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Hosts Australia will face Japan in the AFC Women's Asian Cup final at Stadium Australia in Sydney. Australia, seeking their first major trophy and redemption after a 2023 World Cup semifinal loss at the same venue, has lost both previous Asian Cup finals to Japan. Japan, the highest-ranked team, has been dominant in the tournament with a perfect record and clean sheets leading to the final.

articles.title: Iran footballer Azmoun kicked off national team for disloyalty, say reports | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Reports from Iranian media state that striker Sardar Azmoun has been expelled from the national team for a perceived act of disloyalty. The reports link the expulsion to an Instagram post showing Azmoun with Dubai's ruler, which drew criticism from state TV pundits. His absence would weaken Iran's squad ahead of the upcoming FIFA World Cup, though the national football federation has not officially commented.

overall_summary: The articles cover a range of international news from the last six hours, including military incidents in the US-Iran conflict, domestic US legal and political developments, FIFA disciplinary and financial news, ongoing violence in Gaza, a sports controversy in Africa, a cartel raid in Mexico, and a US commemorative coin approval.

articles.title: US F-35 aircraft makes emergency landing after a combat mission over Iran | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A US F-35 fighter jet made an emergency landing at a Middle Eastern airbase after a combat mission over Iran, landing safely with the pilot in stable condition. US Central Command confirmed the incident is under investigation. CNN reported, citing anonymous sources, that the aircraft was likely hit by Iran, and Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps claimed it targeted a US aircraft, though the US has not confirmed the cause.

articles.title: US states file lawsuit challenging Trump’s revocation of climate finding | Donald Trump News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A coalition of 23 US states, led by California and New York, has filed a legal petition challenging the Trump administration's revocation of the 'endangerment finding,' a key scientific determination that climate change threatens human health and the environment. The lawsuit, filed in the US Court of Appeals, also challenges the repeal of tailpipe emissions standards for certain vehicle model years. The states argue the revocation unlawfully removes foundational protections for addressing climate change.

articles.title: FIFA fines Israel FA citing racism and ‘multiple’ discrimination breaches | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: FIFA has fined the Israeli Football Association (IFA) 150,000 Swiss francs for multiple breaches of its anti-discrimination obligations, citing a failure to take meaningful action against Beitar Jerusalem for persistent racist behavior. The disciplinary committee highlighted 'deficient and substantively inadequate' sanctions against the club. In a separate investigation, FIFA announced no action would be taken against the IFA over allegations concerning Israeli clubs based in the occupied West Bank.

articles.title: FIFA details Infantino’s $6m deal with 33% Club World Cup bonus increase | World Cup 2026 News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: FIFA President Gianni Infantino received a 33% increase in his annual bonus last year, part of a $6 million pay package, as detailed in FIFA's latest accounts. His bonus rose to 2.2 million Swiss francs, linked to the first monthlong men's Club World Cup. FIFA also set a budget target of $14 billion in revenue for the 2027-30 cycle, which includes future World Cups and development allocations.

articles.title: Israel continues Gaza attacks amid regional war, kills several Palestinians | Israel-Palestine conflict News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Israeli drone strikes in Gaza City killed at least three Palestinians and wounded others, according to doctors at al-Shifa Hospital. The attacks occurred as the Rafah border crossing with Egypt reopened for limited medical evacuations after a 19-day closure. The Palestinian Red Crescent Society stated only eight wounded Palestinians and 17 family members would be allowed into Egypt for treatment.

articles.title: Senegal and Morocco tied by religion and trade but divided by AFCON fallout | Africa Cup of Nations News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The Confederation of African Football (CAF) overturned Senegal's Africa Cup of Nations victory and awarded the title to Morocco, a decision met with disbelief in Senegal. Senegal's government says it will pursue legal avenues and called for an investigation into suspected corruption. The move strains the close ties between the two nations, which are built on shared religion, trade, and cultural exchanges.

articles.title: Mexican military says 11 killed in raid targeting Sinaloa cartel leader | Crime News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Mexican authorities say 11 people were killed during a raid in Culiacan that resulted in the capture of Omar Oswaldo Torres, leader of the Los Mayos faction of the Sinaloa Cartel. The Mexican Navy stated its personnel were attacked and returned fire, killing the assailants, and seized high-powered weapons. The raid is part of broader regional efforts against cartels, though experts warn such 'decapitation strategies' can increase long-term violence.

articles.title: US arts commission approves gold coin stamped with Donald Trump’s face | Donald Trump News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The US Commission of Fine Arts, composed of Trump appointees, unanimously approved plans for a commemorative gold coin featuring a portrait of Donald Trump. The coin may sidestep federal law prohibiting living presidents on currency, as it is commemorative and not for circulation. The Trump administration has also advanced plans to put the president's face on a $1 coin, which critics denounce as unlawful and inappropriate for a democracy.
